392:     void Application::InitializeProtocol()
393:     {
394:         auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
395:         auto display = board.GetDisplay();
396:         auto codec = board.GetAudioCodec();
397: 
398:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::LOADING_PROTOCOL);
399: 
400:         if (ota_->HasMqttConfig())
401:         {
402:             protocol_ = std::make_unique<MqttProtocol>();
403:         }
404:         else if (ota_->HasWebsocketConfig())
405:         {
406:             protocol_ = std::make_unique<WebsocketProtocol>();
407:         }
408:         else
409:         {
410:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No protocol specified in the OTA config, using MQTT");
411:             protocol_ = std::make_unique<MqttProtocol>();
412:         }
413: 
414:         protocol_->OnConnected([this]()
415:                                { DismissAlert(); });
416: 
417:         protocol_->OnNetworkError([this](const std::string &message)
418:                                   {
419:         last_error_message_ = message;
420:         xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_ERROR); });
421: 
422:         protocol_->OnIncomingAudio([this](std::unique_ptr<AudioStreamPacket> packet)
423:                                    {
424:         if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateSpeaking) {
425:             audio_service_.PushPacketToDecodeQueue(std::move(packet));
426:         } });
427: 
428:         protocol_->OnAudioChannelOpened([this, codec, &board]()
429:                                         {
430:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
431:         if (protocol_->server_sample_rate() != codec->output_sample_rate()) {
432:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Server sample rate %d does not match device output sample rate %d, resampling may cause distortion",
433:                 protocol_->server_sample_rate(), codec->output_sample_rate());
434:         } });
435: 
436:         protocol_->OnAudioChannelClosed([this, &board]()
437:                                         {
438:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER);
439:         Schedule([this]() {
440:             auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
441:             display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
442:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
443:         }); });
444: 
445:         protocol_->OnIncomingJson([this, display](const cJSON *root)
446:                                   {
447:         // Parse JSON data
448:         auto type = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "type");
449:         if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "tts") == 0) {
450:             auto state = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "state");
451:             if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "start") == 0) {
452:                 Schedule([this]() {
453:                     aborted_ = false;
454:                     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateSpeaking);
455:                 });
456:             } else if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "stop") == 0) {
457:                 Schedule([this]() {
458:                     if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateSpeaking) {
459:                         if (listening_mode_ == kListeningModeManualStop) {
460:                             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
461:                         } else {
462:                             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateListening);
463:                         }
464:                     }
465:                 });
466:             } else if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "sentence_start") == 0) {
467:                 auto text = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "text");
468:                 if (cJSON_IsString(text)) {
469:                     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "<< %s", text->valuestring);
470:                     Schedule([this, display, message = std::string(text->valuestring)]() {
471:                         display->SetChatMessage("assistant", message.c_str());
472:                     });
473:                 }
474:             }
475:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "stt") == 0) {
476:             auto text = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "text");
477:             if (cJSON_IsString(text)) {
478:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, ">> %s", text->valuestring);
479:                 Schedule([this, display, message = std::string(text->valuestring)]() {
480:                     display->SetChatMessage("user", message.c_str());
481:                 });
482:             }
483:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "llm") == 0) {
484:             auto emotion = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "emotion");
485:             if (cJSON_IsString(emotion)) {
486:                 Schedule([this, display, emotion_str = std::string(emotion->valuestring)]() {
487:                     display->SetEmotion(emotion_str.c_str());
488:                 });
489:             }
490:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "mcp") == 0) {
491:             auto payload = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "payload");
492:             if (cJSON_IsObject(payload)) {
493:                 McpServer::GetInstance().ParseMessage(payload);
494:             }
495:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "system") == 0) {
496:             auto command = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "command");
497:             if (cJSON_IsString(command)) {
498:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "System command: %s", command->valuestring);
499:                 if (strcmp(command->valuestring, "reboot") == 0) {
500:                     // Do a reboot if user requests a OTA update
501:                     Schedule([this]() {
502:                         Reboot();
503:                     });
504:                 } else {
505:                     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Unknown system command: %s", command->valuestring);
506:                 }
507:             }
508:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "alert") == 0) {
509:             auto status = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "status");
510:             auto message = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "message");
511:             auto emotion = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "emotion");
512:             if (cJSON_IsString(status) && cJSON_IsString(message) && cJSON_IsString(emotion)) {
513:                 Alert(status->valuestring, message->valuestring, emotion->valuestring, Lang::Sounds::OGG_VIBRATION);
514:             } else {
515:                 ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Alert command requires status, message and emotion");
516:             }
517: #if CONFIG_RECEIVE_CUSTOM_MESSAGE
518:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "custom") == 0) {
519:             auto payload = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "payload");
520:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Received custom message: %s", cJSON_PrintUnformatted(root));
521:             if (cJSON_IsObject(payload)) {
522:                 Schedule([this, display, payload_str = std::string(cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload))]() {
523:                     display->SetChatMessage("system", payload_str.c_str());
524:                 });
525:             } else {
526:                 ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Invalid custom message format: missing payload");
527:             }
528: #endif
529:         } else {
530:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Unknown message type: %s", type->valuestring);
531:         } });
532: 
533:         protocol_->Start();
534:     }

536:     void Application::ShowActivationCode(const std::string &code, const std::string &message)
537:     {
538:         struct digit_sound
539:         {
540:             char digit;
541:             const std::string_view &sound;
542:         };
543:         static const std::array<digit_sound, 10> digit_sounds{{digit_sound{'0', Lang::Sounds::OGG_0},
544:                                                                digit_sound{'1', Lang::Sounds::OGG_1},
545:                                                                digit_sound{'2', Lang::Sounds::OGG_2},
546:                                                                digit_sound{'3', Lang::Sounds::OGG_3},
547:                                                                digit_sound{'4', Lang::Sounds::OGG_4},
548:                                                                digit_sound{'5', Lang::Sounds::OGG_5},
549:                                                                digit_sound{'6', Lang::Sounds::OGG_6},
550:                                                                digit_sound{'7', Lang::Sounds::OGG_7},
551:                                                                digit_sound{'8', Lang::Sounds::OGG_8},
552:                                                                digit_sound{'9', Lang::Sounds::OGG_9}}};
553: 
554:         // This sentence uses 9KB of SRAM, so we need to wait for it to finish
555:         Alert(Lang::Strings::ACTIVATION, message.c_str(), "link", Lang::Sounds::OGG_ACTIVATION);
556: 
557:         for (const auto &digit : code)
558:         {
559:             auto it = std::find_if(digit_sounds.begin(), digit_sounds.end(),
560:                                    [digit](const digit_sound &ds)
561:                                    { return ds.digit == digit; });
562:             if (it != digit_sounds.end())
563:             {
564:                 audio_service_.PlaySound(it->sound);
565:             }
566:         }
567:     }

608:     void Application::HandleToggleChatEvent()
609:     {
610:         auto state = GetDeviceState();
611: 
612:         if (state == kDeviceStateActivating)
613:         {
614:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
615:             return;
616:         }
617:         else if (state == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring)
618:         {
619:             audio_service_.EnableAudioTesting(true);
620:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateAudioTesting);
621:             return;
622:         }
623:         else if (state == kDeviceStateAudioTesting)
624:         {
625:             audio_service_.EnableAudioTesting(false);
626:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring);
627:             return;
628:         }
629: 
630:         if (!protocol_)
631:         {
632:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Protocol not initialized");
633:             return;
634:         }
635: 
636:         if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
637:         {
638:             if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
639:             {
640:                 SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
641:                 if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
642:                 {
643:                     return;
644:                 }
645:             }
646: 
647:             SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
648:         }
649:         else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
650:         {
651:             AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonNone);
652:         }
653:         else if (state == kDeviceStateListening)
654:         {
655:             protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
656:         }
657:     }

659:     void Application::HandleStartListeningEvent()

663:         if (state == kDeviceStateActivating)
664:         {

666:             return;
667:         }
668:         else if (state == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring)
669:         {
670:             audio_service_.EnableAudioTesting(true);

672:             return;
673:         }
674: 
675:         if (!protocol_)
676:         {

686:                 if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
687:                 {

690:             }
691: 
692:             SetListeningMode(kListeningModeManualStop);

721:     void Application::HandleWakeWordDetectedEvent()
722:     {
723:         if (!protocol_)
724:         {
725:             return;
726:         }
727: 
728:         auto state = GetDeviceState();
729: 
730:         if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
731:         {
732:             audio_service_.EncodeWakeWord();
733: 
734:             if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
735:             {
736:                 SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
737:                 if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
738:                 {
739:                     audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(true);
740:                     return;
741:                 }
742:             }
743: 
744:             auto wake_word = audio_service_.GetLastWakeWord();
745:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Wake word detected: %s", wake_word.c_str());
746: #if CONFIG_SEND_WAKE_WORD_DATA
747:             // Encode and send the wake word data to the server
748:             while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopWakeWordPacket())
749:             {
750:                 protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet));
751:             }
752:             // Set the chat state to wake word detected
753:             protocol_->SendWakeWordDetected(wake_word);
754:             SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
755: #else
756:             // Set flag to play popup sound after state changes to listening
757:             // (PlaySound here would be cleared by ResetDecoder in EnableVoiceProcessing)
758:             play_popup_on_listening_ = true;
759:             SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
760: #endif
761:         }
762:         else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
763:         {
764:             AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonWakeWordDetected);
765:         }
766:         else if (state == kDeviceStateActivating)
767:         {
768:             // Restart the activation check if the wake word is detected during activation
769:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
770:         }
771:     }
772: 
773:     void Application::HandleStateChangedEvent()
774:     {
775:         DeviceState new_state = state_machine_.GetState();
776:         clock_ticks_ = 0;
777: 
778:         auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
779:         auto display = board.GetDisplay();
780:         auto led = board.GetLed();
781:         led->OnStateChanged();
782: 
783:         switch (new_state)
784:         {
785:         case kDeviceStateUnknown:
786:         case kDeviceStateIdle:
787:             display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::STANDBY);
788:             display->SetEmotion("neutral");
789:             audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
790:             audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(true);
791:             break;
792:         case kDeviceStateConnecting:
793:             display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::CONNECTING);
794:             display->SetEmotion("neutral");
795:             display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
796:             break;
797:         case kDeviceStateListening:
798:             display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::LISTENING);
799:             display->SetEmotion("neutral");
800: 
801:             // Make sure the audio processor is running
802:             if (!audio_service_.IsAudioProcessorRunning())
803:             {
804:                 // Send the start listening command
805:                 protocol_->SendStartListening(listening_mode_);
806:                 audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(true);
807:                 audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(false);
808:             }
809: 
810:             // Play popup sound after ResetDecoder (in EnableVoiceProcessing) has been called
811:             if (play_popup_on_listening_)
812:             {
813:                 play_popup_on_listening_ = false;
814:                 audio_service_.PlaySound(Lang::Sounds::OGG_POPUP);
815:             }
816:             break;
817:         case kDeviceStateSpeaking:
818:             display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::SPEAKING);
819: 
820:             if (listening_mode_ != kListeningModeRealtime)
821:             {
822:                 audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
823:                 // Only AFE wake word can be detected in speaking mode
824:                 audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(audio_service_.IsAfeWakeWord());
825:             }
826:             audio_service_.ResetDecoder();
827:             break;
828:         case kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring:
829:             audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
830:             audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(false);
831:             break;
832:         default:
833:             // Do nothing
834:             break;
835:         }
836:     }

863:     void Application::Reboot()
864:     {
865:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Rebooting...");
866:         // Disconnect the audio channel
867:         if (protocol_ && protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
868:         {
869:             protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
870:         }
871:         protocol_.reset();
872:         audio_service_.Stop();
873: 
874:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
875:         esp_restart();

878:     bool Application::UpgradeFirmware(const std::string &url, const std::string &version)
879:     {
880:         auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
881:         auto display = board.GetDisplay();
882: 
883:         std::string upgrade_url = url;
884:         std::string version_info = version.empty() ? "(Manual upgrade)" : version;
885: 
886:         // Close audio channel if it's open
887:         if (protocol_ && protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
888:         {
889:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Closing audio channel before firmware upgrade");
890:             protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
891:         }
892:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Starting firmware upgrade from URL: %s", upgrade_url.c_str());
893: 
894:         Alert(Lang::Strings::OTA_UPGRADE, Lang::Strings::UPGRADING, "download", Lang::Sounds::OGG_UPGRADE);
895:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
896: 
897:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateUpgrading);
898: 
899:         std::string message = std::string(Lang::Strings::NEW_VERSION) + version_info;
900:         display->SetChatMessage("system", message.c_str());
901: 
902:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
903:         audio_service_.Stop();
904:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
905: 
906:         bool upgrade_success = Ota::Upgrade(upgrade_url, [display](int progress, size_t speed)
907:                                             { std::thread([display, progress, speed]()
908:                                                           {
909:             char buffer[32];
910:             snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%d%% %uKB/s", progress, speed / 1024);
911:             display->SetChatMessage("system", buffer); })
912:                                                   .detach(); });
913: 
914:         if (!upgrade_success)
915:         {
916:             // Upgrade failed, restart audio service and continue running
917:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Firmware upgrade failed, restarting audio service and continuing operation...");
918:             audio_service_.Start();                             // Restart audio service
919:             board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER); // Restore power save level
920:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, Lang::Strings::UPGRADE_FAILED, "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
921:             vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
922:             return false;
923:         }
924:         else
925:         {
926:             // Upgrade success, reboot immediately
927:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Firmware upgrade successful, rebooting...");
928:             display->SetChatMessage("system", "Upgrade successful, rebooting...");

935:     void Application::WakeWordInvoke(const std::string &wake_word)
936:     {
937:         if (!protocol_)
938:         {
939:             return;
940:         }
941: 
942:         auto state = GetDeviceState();
943: 
944:         if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
945:         {
946:             audio_service_.EncodeWakeWord();
947: 
948:             if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
949:             {
950:                 SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
951:                 if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
952:                 {
953:                     audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(true);
954:                     return;
955:                 }
956:             }
957: 
958:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Wake word detected: %s", wake_word.c_str());
959: #if CONFIG_USE_AFE_WAKE_WORD || CONFIG_USE_CUSTOM_WAKE_WORD
960:             // Encode and send the wake word data to the server
961:             while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopWakeWordPacket())
962:             {
963:                 protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet));
964:             }
965:             // Set the chat state to wake word detected
966:             protocol_->SendWakeWordDetected(wake_word);
967:             SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
968: #else
969:             // Set flag to play popup sound after state changes to listening
970:             // (PlaySound here would be cleared by ResetDecoder in EnableVoiceProcessing)
971:             play_popup_on_listening_ = true;
972:             SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
973: #endif
974:         }
975:         else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
976:         {
977:             Schedule([this]()
978:                      { AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonNone); });
979:         }
980:         else if (state == kDeviceStateListening)
981:         {
982:             Schedule([this]()
983:                      {
984:             if (protocol_) {
985:                 protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
986:             } });
987:         }
988:     }

996: 
997:         if (protocol_ && protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())

1001:

1003:         {

The function manages the main event loop of the application, processing various system and user events such as network changes, audio commands, and state transitions. It coordinates core functionalities like error handling, chat toggling, listening states, and scheduled tasks while updating the device state and UI accordingly. The loop ensures responsive control flow and system stability by reacting to both internal and external triggers in real time.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The overall event loop structure using xEventGroupWaitBits is preserved.
- Most event handling blocks are present but differ significantly in implementation details.
- Key semantic differences include:
  * Audio packet sending logic is oversimplified (missing packet queue processing).
  * Error handling lacks alert functionality and device state management.
  * Network connected event handling is incomplete (no activation logic).
  * Missing critical logic like LED state updates on VAD change.
  * Clock tick handling omits debug logging every 10 seconds.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches (void Application::Run()).
- Basic control flow with while(true) and event bit checking is maintained.
- Uses similar mutex locking pattern for task scheduling.
- However, ordering of event checks differs from ground truth.
- Some API calls are different or missing (e.g., no Alert() call, different Display methods).

Functional Completeness:
- Missing several key functionalities:
  * No handling of MAIN_EVENT_START/STOP_LISTENING events in network connection context.
  * Activation task creation and management is absent.
  * Audio packet queue processing is replaced with a generic SendAudioData() call.
  * Missing system debug logging (SystemInfo::PrintHeapStats).
  * No proper error alerting mechanism with sound/icon notifications.
  * VAD change event lacks LED state update logic.
- Some events like SCHEDULE, WAKE_WORD_DETECTED, TOGGLE_CHAT are handled but with less detail.

results.description: The function handles network disconnection by closing the active audio channel and updating the display status to reflect the network state. It also manages device state transitions and plays a success sound when activation is complete.

172: void Application::Run()
173: {
174:     const EventBits_t ALL_EVENTS =
175:         MAIN_EVENT_SCHEDULE |
176:         MAIN_EVENT_SEND_AUDIO |
177:         MAIN_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_DETECTED |
178:         MAIN_EVENT_VAD_CHANGE |
179:         MAIN_EVENT_CLOCK_TICK |
180:         MAIN_EVENT_ERROR |
181:         MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_CONNECTED |
182:         MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED |
183:         MAIN_EVENT_TOGGLE_CHAT |
184:         MAIN_EVENT_START_LISTENING |
185:         MAIN_EVENT_STOP_LISTENING |
186:         MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE |
187:         MAIN_EVENT_STATE_CHANGED;
188: 
189:     while (true)
190:     {
191:         auto bits = xEventGroupWaitBits(event_group_, ALL_EVENTS, pdTRUE, pdFALSE, portMAX_DELAY);
192: 
193:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_ERROR)
194:         {
195:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
196:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, last_error_message_.c_str(), "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
197:         }
198: 
199:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_CONNECTED)
200:         {
201:             HandleNetworkConnectedEvent();
202:         }
203: 
204:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED)
205:         {
206:             HandleNetworkDisconnectedEvent();
207:         }
208: 
209:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE)
210:         {
211:             HandleActivationDoneEvent();
212:         }
213: 
214:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_STATE_CHANGED)
215:         {
216:             HandleStateChangedEvent();
217:         }
218: 
219:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_TOGGLE_CHAT)
220:         {
221:             HandleToggleChatEvent();
222:         }
223: 
224:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_START_LISTENING)
225:         {
226:             HandleStartListeningEvent();
227:         }
228: 
229:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_STOP_LISTENING)
230:         {
231:             HandleStopListeningEvent();
232:         }
233: 
234:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_SEND_AUDIO)
235:         {
236:             while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopPacketFromSendQueue())
237:             {
238:                 if (protocol_ && !protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet)))
239:                 {
240:                     break;
241:                 }
242:             }
243:         }
244: 
245:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_DETECTED)
246:         {
247:             HandleWakeWordDetectedEvent();
248:         }
249: 
250:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_VAD_CHANGE)
251:         {
252:             if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateListening)
253:             {
254:                 auto led = Board::GetInstance().GetLed();
255:                 led->OnStateChanged();
256:             }
257:         }
258: 
259:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_SCHEDULE)
260:         {
261:             std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);
262:             auto tasks = std::move(main_tasks_);
263:             lock.unlock();
264:             for (auto &task : tasks)
265:             {
266:                 task();
267:             }
268:         }
269: 
270:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_CLOCK_TICK)
271:         {
272:             clock_ticks_++;
273:             auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
274:             display->UpdateStatusBar();
275: 
276:             // Print debug info every 10 seconds
277:             if (clock_ticks_ % 10 == 0)
278:             {
279:                 SystemInfo::PrintHeapStats();
280:             }
281:         }
282:     }
283: }

342: void Application::CheckAssetsVersion()
343: {
344:     // Only allow CheckAssetsVersion to be called once
345:     if (assets_version_checked_)
346:     {
347:         return;
348:     }
349:     assets_version_checked_ = true;
350: 
351:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
352:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
353:     auto &assets = Assets::GetInstance();
354: 
355:     if (!assets.partition_valid())
356:     {
357:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Assets partition is disabled for board %s", BOARD_NAME);
358:         return;
359:     }
360: 
361:     Settings settings("assets", true);
362:     // Check if there is a new assets need to be downloaded
363:     std::string download_url = settings.GetString("download_url");
364: 
365:     if (!download_url.empty())
366:     {
367:         settings.EraseKey("download_url");
368: 
369:         char message[256];
370:         snprintf(message, sizeof(message), Lang::Strings::FOUND_NEW_ASSETS, download_url.c_str());
371:         Alert(Lang::Strings::LOADING_ASSETS, message, "cloud_arrow_down", Lang::Sounds::OGG_UPGRADE);
372: 
373:         // Wait for the audio service to be idle for 3 seconds
374:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
375:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateUpgrading);
376:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
377:         display->SetChatMessage("system", Lang::Strings::PLEASE_WAIT);
378: 
379:         bool success = assets.Download(download_url, [display](int progress, size_t speed) -> void
380:                                        { std::thread([display, progress, speed]()
381:                                                      {
382:                 char buffer[32];
383:                 snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%d%% %uKB/s", progress, speed / 1024);
384:                 display->SetChatMessage("system", buffer); })
385:                                              .detach(); });
386: 
387:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER);
388:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
389: 
390:         if (!success)
391:         {
392:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, Lang::Strings::DOWNLOAD_ASSETS_FAILED, "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
393:             vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(2000));
394:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateActivating);
395:             return;
396:         }
397:     }
398: 
399:     // Apply assets
400:     assets.Apply();
401:     display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
402:     display->SetEmotion("microchip_ai");
403: }

405: void Application::CheckNewVersion()
406: {
407:     const int MAX_RETRY = 10;
408:     int retry_count = 0;
409:     int retry_delay = 10; // Initial retry delay in seconds
410: 
411:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
412:     while (true)
413:     {
414:         auto display = board.GetDisplay();
415:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::CHECKING_NEW_VERSION);
416: 
417:         esp_err_t err = ota_->CheckVersion();
418:         if (err != ESP_OK)
419:         {
420:             retry_count++;
421:             if (retry_count >= MAX_RETRY)
422:             {
423:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Too many retries, exit version check");
424:                 return;
425:             }
426: 
427:             char error_message[128];
428:             snprintf(error_message, sizeof(error_message), "code=%d, url=%s", err, ota_->GetCheckVersionUrl().c_str());
429:             char buffer[256];
430:             snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), Lang::Strings::CHECK_NEW_VERSION_FAILED, retry_delay, error_message);
431:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, buffer, "cloud_slash", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
432: 
433:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Check new version failed, retry in %d seconds (%d/%d)", retry_delay, retry_count, MAX_RETRY);
434:             for (int i = 0; i < retry_delay; i++)
435:             {
436:                 vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
437:                 if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateIdle)
438:                 {
439:                     break;
440:                 }
441:             }
442:             retry_delay *= 2; // Double the retry delay
443:             continue;
444:         }
445:         retry_count = 0;
446:         retry_delay = 10; // Reset retry delay
447: 
448:         if (ota_->HasNewVersion())
449:         {
450:             if (UpgradeFirmware(ota_->GetFirmwareUrl(), ota_->GetFirmwareVersion()))
451:             {
452:                 return; // This line will never be reached after reboot
453:             }
454:             // If upgrade failed, continue to normal operation
455:         }
456: 
457:         // No new version, mark the current version as valid
458:         ota_->MarkCurrentVersionValid();
459:         if (!ota_->HasActivationCode() && !ota_->HasActivationChallenge())
460:         {
461:             // Exit the loop if done checking new version
462:             break;
463:         }
464: 
465:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::ACTIVATION);
466:         // Activation code is shown to the user and waiting for the user to input
467:         if (ota_->HasActivationCode())
468:         {
469:             ShowActivationCode(ota_->GetActivationCode(), ota_->GetActivationMessage());
470:         }
471: 
472:         // This will block the loop until the activation is done or timeout
473:         for (int i = 0; i < 10; ++i)
474:         {
475:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activating... %d/%d", i + 1, 10);
476:             esp_err_t err = ota_->Activate();
477:             if (err == ESP_OK)
478:             {
479:                 break;
480:             }
481:             else if (err == ESP_ERR_TIMEOUT)
482:             {
483:                 vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
484:             }
485:             else
486:             {
487:                 vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(10000));
488:             }
489:             if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateIdle)
490:             {
491:                 break;
492:             }
493:         }
494:     }
495: }

497: void Application::InitializeProtocol()

500:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
501:     auto codec = board.GetAudioCodec();
502: 
503:     display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::LOADING_PROTOCOL);
504: 
505:     if (ota_->HasMqttConfig())
506:     {
507:         protocol_ = std::make_unique<MqttProtocol>();
508:     }
509:     else if (ota_->HasWebsocketConfig())
510:     {
511:         protocol_ = std::make_unique<WebsocketProtocol>();
512:     }
513:     else
514:     {
515:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No protocol specified in the OTA config, using MQTT");
516:         protocol_ = std::make_unique<MqttProtocol>();
517:     }
518: 
519:     protocol_->OnConnected([this]()
520:                            { DismissAlert(); });
521: 
522:     protocol_->OnNetworkError([this](const std::string &message)
523:                               {
524:         last_error_message_ = message;
525:         xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_ERROR); });
526: 
527:     protocol_->OnIncomingAudio([this](std::unique_ptr<AudioStreamPacket> packet)
528:                                {
529:         if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateSpeaking) {
530:             audio_service_.PushPacketToDecodeQueue(std::move(packet));
531:         } });
532: 
533:     protocol_->OnAudioChannelOpened([this, codec, &board]()
534:                                     {
535:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
536:         if (protocol_->server_sample_rate() != codec->output_sample_rate()) {
537:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Server sample rate %d does not match device output sample rate %d, resampling may cause distortion",
538:                 protocol_->server_sample_rate(), codec->output_sample_rate());
539:         } });
540: 
541:     protocol_->OnAudioChannelClosed([this, &board]()
542:                                     {
543:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER);
544:         Schedule([this]() {
545:             auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
546:             display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
547:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
548:         }); });
549: 
550:     protocol_->OnIncomingJson([this, display](const cJSON *root)
551:                               {
552:         // Parse JSON data
553:         auto type = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "type");
554:         if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "tts") == 0) {
555:             auto state = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "state");
556:             if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "start") == 0) {
557:                 Schedule([this]() {
558:                     aborted_ = false;
559:                     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateSpeaking);
560:                 });
561:             } else if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "stop") == 0) {
562:                 Schedule([this]() {
563:                     if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateSpeaking) {
564:                         if (listening_mode_ == kListeningModeManualStop) {
565:                             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
566:                         } else {
567:                             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateListening);
568:                         }
569:                     }
570:                 });
571:             } else if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "sentence_start") == 0) {
572:                 auto text = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "text");
573:                 if (cJSON_IsString(text)) {
574:                     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "<< %s", text->valuestring);
575:                     Schedule([this, display, message = std::string(text->valuestring)]() {
576:                         display->SetChatMessage("assistant", message.c_str());
577:                     });
578:                 }
579:             }
580:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "stt") == 0) {
581:             auto text = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "text");
582:             if (cJSON_IsString(text)) {
583:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, ">> %s", text->valuestring);
584:                 Schedule([this, display, message = std::string(text->valuestring)]() {
585:                     display->SetChatMessage("user", message.c_str());
586:                 });
587:             }
588:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "llm") == 0) {
589:             auto emotion = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "emotion");
590:             if (cJSON_IsString(emotion)) {
591:                 Schedule([this, display, emotion_str = std::string(emotion->valuestring)]() {
592:                     display->SetEmotion(emotion_str.c_str());
593:                 });
594:             }
595:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "mcp") == 0) {
596:             auto payload = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "payload");
597:             if (cJSON_IsObject(payload)) {
598:                 McpServer::GetInstance().ParseMessage(payload);
599:             }
600:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "system") == 0) {
601:             auto command = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "command");
602:             if (cJSON_IsString(command)) {
603:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "System command: %s", command->valuestring);
604:                 if (strcmp(command->valuestring, "reboot") == 0) {
605:                     // Do a reboot if user requests a OTA update
606:                     Schedule([this]() {
607:                         Reboot();
608:                     });
609:                 } else {
610:                     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Unknown system command: %s", command->valuestring);
611:                 }
612:             }
613:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "alert") == 0) {
614:             auto status = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "status");
615:             auto message = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "message");
616:             auto emotion = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "emotion");
617:             if (cJSON_IsString(status) && cJSON_IsString(message) && cJSON_IsString(emotion)) {
618:                 Alert(status->valuestring, message->valuestring, emotion->valuestring, Lang::Sounds::OGG_VIBRATION);
619:             } else {
620:                 ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Alert command requires status, message and emotion");
621:             }
622: #if CONFIG_RECEIVE_CUSTOM_MESSAGE
623:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "custom") == 0) {
624:             auto payload = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "payload");
625:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Received custom message: %s", cJSON_PrintUnformatted(root));
626:             if (cJSON_IsObject(payload)) {
627:                 Schedule([this, display, payload_str = std::string(cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload))]() {
628:                     display->SetChatMessage("system", payload_str.c_str());
629:                 });
630:             } else {
631:                 ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Invalid custom message format: missing payload");
632:             }
633: #endif
634:         } else {
635:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Unknown message type: %s", type->valuestring);
636:         } });
637: 
638:     protocol_->Start();
639: }

641: void Application::ShowActivationCode(const std::string &code, const std::string &message)
642: {
643:     struct digit_sound
644:     {
645:         char digit;
646:         const std::string_view &sound;
647:     };
648:     static const std::array<digit_sound, 10> digit_sounds{{digit_sound{'0', Lang::Sounds::OGG_0},
649:                                                            digit_sound{'1', Lang::Sounds::OGG_1},
650:                                                            digit_sound{'2', Lang::Sounds::OGG_2},
651:                                                            digit_sound{'3', Lang::Sounds::OGG_3},
652:                                                            digit_sound{'4', Lang::Sounds::OGG_4},
653:                                                            digit_sound{'5', Lang::Sounds::OGG_5},
654:                                                            digit_sound{'6', Lang::Sounds::OGG_6},
655:                                                            digit_sound{'7', Lang::Sounds::OGG_7},
656:                                                            digit_sound{'8', Lang::Sounds::OGG_8},
657:                                                            digit_sound{'9', Lang::Sounds::OGG_9}}};
658: 
659:     // This sentence uses 9KB of SRAM, so we need to wait for it to finish
660:     Alert(Lang::Strings::ACTIVATION, message.c_str(), "link", Lang::Sounds::OGG_ACTIVATION);
661: 
662:     for (const auto &digit : code)
663:     {
664:         auto it = std::find_if(digit_sounds.begin(), digit_sounds.end(),
665:                                [digit](const digit_sound &ds)
666:                                { return ds.digit == digit; });
667:         if (it != digit_sounds.end())
668:         {
669:             audio_service_.PlaySound(it->sound);
670:         }
671:     }
672: }

713: void Application::HandleToggleChatEvent()
714: {
715:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
716: 
717:     if (state == kDeviceStateActivating)
718:     {
719:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
720:         return;
721:     }
722:     else if (state == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring)
723:     {
724:         audio_service_.EnableAudioTesting(true);
725:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateAudioTesting);
726:         return;
727:     }
728:     else if (state == kDeviceStateAudioTesting)
729:     {
730:         audio_service_.EnableAudioTesting(false);
731:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring);
732:         return;
733:     }
734: 
735:     if (!protocol_)
736:     {
737:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Protocol not initialized");
738:         return;
739:     }
740: 
741:     if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
742:     {
743:         if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
744:         {
745:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
746:             if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
747:             {
748:                 return;
749:             }
750:         }
751: 
752:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
753:     }
754:     else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
755:     {
756:         AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonNone);
757:     }
758:     else if (state == kDeviceStateListening)
759:     {
760:         protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
761:     }
762: }

764: void Application::HandleStartListeningEvent()

826: void Application::HandleWakeWordDetectedEvent()
827: {
828:     if (!protocol_)
829:     {
830:         return;
831:     }
832: 
833:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
834: 
835:     if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
836:     {
837:         audio_service_.EncodeWakeWord();
838: 
839:         if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
840:         {
841:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
842:             if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
843:             {
844:                 audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(true);
845:                 return;
846:             }
847:         }
848: 
849:         auto wake_word = audio_service_.GetLastWakeWord();
850:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Wake word detected: %s", wake_word.c_str());
851: #if CONFIG_SEND_WAKE_WORD_DATA
852:         // Encode and send the wake word data to the server
853:         while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopWakeWordPacket())
854:         {
855:             protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet));
856:         }
857:         // Set the chat state to wake word detected
858:         protocol_->SendWakeWordDetected(wake_word);
859:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
860: #else
861:         // Set flag to play popup sound after state changes to listening
862:         // (PlaySound here would be cleared by ResetDecoder in EnableVoiceProcessing)
863:         play_popup_on_listening_ = true;
864:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
865: #endif
866:     }
867:     else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
868:     {
869:         AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonWakeWordDetected);
870:     }
871:     else if (state == kDeviceStateActivating)
872:     {
873:         // Restart the activation check if the wake word is detected during activation
874:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
875:     }
876: }

878: void Application::HandleStateChangedEvent()
879: {
880:     DeviceState new_state = state_machine_.GetState();
881:     clock_ticks_ = 0;
882: 
883:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
884:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
885:     auto led = board.GetLed();
886:     led->OnStateChanged();
887: 
888:     switch (new_state)
889:     {
890:     case kDeviceStateUnknown:
891:     case kDeviceStateIdle:
892:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::STANDBY);
893:         display->SetEmotion("neutral");
894:         audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
895:         audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(true);
896:         break;
897:     case kDeviceStateConnecting:
898:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::CONNECTING);
899:         display->SetEmotion("neutral");
900:         display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
901:         break;
902:     case kDeviceStateListening:
903:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::LISTENING);
904:         display->SetEmotion("neutral");
905: 
906:         // Make sure the audio processor is running
907:         if (!audio_service_.IsAudioProcessorRunning())
908:         {
909:             // Send the start listening command
910:             protocol_->SendStartListening(listening_mode_);
911:             audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(true);
912:             audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(false);
913:         }
914: 
915:         // Play popup sound after ResetDecoder (in EnableVoiceProcessing) has been called
916:         if (play_popup_on_listening_)
917:         {
918:             play_popup_on_listening_ = false;
919:             audio_service_.PlaySound(Lang::Sounds::OGG_POPUP);
920:         }
921:         break;
922:     case kDeviceStateSpeaking:
923:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::SPEAKING);
924: 
925:         if (listening_mode_ != kListeningModeRealtime)
926:         {
927:             audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
928:             // Only AFE wake word can be detected in speaking mode
929:             audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(audio_service_.IsAfeWakeWord());
930:         }
931:         audio_service_.ResetDecoder();
932:         break;
933:     case kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring:
934:         audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
935:         audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(false);
936:         break;
937:     default:
938:         // Do nothing
939:         break;
940:     }
941: }

968: void Application::Reboot()
969: {
970:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Rebooting...");
971:     // Disconnect the audio channel
972:     if (protocol_ && protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
973:     {
974:         protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
975:     }

978:

980:     esp_restart();
981: }

983: bool Application::UpgradeFirmware(const std::string &url, const std::string &version)
984: {
985:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
986:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
987: 
988:     std::string upgrade_url = url;
989:     std::string version_info = version.empty() ? "(Manual upgrade)" : version;
990: 
991:     // Close audio channel if it's open
992:     if (protocol_ && protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
993:     {
994:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Closing audio channel before firmware upgrade");
995:         protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
996:     }
997:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Starting firmware upgrade from URL: %s", upgrade_url.c_str());
998: 
999:     Alert(Lang::Strings::OTA_UPGRADE, Lang::Strings::UPGRADING, "download", Lang::Sounds::OGG_UPGRADE);
1000:     vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
1001: 
1002:     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateUpgrading);
1003: 
1004:     std::string message = std::string(Lang::Strings::NEW_VERSION) + version_info;
1005:     display->SetChatMessage("system", message.c_str());
1006: 
1007:     board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
1008:     audio_service_.Stop();
1009:     vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
1010: 
1011:     bool upgrade_success = Ota::Upgrade(upgrade_url, [display](int progress, size_t speed)
1012:                                         { std::thread([display, progress, speed]()
1013:                                                       {
1014:             char buffer[32];
1015:             snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%d%% %uKB/s", progress, speed / 1024);
1016:             display->SetChatMessage("system", buffer); })
1017:                                               .detach(); });
1018: 
1019:     if (!upgrade_success)
1020:     {
1021:         // Upgrade failed, restart audio service and continue running
1022:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Firmware upgrade failed, restarting audio service and continuing operation...");
1023:         audio_service_.Start();                             // Restart audio service
1024:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER); // Restore power save level
1025:         Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, Lang::Strings::UPGRADE_FAILED, "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
1026:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
1027:         return false;
1028:     }
1029:     else
1030:     {
1031:         // Upgrade success, reboot immediately
1032:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Firmware upgrade successful, rebooting...");
1033:         display->SetChatMessage("system", "Upgrade successful, rebooting...");
1034:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000)); // Brief pause to show message
1035:         Reboot();
1036:         return true;
1037:     }
1038: }
1039: 
1040: void Application::WakeWordInvoke(const std::string &wake_word)
1041: {
1042:     if (!protocol_)
1043:     {
1044:         return;
1045:     }
1046: 
1047:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
1048: 
1049:     if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
1050:     {
1051:         audio_service_.EncodeWakeWord();
1052: 
1053:         if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
1054:         {
1055:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
1056:             if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
1057:             {
1058:                 audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(true);
1059:                 return;
1060:             }
1061:         }
1062: 
1063:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Wake word detected: %s", wake_word.c_str());
1064: #if CONFIG_USE_AFE_WAKE_WORD || CONFIG_USE_CUSTOM_WAKE_WORD
1065:         // Encode and send the wake word data to the server
1066:         while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopWakeWordPacket())
1067:         {
1068:             protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet));
1069:         }
1070:         // Set the chat state to wake word detected
1071:         protocol_->SendWakeWordDetected(wake_word);
1072:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
1073: #else
1074:         // Set flag to play popup sound after state changes to listening
1075:         // (PlaySound here would be cleared by ResetDecoder in EnableVoiceProcessing)
1076:         play_popup_on_listening_ = true;
1077:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
1078: #endif
1079:     }
1080:     else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
1081:     {
1082:         Schedule([this]()
1083:                  { AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonNone); });
1084:     }
1085:     else if (state == kDeviceStateListening)
1086:     {
1087:         Schedule([this]()
1088:                  {
1089:             if (protocol_) {
1090:                 protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
1091:             } });
1092:     }
1093: }
1094: 
1095: bool Application::CanEnterSleepMode()
1096: {
1097:     if (GetDeviceState() != kDeviceStateIdle)
1098:     {
1099:         return false;
1100:     }
1101: 
1102:     if (protocol_ && protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
1103:     {
1104:         return false;
1105:     }
1106: 
1107:     if (!audio_service_.IsIdle())
1108:     {
1109:         return false;
1110:     }
1111: 
1112:     // Now it is safe to enter sleep mode
1113:     return true;
1114: }

1116: void Application::SendMcpMessage(const std::string &payload)
1117: {
1118:     // Always schedule to run in main task for thread safety
1119:     Schedule([this, payload = std::move(payload)]()
1120:              {
1121:         if (protocol_) {
1122:             protocol_->SendMcpMessage(payload);
1123:         } });
1124: }
1125: 
1126: void Application::SetAecMode(AecMode mode)
1127: {
1128:     aec_mode_ = mode;
1129:     Schedule([this]()
1130:              {
1131:         auto& board = Board::GetInstance();
1132:         auto display = board.GetDisplay();
1133:         switch (aec_mode_) {
1134:         case kAecOff:
1135:             audio_service_.EnableDeviceAec(false);
1136:             display->ShowNotification(Lang::Strings::RTC_MODE_OFF);
1137:             break;
1138:         case kAecOnServerSide:
1139:             audio_service_.EnableDeviceAec(false);
1140:             display->ShowNotification(Lang::Strings::RTC_MODE_ON);
1141:             break;
1142:         case kAecOnDeviceSide:
1143:             audio_service_.EnableDeviceAec(true);
1144:             display->ShowNotification(Lang::Strings::RTC_MODE_ON);

The function handles network disconnection by closing the active audio channel and updating the display status to reflect the network state. It also manages device state transitions and plays a success sound when activation is complete.

qualitative.analysis:
The generated code does not align well semantically with the ground truth. The ground truth handles a network disconnection event (`HandleNetworkDisconnectedEvent`), focusing on closing audio channels and updating the UI status bar. In contrast, the generated code implements a network connection event handler (`HandleNetworkConnectedEvent`) that starts an activation process, which is logically different.

Structurally, both snippets interact with device states, logging, task management, and display updates, showing some similarity in used APIs and control flow patterns like state checking and UI updates. However, the core logic differs significantly—ground truth cleans up resources while the generated code initiates new processes.

Regarding functional completeness, the generated code misses critical parts present in the ground truth such as handling specific device states related to audio (e.g., `kDeviceStateConnecting`, `kDeviceStateListening`) or explicitly closing audio channels. It also introduces unrelated functionality (activation task creation), making it incomplete for the intended disconnection use case.

results.description: The `CheckAssetsVersion` function verifies the validity of the assets partition on the device's storage and ensures this check is performed only once during the application lifecycle. If the partition is invalid or disabled, it logs a warning message indicating that assets are not available for the current board configuration. This function also signals the completion of the activation process by setting an event bit in the main event group.

172: void Application::Run()
173: {
174:     const EventBits_t ALL_EVENTS =
175:         MAIN_EVENT_SCHEDULE |
176:         MAIN_EVENT_SEND_AUDIO |
177:         MAIN_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_DETECTED |
178:         MAIN_EVENT_VAD_CHANGE |
179:         MAIN_EVENT_CLOCK_TICK |
180:         MAIN_EVENT_ERROR |
181:         MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_CONNECTED |
182:         MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED |
183:         MAIN_EVENT_TOGGLE_CHAT |
184:         MAIN_EVENT_START_LISTENING |
185:         MAIN_EVENT_STOP_LISTENING |
186:         MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE |
187:         MAIN_EVENT_STATE_CHANGED;
188: 
189:     while (true)
190:     {
191:         auto bits = xEventGroupWaitBits(event_group_, ALL_EVENTS, pdTRUE, pdFALSE, portMAX_DELAY);
192: 
193:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_ERROR)
194:         {
195:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
196:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, last_error_message_.c_str(), "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
197:         }
198: 
199:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_CONNECTED)
200:         {
201:             HandleNetworkConnectedEvent();
202:         }
203: 
204:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED)
205:         {
206:             HandleNetworkDisconnectedEvent();
207:         }
208: 
209:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE)
210:         {
211:             HandleActivationDoneEvent();
212:         }
213: 
214:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_STATE_CHANGED)
215:         {
216:             HandleStateChangedEvent();
217:         }
218: 
219:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_TOGGLE_CHAT)
220:         {
221:             HandleToggleChatEvent();
222:         }
223: 
224:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_START_LISTENING)
225:         {
226:             HandleStartListeningEvent();
227:         }
228: 
229:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_STOP_LISTENING)
230:         {
231:             HandleStopListeningEvent();
232:         }
233: 
234:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_SEND_AUDIO)
235:         {
236:             while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopPacketFromSendQueue())
237:             {
238:                 if (protocol_ && !protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet)))
239:                 {
240:                     break;
241:                 }
242:             }
243:         }
244: 
245:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_DETECTED)
246:         {
247:             HandleWakeWordDetectedEvent();
248:         }
249: 
250:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_VAD_CHANGE)
251:         {
252:             if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateListening)
253:             {
254:                 auto led = Board::GetInstance().GetLed();
255:                 led->OnStateChanged();
256:             }
257:         }
258: 
259:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_SCHEDULE)
260:         {
261:             std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);
262:             auto tasks = std::move(main_tasks_);
263:             lock.unlock();
264:             for (auto &task : tasks)
265:             {
266:                 task();
267:             }
268:         }
269: 
270:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_CLOCK_TICK)
271:         {
272:             clock_ticks_++;
273:             auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
274:             display->UpdateStatusBar();
275: 
276:             // Print debug info every 10 seconds
277:             if (clock_ticks_ % 10 == 0)
278:             {

280:             }
281:         }
282:     }
283: }

410: {
411:     const int MAX_RETRY = 10;
412:     int retry_count = 0;
413:     int retry_delay = 10; // Initial retry delay in seconds
414: 
415:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
416:     while (true)
417:     {
418:         auto display = board.GetDisplay();
419:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::CHECKING_NEW_VERSION);
420: 
421:         esp_err_t err = ota_->CheckVersion();
422:         if (err != ESP_OK)
423:         {
424:             retry_count++;
425:             if (retry_count >= MAX_RETRY)
426:             {
427:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Too many retries, exit version check");
428:                 return;
429:             }
430: 
431:             char error_message[128];
432:             snprintf(error_message, sizeof(error_message), "code=%d, url=%s", err, ota_->GetCheckVersionUrl().c_str());
433:             char buffer[256];
434:             snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), Lang::Strings::CHECK_NEW_VERSION_FAILED, retry_delay, error_message);
435:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, buffer, "cloud_slash", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
436: 
437:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Check new version failed, retry in %d seconds (%d/%d)", retry_delay, retry_count, MAX_RETRY);
438:             for (int i = 0; i < retry_delay; i++)

440:                 vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
441:                 if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateIdle)
442:                 {
443:                     break;
444:                 }
445:             }

447:             continue;

451: 
452:         if (ota_->HasNewVersion())

455:             {

600:             auto payload = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "payload");
601:             if (cJSON_IsObject(payload)) {
602:                 McpServer::GetInstance().ParseMessage(payload);
603:             }
604:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "system") == 0) {
605:             auto command = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "command");
606:             if (cJSON_IsString(command)) {
607:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "System command: %s", command->valuestring);
608:                 if (strcmp(command->valuestring, "reboot") == 0) {
609:                     // Do a reboot if user requests a OTA update
610:                     Schedule([this]() {
611:                         Reboot();
612:                     });
613:                 } else {
614:                     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Unknown system command: %s", command->valuestring);
615:                 }
616:             }
617:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "alert") == 0) {
618:             auto status = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "status");
619:             auto message = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "message");
620:             auto emotion = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "emotion");
621:             if (cJSON_IsString(status) && cJSON_IsString(message) && cJSON_IsString(emotion)) {
622:                 Alert(status->valuestring, message->valuestring, emotion->valuestring, Lang::Sounds::OGG_VIBRATION);
623:             } else {
624:                 ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Alert command requires status, message and emotion");
625:             }
626: #if CONFIG_RECEIVE_CUSTOM_MESSAGE
627:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "custom") == 0) {
628:             auto payload = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "payload");
629:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Received custom message: %s", cJSON_PrintUnformatted(root));
630:             if (cJSON_IsObject(payload)) {
631:                 Schedule([this, display, payload_str = std::string(cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload))]() {
632:                     display->SetChatMessage("system", payload_str.c_str());
633:                 });
634:             } else {
635:                 ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Invalid custom message format: missing payload");
636:             }
637: #endif
638:         } else {
639:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Unknown message type: %s", type->valuestring);
640:         } });
641: 
642:     protocol_->Start();
643: }

645: void Application::ShowActivationCode(const std::string &code, const std::string &message)
646: {
647:     struct digit_sound
648:     {
649:         char digit;
650:         const std::string_view &sound;
651:     };
652:     static const std::array<digit_sound, 10> digit_sounds{{digit_sound{'0', Lang::Sounds::OGG_0},
653:                                                            digit_sound{'1', Lang::Sounds::OGG_1},
654:                                                            digit_sound{'2', Lang::Sounds::OGG_2},
655:                                                            digit_sound{'3', Lang::Sounds::OGG_3},
656:                                                            digit_sound{'4', Lang::Sounds::OGG_4},
657:                                                            digit_sound{'5', Lang::Sounds::OGG_5},
658:                                                            digit_sound{'6', Lang::Sounds::OGG_6},
659:                                                            digit_sound{'7', Lang::Sounds::OGG_7},
660:                                                            digit_sound{'8', Lang::Sounds::OGG_8},
661:                                                            digit_sound{'9', Lang::Sounds::OGG_9}}};
662: 
663:     // This sentence uses 9KB of SRAM, so we need to wait for it to finish
664:     Alert(Lang::Strings::ACTIVATION, message.c_str(), "link", Lang::Sounds::OGG_ACTIVATION);
665: 
666:     for (const auto &digit : code)
667:     {
668:         auto it = std::find_if(digit_sounds.begin(), digit_sounds.end(),
669:                                [digit](const digit_sound &ds)
670:                                { return ds.digit == digit; });
671:         if (it != digit_sounds.end())
672:         {
673:             audio_service_.PlaySound(it->sound);
674:         }
675:     }

717: void Application::HandleToggleChatEvent()
718: {
719:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
720: 
721:     if (state == kDeviceStateActivating)
722:     {
723:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
724:         return;
725:     }
726:     else if (state == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring)
727:     {
728:         audio_service_.EnableAudioTesting(true);
729:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateAudioTesting);
730:         return;
731:     }
732:     else if (state == kDeviceStateAudioTesting)
733:     {
734:         audio_service_.EnableAudioTesting(false);
735:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring);
736:         return;
737:     }
738: 
739:     if (!protocol_)
740:     {

742:         return;
743:     }
744: 
745:     if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
746:     {
747:         if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
748:         {
749:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
750:             if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
751:             {
752:                 return;
753:             }
754:         }
755: 
756:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
757:     }
758:     else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
759:     {

830: void Application::HandleWakeWordDetectedEvent()
831: {
832:     if (!protocol_)
833:     {
834:         return;
835:     }
836: 
837:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
838: 
839:     if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
840:     {
841:         audio_service_.EncodeWakeWord();
842: 
843:         if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
844:         {
845:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
846:             if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
847:             {
848:                 audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(true);
849:                 return;
850:             }
851:         }
852: 
853:         auto wake_word = audio_service_.GetLastWakeWord();
854:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Wake word detected: %s", wake_word.c_str());
855: #if CONFIG_SEND_WAKE_WORD_DATA
856:         // Encode and send the wake word data to the server
857:         while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopWakeWordPacket())
858:         {
859:             protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet));
860:         }
861:         // Set the chat state to wake word detected
862:         protocol_->SendWakeWordDetected(wake_word);
863:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
864: #else
865:         // Set flag to play popup sound after state changes to listening
866:         // (PlaySound here would be cleared by ResetDecoder in EnableVoiceProcessing)
867:         play_popup_on_listening_ = true;
868:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
869: #endif
870:     }
871:     else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
872:     {
873:         AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonWakeWordDetected);
874:     }
875:     else if (state == kDeviceStateActivating)
876:     {
877:         // Restart the activation check if the wake word is detected during activation
878:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
879:     }
880: }

882: void Application::HandleStateChangedEvent()
883: {
884:     DeviceState new_state = state_machine_.GetState();
885:     clock_ticks_ = 0;
886: 
887:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
888:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
889:     auto led = board.GetLed();
890:     led->OnStateChanged();
891: 
892:     switch (new_state)
893:     {
894:     case kDeviceStateUnknown:
895:     case kDeviceStateIdle:
896:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::STANDBY);
897:         display->SetEmotion("neutral");
898:         audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
899:         audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(true);
900:         break;
901:     case kDeviceStateConnecting:
902:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::CONNECTING);
903:         display->SetEmotion("neutral");
904:         display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
905:         break;
906:     case kDeviceStateListening:
907:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::LISTENING);
908:         display->SetEmotion("neutral");
909: 
910:         // Make sure the audio processor is running
911:         if (!audio_service_.IsAudioProcessorRunning())
912:         {
913:             // Send the start listening command
914:             protocol_->SendStartListening(listening_mode_);
915:             audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(true);
916:             audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(false);
917:         }
918: 
919:         // Play popup sound after ResetDecoder (in EnableVoiceProcessing) has been called
920:         if (play_popup_on_listening_)
921:         {
922:             play_popup_on_listening_ = false;
923:             audio_service_.PlaySound(Lang::Sounds::OGG_POPUP);
924:         }
925:         break;
926:     case kDeviceStateSpeaking:
927:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::SPEAKING);
928: 
929:         if (listening_mode_ != kListeningModeRealtime)
930:         {
931:             audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
932:             // Only AFE wake word can be detected in speaking mode
933:             audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(audio_service_.IsAfeWakeWord());
934:         }
935:         audio_service_.ResetDecoder();
936:         break;
937:     case kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring:
938:         audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
939:         audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(false);
940:         break;
941:     default:
942:         // Do nothing
943:         break;
944:     }
945: }

987: bool Application::UpgradeFirmware(const std::string &url, const std::string &version)
988: {
989:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
990:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
991: 
992:     std::string upgrade_url = url;
993:     std::string version_info = version.empty() ? "(Manual upgrade)" : version;
994: 
995:     // Close audio channel if it's open
996:     if (protocol_ && protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
997:     {
998:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Closing audio channel before firmware upgrade");
999:         protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
1000:     }
1001:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Starting firmware upgrade from URL: %s", upgrade_url.c_str());
1002: 
1003:     Alert(Lang::Strings::OTA_UPGRADE, Lang::Strings::UPGRADING, "download", Lang::Sounds::OGG_UPGRADE);
1004:     vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
1005: 
1006:     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateUpgrading);
1007: 
1008:     std::string message = std::string(Lang::Strings::NEW_VERSION) + version_info;
1009:     display->SetChatMessage("system", message.c_str());
1010: 
1011:     board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
1012:     audio_service_.Stop();
1013:     vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
1014: 
1015:     bool upgrade_success = Ota::Upgrade(upgrade_url, [display](int progress, size_t speed)
1016:                                         { std::thread([display, progress, speed]()
1017:                                                       {
1018:             char buffer[32];
1019:             snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%d%% %uKB/s", progress, speed / 1024);
1020:             display->SetChatMessage("system", buffer); })
1021:                                               .detach(); });
1022: 
1023:     if (!upgrade_success)
1024:     {
1025:         // Upgrade failed, restart audio service and continue running
1026:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Firmware upgrade failed, restarting audio service and continuing operation...");
1027:         audio_service_.Start();                             // Restart audio service
1028:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER); // Restore power save level
1029:         Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, Lang::Strings::UPGRADE_FAILED, "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
1030:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
1031:         return false;
1032:     }
1033:     else
1034:     {
1035:         // Upgrade success, reboot immediately
1036:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Firmware upgrade successful, rebooting...");
1037:         display->SetChatMessage("system", "Upgrade successful, rebooting...");
1038:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000)); // Brief pause to show message
1039:         Reboot();
1040:         return true;
1041:     }
1042: }

The `CheckAssetsVersion` function verifies the validity of the assets partition on the device's storage and ensures this check is performed only once during the application lifecycle. If the partition is invalid or disabled, it logs a warning message indicating that assets are not available for the current board configuration. This function also signals the completion of the activation process by setting an event bit in the main event group.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code implements a version of `HandleActivationDoneEvent()` but diverges significantly in logic from the ground truth.
- Ground truth focuses on initializing protocol, signaling event completion, and beginning asset version check (with early return if already checked).
- Generated code includes UI updates, audio playback, OTA handling, asset downloading, and power management — not reflected in the ground truth.
- There's no semantic alignment between what the ground truth implies (`InitializeProtocol`, signal via event group) and what is implemented in the generated code.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches (`void Application::HandleActivationDoneEvent()`), which contributes positively.
- However, control flow differs greatly; e.g., use of lambdas, threading, delays, state changes are unique to the generated code.
- APIs like `xEventGroupSetBits`, `InitializeProtocol` are missing entirely in generated code.
- No evidence of intended modular steps such as signaling activation completion or invoking `CheckAssetsVersion`.

Functional Completeness:
- The generated code introduces extensive functionality beyond implied scope: UI notifications, sound effects, OTA reset, downloading assets, applying assets, etc.
- It does not implement any part of the core responsibility suggested by the name and partial body of the ground truth — namely signaling that activation is done or checking assets version.
- Some general embedded system practices align (e.g., logging, using Board instance), but these are common patterns rather than specific functional overlaps.

Overall, while syntactically valid and internally consistent, the generated code addresses a different problem than the one implied by the ground truth snippet.

results.description: The function manages the download and application of assets, displaying progress and handling errors, while also checking for new firmware versions with retry logic and user alerts. It coordinates hardware power management and updates the display with status messages throughout the process.

45:         .skip_unhandled_events = true};
46:     esp_timer_create(&clock_timer_args, &clock_timer_handle_);
47: }

49: Application::~Application()
50: {
51:     if (clock_timer_handle_ != nullptr)
52:     {
53:         esp_timer_stop(clock_timer_handle_);
54:         esp_timer_delete(clock_timer_handle_);
55:     }
56:     vEventGroupDelete(event_group_);
57: }

59: bool Application::SetDeviceState(DeviceState state)
