64: void Application::Initialize()
65: {
66:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
67:     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateStarting);
68: 
69:     // Setup the display
70:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
71: 
72:     // Print board name/version info
73:     display->SetChatMessage("system", SystemInfo::GetUserAgent().c_str());
74: 
75:     // Setup the audio service
76:     auto codec = board.GetAudioCodec();
77:     audio_service_.Initialize(codec);
78:     audio_service_.Start();
79: 
80:     AudioServiceCallbacks callbacks;
81:     callbacks.on_send_queue_available = [this]()
82:     {
83:         xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_SEND_AUDIO);
84:     };
85:     callbacks.on_wake_word_detected = [this](const std::string &wake_word)
86:     {
87:         xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_DETECTED);
88:     };
89:     callbacks.on_vad_change = [this](bool speaking)
90:     {
91:         xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_VAD_CHANGE);
92:     };
93:     audio_service_.SetCallbacks(callbacks);
94: 
95:     // Add state change listeners
96:     state_machine_.AddStateChangeListener([this](DeviceState old_state, DeviceState new_state)
97:                                           { xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_STATE_CHANGED); });
98: 
99:     // Start the clock timer to update the status bar
100:     esp_timer_start_periodic(clock_timer_handle_, 1000000);
101: 
102:     // Add MCP common tools (only once during initialization)
103:     auto &mcp_server = McpServer::GetInstance();
104:     mcp_server.AddCommonTools();
105:     mcp_server.AddUserOnlyTools();
106: 
107:     // Set network event callback for UI updates and network state handling
108:     board.SetNetworkEventCallback([this](NetworkEvent event, const std::string &data)
109:                                   {
110:         auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
111:         
112:         switch (event) {
113:             case NetworkEvent::Scanning:
114:                 display->ShowNotification(Lang::Strings::SCANNING_WIFI, 30000);
115:                 xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED);
116:                 break;
117:             case NetworkEvent::Connecting: {
118:                 if (data.empty()) {
119:                     // Cellular network - registering without carrier info yet
120:                     display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::REGISTERING_NETWORK);
121:                 } else {
122:                     // WiFi or cellular with carrier info
123:                     std::string msg = Lang::Strings::CONNECT_TO;
124:                     msg += data;
125:                     msg += "...";
126:                     display->ShowNotification(msg.c_str(), 30000);
127:                 }
128:                 break;
129:             }
130:             case NetworkEvent::Connected: {
131:                 std::string msg = Lang::Strings::CONNECTED_TO;
132:                 msg += data;
133:                 display->ShowNotification(msg.c_str(), 30000);
134:                 xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_CONNECTED);
135:                 break;
136:             }
137:             case NetworkEvent::Disconnected:
138:                 xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED);
139:                 break;
140:             case NetworkEvent::WifiConfigModeEnter:
141:                 // WiFi config mode enter is handled by WifiBoard internally
142:                 break;
143:             case NetworkEvent::WifiConfigModeExit:
144:                 // WiFi config mode exit is handled by WifiBoard internally
145:                 break;
146:             // Cellular modem specific events
147:             case NetworkEvent::ModemDetecting:
148:                 display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::DETECTING_MODULE);
149:                 break;
150:             case NetworkEvent::ModemErrorNoSim:
151:                 Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, Lang::Strings::PIN_ERROR, "triangle_exclamation", Lang::Sounds::OGG_ERR_PIN);
152:                 break;
153:             case NetworkEvent::ModemErrorRegDenied:
154:                 Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, Lang::Strings::REG_ERROR, "triangle_exclamation", Lang::Sounds::OGG_ERR_REG);
155:                 break;
156:             case NetworkEvent::ModemErrorInitFailed:
157:                 display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::DETECTING_MODULE);
158:                 display->SetChatMessage("system", Lang::Strings::DETECTING_MODULE);
159:                 break;
160:             case NetworkEvent::ModemErrorTimeout:
161:                 display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::REGISTERING_NETWORK);
162:                 break;
163:         } });
164: 
165:     // Start network asynchronously
166:     board.StartNetwork();
167: 
168:     // Update the status bar immediately to show the network state
169:     display->UpdateStatusBar(true);
170: }
171: 
172: void Application::Run()
173: {
174:     const EventBits_t ALL_EVENTS =
175:         MAIN_EVENT_SCHEDULE |
176:         MAIN_EVENT_SEND_AUDIO |
177:         MAIN_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_DETECTED |
178:         MAIN_EVENT_VAD_CHANGE |
179:         MAIN_EVENT_CLOCK_TICK |
180:         MAIN_EVENT_ERROR |
181:         MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_CONNECTED |
182:         MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED |
183:         MAIN_EVENT_TOGGLE_CHAT |
184:         MAIN_EVENT_START_LISTENING |
185:         MAIN_EVENT_STOP_LISTENING |
186:         MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE |
187:         MAIN_EVENT_STATE_CHANGED;
188: 
189:     while (true)
190:     {
191:         auto bits = xEventGroupWaitBits(event_group_, ALL_EVENTS, pdTRUE, pdFALSE, portMAX_DELAY);
192: 
193:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_ERROR)
194:         {
195:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
196:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, last_error_message_.c_str(), "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
197:         }
198: 
199:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_CONNECTED)
200:         {
201:             HandleNetworkConnectedEvent();
202:         }
203: 
204:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED)
205:         {
206:             HandleNetworkDisconnectedEvent();
207:         }
208: 
209:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE)
210:         {
211:             HandleActivationDoneEvent();
212:         }
213: 
214:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_STATE_CHANGED)
215:         {
216:             HandleStateChangedEvent();
217:         }
218: 
219:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_TOGGLE_CHAT)
220:         {
221:             HandleToggleChatEvent();
222:         }
223: 
224:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_START_LISTENING)
225:         {
226:             HandleStartListeningEvent();
227:         }
228: 
229:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_STOP_LISTENING)
230:         {
231:             HandleStopListeningEvent();
232:         }
233: 
234:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_SEND_AUDIO)
235:         {
236:             while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopPacketFromSendQueue())
237:             {
238:                 if (protocol_ && !protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet)))
239:                 {
240:                     break;
241:                 }
242:             }
243:         }
244: 
245:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_DETECTED)
246:         {
247:             HandleWakeWordDetectedEvent();
248:         }
249: 
250:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_VAD_CHANGE)
251:         {
252:             if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateListening)
253:             {
254:                 auto led = Board::GetInstance().GetLed();
255:                 led->OnStateChanged();
256:             }
257:         }
258: 
259:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_SCHEDULE)
260:         {
261:             std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);
262:             auto tasks = std::move(main_tasks_);
263:             lock.unlock();
264:             for (auto &task : tasks)
265:             {
266:                 task();
267:             }
268:         }
269: 
270:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_CLOCK_TICK)
271:         {
272:             clock_ticks_++;
273:             auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
274:             display->UpdateStatusBar();
275: 
276:             // Print debug info every 10 seconds
277:             if (clock_ticks_ % 10 == 0)
278:             {
279:                 SystemInfo::PrintHeapStats();
280:             }
281:         }
282:     }
283: }
284: 
285: void Application::HandleNetworkConnectedEvent()
286: {
287:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Network connected");
288:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
289: 
290:     if (state == kDeviceStateStarting || state == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring)
291:     {
292:         // Network is ready, start activation
293:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateActivating);
294:         if (activation_task_handle_ != nullptr)
295:         {
296:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Activation task already running");
297:             return;
298:         }
299: 
300:         xTaskCreate([](void *arg)
301:                     {
302:             Application* app = static_cast<Application*>(arg);
303:             app->ActivationTask();
304:             app->activation_task_handle_ = nullptr;
305:             vTaskDelete(NULL); }, "activation", 4096 * 2, this, 2, &activation_task_handle_);
306:     }
307: 
308:     // Update the status bar immediately to show the network state
309:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
310:     display->UpdateStatusBar(true);
311: }
312: 
313: void Application::HandleNetworkDisconnectedEvent()
314: {
315:     // Close current conversation when network disconnected
316:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
317:     if (state == kDeviceStateConnecting || state == kDeviceStateListening || state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
318:     {
319:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Closing audio channel due to network disconnection");
320:         protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
321:     }
322: 
323:     // Update the status bar immediately to show the network state
324:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
325:     display->UpdateStatusBar(true);
326: }
327: 
328: void Application::HandleActivationDoneEvent()
329: {
330:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation done");
331: 
332:     SystemInfo::PrintHeapStats();
333:     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
334: 
335:     has_server_time_ = ota_->HasServerTime();
336: 
337:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
338:     std::string message = std::string(Lang::Strings::VERSION) + ota_->GetCurrentVersion();
339:     display->ShowNotification(message.c_str());
340:     display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
341: 
342:     // Play the success sound to indicate the device is ready
343:     audio_service_.PlaySound(Lang::Sounds::OGG_SUCCESS);
344: 
345:     // Release OTA object after activation is complete
346:     ota_.reset();
347:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
348:     board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER);
349: }
350: 
351: void Application::ActivationTask()
352: {
353:     // Create OTA object for activation process
354:     ota_ = std::make_unique<Ota>();
355: 
356:     // Check for new assets version
357:     CheckAssetsVersion();
358: 
359:     // Check for new firmware version
360:     CheckNewVersion();
361: 
362:     // Initialize the protocol
363:     InitializeProtocol();
364: 
365:     // Signal completion to main loop
366:     xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE);
367: }
368: 
369: void Application::CheckAssetsVersion()
370: {
371:     // Only allow CheckAssetsVersion to be called once
372:     if (assets_version_checked_)
373:     {
374:         return;
375:     }
376:     assets_version_checked_ = true;
377: 
378:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
379:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
380:     auto &assets = Assets::GetInstance();
381: 
382:     if (!assets.partition_valid())
383:     {
384:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Assets partition is disabled for board %s", BOARD_NAME);
385:         return;
386:     }
387: 
388:     Settings settings("assets", true);
389:     // Check if there is a new assets need to be downloaded
390:     std::string download_url = settings.GetString("download_url");
391: 
392:     if (!download_url.empty())
393:     {
394:         settings.EraseKey("download_url");
395: 
396:         char message[256];
397:         snprintf(message, sizeof(message), Lang::Strings::FOUND_NEW_ASSETS, download_url.c_str());
398:         Alert(Lang::Strings::LOADING_ASSETS, message, "cloud_arrow_down", Lang::Sounds::OGG_UPGRADE);
399: 
400:         // Wait for the audio service to be idle for 3 seconds
401:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
402:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateUpgrading);
403:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
404:         display->SetChatMessage("system", Lang::Strings::PLEASE_WAIT);
405: 
406:         bool success = assets.Download(download_url, [display](int progress, size_t speed) -> void {
407:             if (ota_->HasNewVersion())
408:             {
409:                 if (UpgradeFirmware(ota_->GetFirmwareUrl(), ota_->GetFirmwareVersion()))
410:                 {
411:                     return; // This line will never be reached after reboot
412:                 }
413:                 // If upgrade failed, continue to normal operation
414:             }
415: 
416:             // No new version, mark the current version as valid
417:             ota_->MarkCurrentVersionValid();
418:             if (!ota_->HasActivationCode() && !ota_->HasActivationChallenge())
419:             {
420:                 // Exit the loop if done checking new version
421:                 break;
422:             }
423: 
424:             display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::ACTIVATION);
425:             // Activation code is shown to the user and waiting for the user to input
426:             if (ota_->HasActivationCode())
427:             {
428:                 ShowActivationCode(ota_->GetActivationCode(), ota_->GetActivationMessage());
429:             }
430: 
431:             // This will block the loop until the activation is done or timeout
432:             for (int i = 0; i < 10; ++i)
433:             {
434:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activating... %d/%d", i + 1, 10);
435:                 esp_err_t err = ota_->Activate();
436:                 if (err == ESP_OK)
437:                 {
438:                     break;
439:                 }
440:                 else if (err == ESP_ERR_TIMEOUT)
441:                 {
442:                     vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
443:                 }
444:                 else
445:                 {
446:                     vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(10000));
447:                 }
448:                 if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateIdle)
449:                 {
450:                     break;
451:                 }
452:             }
453:     }
454:     }
455: 
456:     void Application::InitializeProtocol()
457:     {
458:         auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
459:         auto display = board.GetDisplay();
460:         auto codec = board.GetAudioCodec();
461: 
462:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::LOADING_PROTOCOL);
463: 
464:         if (ota_->HasMqttConfig())
465:         {
466:             protocol_ = std::make_unique<MqttProtocol>();
467:         }
468:         else if (ota_->HasWebsocketConfig())
469:         {
470:             protocol_ = std::make_unique<WebsocketProtocol>();
471:         }
472:         else
473:         {
474:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No protocol specified in the OTA config, using MQTT");
475:             protocol_ = std::make_unique<MqttProtocol>();
476:         }
477: 
478:         protocol_->OnConnected([this]()
479:                                { DismissAlert(); });
480: 
481:         protocol_->OnNetworkError([this](const std::string &message)
482:                                   {
483:         last_error_message_ = message;
484:         xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_ERROR); });
485: 
486:         protocol_->OnIncomingAudio([this](std::unique_ptr<AudioStreamPacket> packet)
487:                                    {
488:         if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateSpeaking) {
489:             audio_service_.PushPacketToDecodeQueue(std::move(packet));
490:         } });
491: 
492:         protocol_->OnAudioChannelOpened([this, codec, &board]()
493:                                         {
494:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
495:         if (protocol_->server_sample_rate() != codec->output_sample_rate()) {
496:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Server sample rate %d does not match device output sample rate %d, resampling may cause distortion",
497:                 protocol_->server_sample_rate(), codec->output_sample_rate());
498:         } });
499: 
500:         protocol_->OnAudioChannelClosed([this, &board]()
501:                                         {
502:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER);
503:         Schedule([this]() {
504:             auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
505:             display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
506:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
507:         }); });
508: 
509:         protocol_->OnIncomingJson([this, display](const cJSON *root)
510:                                   {
511:         // Parse JSON data
512:         auto type = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "type");
513:         if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "tts") == 0) {
514:             auto state = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "state");
515:             if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "start") == 0) {
516:                 Schedule([this]() {
517:                     aborted_ = false;
518:                     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateSpeaking);
519:                 });
520:             } else if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "stop") == 0) {
521:                 Schedule([this]() {
522:                     if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateSpeaking) {
523:                         if (listening_mode_ == kListeningModeManualStop) {
524:                             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
525:                         } else {
526:                             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateListening);
527:                         }
528:                     }
529:                 });
530:             } else if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "sentence_start") == 0) {
531:                 auto text = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "text");
532:                 if (cJSON_IsString(text)) {
533:                     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "<< %s", text->valuestring);
534:                     Schedule([this, display, message = std::string(text->valuestring)]() {
535:                         display->SetChatMessage("assistant", message.c_str());
536:                     });
537:                 }
538:             }
539:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "stt") == 0) {
540:             auto text = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "text");
541:             if (cJSON_IsString(text)) {
542:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, ">> %s", text->valuestring);
543:                 Schedule([this, display, message = std::string(text->valuestring)]() {
544:                     display->SetChatMessage("user", message.c_str());
545:                 });
546:             }
547:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "llm") == 0) {
548:             auto emotion = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "emotion");
549:             if (cJSON_IsString(emotion)) {
550:                 Schedule([this, display, emotion_str = std::string(emotion->valuestring)]() {
551:                     display->SetEmotion(emotion_str.c_str());
552:                 });
553:             }
554:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "mcp") == 0) {
555:             auto payload = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "payload");
556:             if (cJSON_IsObject(payload)) {
557:                 McpServer::GetInstance().ParseMessage(payload);
558:             }
559:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "system") == 0) {
560:             auto command = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "command");
561:             if (cJSON_IsString(command)) {
562:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "System command: %s", command->valuestring);
563:                 if (strcmp(command->valuestring, "reboot") == 0) {
564:                     // Do a reboot if user requests a OTA update
565:                     Schedule([this]() {
566:                         Reboot();
567:                     });
568:                 } else {
569:                     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Unknown system command: %s", command->valuestring);
570:                 }
571:             }
572:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "alert") == 0) {
573:             auto status = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "status");
574:             auto message = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "message");
575:             auto emotion = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "emotion");
576:             if (cJSON_IsString(status) && cJSON_IsString(message) && cJSON_IsString(emotion)) {
577:                 Alert(status->valuestring, message->valuestring, emotion->valuestring, Lang::Sounds::OGG_VIBRATION);
578:             } else {
579:                 ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Alert command requires status, message and emotion");
580:             }
581: #if CONFIG_RECEIVE_CUSTOM_MESSAGE
582:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "custom") == 0) {
583:             auto payload = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "payload");
584:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Received custom message: %s", cJSON_PrintUnformatted(root));
585:             if (cJSON_IsObject(payload)) {
586:                 Schedule([this, display, payload_str = std::string(cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload))]() {
587:                     display->SetChatMessage("system", payload_str.c_str());
588:                 });
589:             } else {
590:                 ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Invalid custom message format: missing payload");
591:             }
592: #endif
593:         } else {
594:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Unknown message type: %s", type->valuestring);
595:         } });
596: 
597:         protocol_->Start();
598:     }

600:     void Application::ShowActivationCode(const std::string &code, const std::string &message)
601:     {
602:         struct digit_sound
603:         {
604:             char digit;
605:             const std::string_view &sound;
606:         };
607:         static const std::array<digit_sound, 10> digit_sounds{{digit_sound{'0', Lang::Sounds::OGG_0},
608:                                                                digit_sound{'1', Lang::Sounds::OGG_1},
609:                                                                digit_sound{'2', Lang::Sounds::OGG_2},
610:                                                                digit_sound{'3', Lang::Sounds::OGG_3},
611:                                                                digit_sound{'4', Lang::Sounds::OGG_4},
612:                                                                digit_sound{'5', Lang::Sounds::OGG_5},
613:                                                                digit_sound{'6', Lang::Sounds::OGG_6},
614:                                                                digit_sound{'7', Lang::Sounds::OGG_7},
615:                                                                digit_sound{'8', Lang::Sounds::OGG_8},
616:                                                                digit_sound{'9', Lang::Sounds::OGG_9}}};
617: 
618:         // This sentence uses 9KB of SRAM, so we need to wait for it to finish
619:         Alert(Lang::Strings::ACTIVATION, message.c_str(), "link", Lang::Sounds::OGG_ACTIVATION);
620: 
621:         for (const auto &digit : code)
622:         {
623:             auto it = std::find_if(digit_sounds.begin(), digit_sounds.end(),
624:                                    [digit](const digit_sound &ds)
625:                                    { return ds.digit == digit; });
626:             if (it != digit_sounds.end())
627:             {
628:                 audio_service_.PlaySound(it->sound);
629:             }
630:         }
631:     }
632: 
633:     void Application::Alert(const char *status, const char *message, const char *emotion, const std::string_view &sound)
634:     {
635:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Alert [%s] %s: %s", emotion, status, message);
636:         auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
637:         display->SetStatus(status);
638:         display->SetEmotion(emotion);
639:         display->SetChatMessage("system", message);
640:         if (!sound.empty())
641:         {
642:             audio_service_.PlaySound(sound);
643:         }
644:     }

649:         {

651:             display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::STANDBY);
652:             display->SetEmotion("neutral");
653:             display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
654:         }
655:     }

672:     void Application::HandleToggleChatEvent()
673:     {
674:         auto state = GetDeviceState();
675: 
676:         if (state == kDeviceStateActivating)
677:         {
678:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
679:             return;
680:         }
681:         else if (state == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring)
682:         {
683:             audio_service_.EnableAudioTesting(true);
684:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateAudioTesting);
685:             return;
686:         }

812:             while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopWakeWordPacket())

827:         {

837:     void Application::HandleStateChangedEvent()
838:     {
839:         DeviceState new_state = state_machine_.GetState();
840:         clock_ticks_ = 0;
841: 
842:         auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
843:         auto display = board.GetDisplay();

847:         switch (new_state)

849:         case kDeviceStateUnknown:
850:         case kDeviceStateIdle:
851:             display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::STANDBY);
852:             display->SetEmotion("neutral");
853:             audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
854:             audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(true);

856:         case kDeviceStateConnecting:
857:             display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::CONNECTING);
858:             display->SetEmotion("neutral");
859:             display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
860:             break;
861:         case kDeviceStateListening:
862:             display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::LISTENING);
863:             display->SetEmotion("neutral");
864: 
865:             // Make sure the audio processor is running
866:             if (!audio_service_.IsAudioProcessorRunning())
867:             {
868:                 // Send the start listening command
869:                 protocol_->SendStartListening(listening_mode_);
870:                 audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(true);
871:                 audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(false);
872:             }
873: 
874:             // Play popup sound after ResetDecoder (in EnableVoiceProcessing) has been called
875:             if (play_popup_on_listening_)
876:             {
877:                 play_popup_on_listening_ = false;
878:                 audio_service_.PlaySound(Lang::Sounds::OGG_POPUP);
879:             }
880:             break;
881:         case kDeviceStateSpeaking:
882:             display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::SPEAKING);
883: 
884:             if (listening_mode_ != kListeningModeRealtime)
885:             {
886:                 audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
887:                 // Only AFE wake word can be detected in speaking mode
888:                 audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(audio_service_.IsAfeWakeWord());
889:             }
890:             audio_service_.ResetDecoder();
891:             break;
892:         case kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring:
893:             audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
894:             audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(false);
895:             break;
896:         default:
897:             // Do nothing
898:             break;
899:         }
900:     }

942:     bool Application::UpgradeFirmware(const std::string &url, const std::string &version)
943:     {
944:         auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
945:         auto display = board.GetDisplay();
946: 
947:         std::string upgrade_url = url;
948:         std::string version_info = version.empty() ? "(Manual upgrade)" : version;
949: 
950:         // Close audio channel if it's open
951:         if (protocol_ && protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
952:         {
953:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Closing audio channel before firmware upgrade");
954:             protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
955:         }
956:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Starting firmware upgrade from URL: %s", upgrade_url.c_str());
957: 
958:         Alert(Lang::Strings::OTA_UPGRADE, Lang::Strings::UPGRADING, "download", Lang::Sounds::OGG_UPGRADE);
959:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
960: 
961:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateUpgrading);
962: 
963:         std::string message = std::string(Lang::Strings::NEW_VERSION) + version_info;
964:         display->SetChatMessage("system", message.c_str());
965: 
966:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
967:         audio_service_.Stop();
968:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
969: 
970:         bool upgrade_success = Ota::Upgrade(upgrade_url, [display](int progress, size_t speed)
971:                                             { std::thread([display, progress, speed]()
972:                                                           {
973:             char buffer[32];
974:             snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%d%% %uKB/s", progress, speed / 1024);
975:             display->SetChatMessage("system", buffer); })
976:                                                   .detach(); });
977: 
978:         if (!upgrade_success)
979:         {
980:             // Upgrade failed, restart audio service and continue running
981:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Firmware upgrade failed, restarting audio service and continuing operation...");
982:             audio_service_.Start();                             // Restart audio service
983:             board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER); // Restore power save level
984:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, Lang::Strings::UPGRADE_FAILED, "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
985:             vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
986:             return false;
987:         }
988:         else
989:         {
990:             // Upgrade success, reboot immediately
991:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Firmware upgrade successful, rebooting...");
992:             display->SetChatMessage("system", "Upgrade successful, rebooting...");
993:             vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000)); // Brief pause to show message
994:             Reboot();
995:             return true;
996:         }
997:     }

The function manages the download and application of assets, displaying progress and handling errors, while also checking for new firmware versions with retry logic and user alerts. It coordinates hardware power management and updates the display with status messages throughout the process.

qualitative.analysis:
The generated code attempts to implement asset version checking but diverges significantly from the ground truth semantically and structurally.

Semantic Analysis:
- The generated code includes additional logic for firmware upgrades and activation that is not part of the ground truth's CheckAssetsVersion functionality.
- Ground truth focuses purely on downloading and applying assets, while generated code mixes in OTA firmware upgrade logic.
- Generated code uses a flag (assets_version_checked_) to prevent multiple executions, which is not present in ground truth.
- Progress reporting mechanism differs - ground truth uses a detached thread for UI updates, while generated code does direct updates.

Structural Differences:
- Function signature differs - ground truth appears to be part of a larger function that continues into CheckNewVersion(), while generated code is a standalone function.
- Control flow is substantially different - ground truth has a retry mechanism with exponential backoff, while generated code has a simple single attempt.
- Error handling approaches differ - ground truth has systematic retry logic, generated code shows alert and returns on failure.
- Power management calls are in different locations and contexts.

Completeness Assessment:
- Generated code covers core asset download functionality but adds extraneous features.
- Missing key elements from ground truth: retry mechanism, proper state transitions, systematic error recovery.
- Includes functionality not present in ground truth: firmware upgrade logic, activation code handling.
- Some overlap exists in asset download, progress reporting, and error messaging, but the overall approach is quite different.

The scores reflect that while there's some functional overlap, the implementations are fundamentally different in approach and scope.

results.description: The function manages device activation and firmware updates by checking for new versions, performing upgrades if available, and handling activation codes or challenges. If no update is needed, it marks the current firmware as valid and proceeds to activate the device if required. The protocol initialization sets up communication channels (MQTT/WebSocket) and handles audio streaming with power management based on connection states.

185:         MAIN_EVENT_STOP_LISTENING |
186:         MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE |
187:         MAIN_EVENT_STATE_CHANGED;
188: 
189:     while (true)
190:     {
191:         auto bits = xEventGroupWaitBits(event_group_, ALL_EVENTS, pdTRUE, pdFALSE, portMAX_DELAY);
192: 
193:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_ERROR)
194:         {
195:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
196:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, last_error_message_.c_str(), "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
197:         }
198: 
199:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_CONNECTED)
200:         {
201:             HandleNetworkConnectedEvent();
202:         }
203: 
204:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED)
205:         {
206:             HandleNetworkDisconnectedEvent();
207:         }
208: 
209:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE)
210:         {
211:             HandleActivationDoneEvent();
212:         }
213: 
214:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_STATE_CHANGED)
215:         {
216:             HandleStateChangedEvent();
217:         }
218: 
219:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_TOGGLE_CHAT)
220:         {
221:             HandleToggleChatEvent();
222:         }
223: 
224:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_START_LISTENING)
225:         {
226:             HandleStartListeningEvent();
227:         }
228: 
229:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_STOP_LISTENING)
230:         {
231:             HandleStopListeningEvent();
232:         }
233: 
234:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_SEND_AUDIO)
235:         {
236:             while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopPacketFromSendQueue())
237:             {
238:                 if (protocol_ && !protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet)))
239:                 {
240:                     break;
241:                 }
242:             }
243:         }
244: 
245:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_DETECTED)
246:         {
247:             HandleWakeWordDetectedEvent();
248:         }
249: 
250:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_VAD_CHANGE)
251:         {
252:             if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateListening)
253:             {
254:                 auto led = Board::GetInstance().GetLed();
255:                 led->OnStateChanged();
256:             }
257:         }
258: 
259:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_SCHEDULE)
260:         {
261:             std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);
262:             auto tasks = std::move(main_tasks_);
263:             lock.unlock();
264:             for (auto &task : tasks)
265:             {
266:                 task();
267:             }
268:         }
269: 
270:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_CLOCK_TICK)
271:         {
272:             clock_ticks_++;
273:             auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
274:             display->UpdateStatusBar();
275: 
276:             // Print debug info every 10 seconds
277:             if (clock_ticks_ % 10 == 0)
278:             {
279:                 SystemInfo::PrintHeapStats();
280:             }
281:         }
282:     }
283: }
284: 
285: void Application::HandleNetworkConnectedEvent()
286: {
287:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Network connected");
288:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
289: 
290:     if (state == kDeviceStateStarting || state == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring)
291:     {
292:         // Network is ready, start activation
293:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateActivating);
294:         if (activation_task_handle_ != nullptr)
295:         {
296:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Activation task already running");
297:             return;
298:         }
299: 
300:         xTaskCreate([](void *arg)
301:                     {
302:             Application* app = static_cast<Application*>(arg);
303:             app->ActivationTask();
304:             app->activation_task_handle_ = nullptr;
305:             vTaskDelete(NULL); }, "activation", 4096 * 2, this, 2, &activation_task_handle_);
306:     }
307: 
308:     // Update the status bar immediately to show the network state
309:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
310:     display->UpdateStatusBar(true);
311: }
312: 
313: void Application::HandleNetworkDisconnectedEvent()
314: {
315:     // Close current conversation when network disconnected
316:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
317:     if (state == kDeviceStateConnecting || state == kDeviceStateListening || state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
318:     {
319:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Closing audio channel due to network disconnection");
320:         protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
321:     }
322: 
323:     // Update the status bar immediately to show the network state
324:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
325:     display->UpdateStatusBar(true);
326: }
327: 
328: void Application::HandleActivationDoneEvent()
329: {
330:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation done");
331: 
332:     SystemInfo::PrintHeapStats();
333:     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
334: 
335:     has_server_time_ = ota_->HasServerTime();
336: 
337:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
338:     std::string message = std::string(Lang::Strings::VERSION) + ota_->GetCurrentVersion();
339:     display->ShowNotification(message.c_str());
340:     display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
341: 
342:     // Play the success sound to indicate the device is ready
343:     audio_service_.PlaySound(Lang::Sounds::OGG_SUCCESS);
344: 
345:     // Release OTA object after activation is complete
346:     ota_.reset();
347:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
348:     board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER);
349: }
350: 
351: void Application::ActivationTask()
352: {
353:     // Create OTA object for activation process
354:     ota_ = std::make_unique<Ota>();
355: 
356:     // Check for new assets version
357:     CheckAssetsVersion();
358: 
359:     // Check for new firmware version
360:     CheckNewVersion();
361: 
362:     // Initialize the protocol
363:     InitializeProtocol();
364: 
365:     // Signal completion to main loop
366:     xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE);
367: }
368: 
369: void Application::CheckAssetsVersion()
370: {
371:     // Only allow CheckAssetsVersion to be called once
372:     if (assets_version_checked_)
373:     {
374:         return;
375:     }
376:     assets_version_checked_ = true;
377: 
378:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
379:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
380:     auto &assets = Assets::GetInstance();
381: 
382:     if (!assets.partition_valid())
383:     {
384:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Assets partition is disabled for board %s", BOARD_NAME);
385:         return;
386:     }
387: 
388:     Settings settings("assets", true);
389:     // Check if there is a new assets need to be downloaded
390:     std::string download_url = settings.GetString("download_url");
391: 
392:     if (!download_url.empty())
393:     {
394:         settings.EraseKey("download_url");
395: 
396:         char message[256];
397:         snprintf(message, sizeof(message), Lang::Strings::FOUND_NEW_ASSETS, download_url.c_str());
398:         Alert(Lang::Strings::LOADING_ASSETS, message, "cloud_arrow_down", Lang::Sounds::OGG_UPGRADE);
399: 
400:         // Wait for the audio service to be idle for 3 seconds
401:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
402:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateUpgrading);
403:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
404:         display->SetChatMessage("system", Lang::Strings::PLEASE_WAIT);
405: 
406:         bool success = assets.Download(download_url, [display](int progress, size_t speed) -> void
407:                                        {
408:             if (ota_->HasNewVersion())
409:             {
410:                 if (UpgradeFirmware(ota_->GetFirmwareUrl(), ota_->GetFirmwareVersion()))
411:                 {
412:                     return; // This line will never be reached after reboot
413:                 }
414:                 // If upgrade failed, continue to normal operation
415:             }
416: 
417:             // No new version, mark the current version as valid
418:             ota_->MarkCurrentVersionValid();
419:             if (!ota_->HasActivationCode() && !ota_->HasActivationChallenge())
420:             {
421:                 // Exit the loop if done checking new version
422:                 break;
423:             }
424: 
425:             display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::ACTIVATION);
426:             // Activation code is shown to the user and waiting for the user to input
427:             if (ota_->HasActivationCode())
428:             {
429:                 ShowActivationCode(ota_->GetActivationCode(), ota_->GetActivationMessage());
430:             }
431: 
432:             // This will block the loop until the activation is done or timeout
433:             for (int i = 0; i < 10; ++i)
434:                     if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateSpeaking) {
435:                         if (listening_mode_ == kListeningModeManualStop) {
436:                             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
437:                         } else {
438:                             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateListening);
439:                         }
440:                     } });
441:     }
442:     else if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "sentence_start") == 0)
443:     {
444:         auto text = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "text");
445:         if (cJSON_IsString(text))
446:         {
447:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "<< %s", text->valuestring);
448:             Schedule([this, display, message = std::string(text->valuestring)]()
449:                      { display->SetChatMessage("assistant", message.c_str()); });
450:         }
451:     }
452: }
453: else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "stt") == 0)
454: {
455:     auto text = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "text");
456:     if (cJSON_IsString(text))
457:     {
458:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, ">> %s", text->valuestring);
459:         Schedule([this, display, message = std::string(text->valuestring)]()
460:                  { display->SetChatMessage("user", message.c_str()); });
461:     }
462: }
463: else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "llm") == 0)
464: {
465:     auto emotion = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "emotion");
466:     if (cJSON_IsString(emotion))
467:     {
468:         Schedule([this, display, emotion_str = std::string(emotion->valuestring)]()
469:                  { display->SetEmotion(emotion_str.c_str()); });
470:     }
471: }
472: else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "mcp") == 0)
473: {
474:     auto payload = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "payload");
475:     if (cJSON_IsObject(payload))
476:     {
477:         McpServer::GetInstance().ParseMessage(payload);
478:     }
479: }
480: else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "system") == 0)
481: {
482:     auto command = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "command");
483:     if (cJSON_IsString(command))
484:     {
485:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "System command: %s", command->valuestring);
486:         if (strcmp(command->valuestring, "reboot") == 0)
487:         {
488:             // Do a reboot if user requests a OTA update
489:             Schedule([this]()
490:                      { Reboot(); });
491:         }
492:         else
493:         {
494:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Unknown system command: %s", command->valuestring);
495:         }
496:     }
497: }
498: else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "alert") == 0)
499: {
500:     auto status = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "status");
501:     auto message = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "message");
502:     auto emotion = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "emotion");
503:     if (cJSON_IsString(status) && cJSON_IsString(message) && cJSON_IsString(emotion))
504:     {
505:         Alert(status->valuestring, message->valuestring, emotion->valuestring, Lang::Sounds::OGG_VIBRATION);
506:     }
507:     else
508:     {
509:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Alert command requires status, message and emotion");
510:     }
511: #if CONFIG_RECEIVE_CUSTOM_MESSAGE
512: }
513: else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "custom") == 0)
514: {
515:     auto payload = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "payload");
516:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Received custom message: %s", cJSON_PrintUnformatted(root));
517:     if (cJSON_IsObject(payload))
518:     {
519:         Schedule([this, display, payload_str = std::string(cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload))]()
520:                  { display->SetChatMessage("system", payload_str.c_str()); });
521:     }
522:     else
523:     {
524:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Invalid custom message format: missing payload");
525:     }
526: #endif
527: }
528: else
529: {
530:     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Unknown message type: %s", type->valuestring);
531: }
532: });
533: 
534: protocol_->Start();
535: }

537: void Application::ShowActivationCode(const std::string &code, const std::string &message)
538: {
539:     struct digit_sound
540:     {
541:         char digit;
542:         const std::string_view &sound;
543:     };
544:     static const std::array<digit_sound, 10> digit_sounds{{digit_sound{'0', Lang::Sounds::OGG_0},
545:                                                            digit_sound{'1', Lang::Sounds::OGG_1},
546:                                                            digit_sound{'2', Lang::Sounds::OGG_2},
547:                                                            digit_sound{'3', Lang::Sounds::OGG_3},
548:                                                            digit_sound{'4', Lang::Sounds::OGG_4},
549:                                                            digit_sound{'5', Lang::Sounds::OGG_5},
550:                                                            digit_sound{'6', Lang::Sounds::OGG_6},
551:                                                            digit_sound{'7', Lang::Sounds::OGG_7},
552:                                                            digit_sound{'8', Lang::Sounds::OGG_8},
553:                                                            digit_sound{'9', Lang::Sounds::OGG_9}}};
554: 
555:     // This sentence uses 9KB of SRAM, so we need to wait for it to finish
556:     Alert(Lang::Strings::ACTIVATION, message.c_str(), "link", Lang::Sounds::OGG_ACTIVATION);
557: 
558:     for (const auto &digit : code)
559:     {
560:         auto it = std::find_if(digit_sounds.begin(), digit_sounds.end(),
561:                                [digit](const digit_sound &ds)
562:                                { return ds.digit == digit; });
563:         if (it != digit_sounds.end())
564:         {
565:             audio_service_.PlaySound(it->sound);
566:         }
567:     }
568: }

570: void Application::Alert(const char *status, const char *message, const char *emotion, const std::string_view &sound)

573:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
574:     display->SetStatus(status);
575:     display->SetEmotion(emotion);
576:     display->SetChatMessage("system", message);
577:     if (!sound.empty())
578:     {
579:         audio_service_.PlaySound(sound);
580:     }
581: }

609: void Application::HandleToggleChatEvent()
610: {
611:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
612: 
613:     if (state == kDeviceStateActivating)
614:     {
615:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
616:         return;
617:     }
618:     else if (state == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring)
619:     {
620:         audio_service_.EnableAudioTesting(true);
621:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateAudioTesting);
622:         return;
623:     }
624:     else if (state == kDeviceStateAudioTesting)
625:     {
626:         audio_service_.EnableAudioTesting(false);
627:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring);
628:         return;
629:     }
630: 
631:     if (!protocol_)
632:     {
633:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Protocol not initialized");
634:         return;
635:     }
636: 
637:     if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
638:     {
639:         if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
640:         {
641:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
642:             if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
643:             {
644:                 return;
645:             }
646:         }
647: 
648:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
649:     }
650:     else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
651:     {
652:         AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonNone);
653:     }
654:     else if (state == kDeviceStateListening)
655:     {
656:         protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
657:     }
658: }
659: 
660: void Application::HandleStartListeningEvent()
661: {
662:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
663: 
664:     if (state == kDeviceStateActivating)
665:     {
666:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
667:         return;
668:     }
669:     else if (state == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring)
670:     {
671:         audio_service_.EnableAudioTesting(true);
672:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateAudioTesting);
673:         return;
674:     }
675: 
676:     if (!protocol_)
677:     {
678:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Protocol not initialized");
679:         return;
680:     }
681: 
682:     if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
683:     {
684:         if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
685:         {
686:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
687:             if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
688:             {
689:                 return;
690:             }
691:         }
692: 
693:         SetListeningMode(kListeningModeManualStop);
694:     }
695:     else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
696:     {
697:         AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonNone);
698:         SetListeningMode(kListeningModeManualStop);
699:     }
700: }

702: void Application::HandleStopListeningEvent()
703: {
704:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
705: 
706:     if (state == kDeviceStateAudioTesting)
707:     {
708:         audio_service_.EnableAudioTesting(false);
709:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring);
710:         return;
711:     }
712:     else if (state == kDeviceStateListening)
713:     {
714:         if (protocol_)
715:         {
716:             protocol_->SendStopListening();
717:         }
718:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
719:     }
720: }
721: 
722: void Application::HandleWakeWordDetectedEvent()
723: {
724:     if (!protocol_)
725:     {
726:         return;
727:     }
728: 
729:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
730: 
731:     if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
732:     {
733:         audio_service_.EncodeWakeWord();
734: 
735:         if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
736:         {
737:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
738:             if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
739:             {
740:                 audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(true);
741:                 return;
742:             }
743:         }
744: 
745:         auto wake_word = audio_service_.GetLastWakeWord();
746:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Wake word detected: %s", wake_word.c_str());
747: #if CONFIG_SEND_WAKE_WORD_DATA
748:         // Encode and send the wake word data to the server
749:         while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopWakeWordPacket())
750:         {
751:             protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet));
752:         }
753:         // Set the chat state to wake word detected
754:         protocol_->SendWakeWordDetected(wake_word);
755:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
756: #else
757:         // Set flag to play popup sound after state changes to listening
758:         // (PlaySound here would be cleared by ResetDecoder in EnableVoiceProcessing)
759:         play_popup_on_listening_ = true;
760:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
761: #endif
762:     }
763:     else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
764:     {
765:         AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonWakeWordDetected);
766:     }
767:     else if (state == kDeviceStateActivating)
768:     {
769:         // Restart the activation check if the wake word is detected during activation
770:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
771:     }
772: }
773: 
774: void Application::HandleStateChangedEvent()
775: {
776:     DeviceState new_state = state_machine_.GetState();
777:     clock_ticks_ = 0;
778: 
779:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
780:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
781:     auto led = board.GetLed();
782:     led->OnStateChanged();
783: 
784:     switch (new_state)
785:     {
786:     case kDeviceStateUnknown:
787:     case kDeviceStateIdle:
788:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::STANDBY);
789:         display->SetEmotion("neutral");
790:         audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
791:         audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(true);
792:         break;
793:     case kDeviceStateConnecting:
794:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::CONNECTING);
795:         display->SetEmotion("neutral");
796:         display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
797:         break;
798:     case kDeviceStateListening:
799:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::LISTENING);
800:         display->SetEmotion("neutral");
801: 
802:         // Make sure the audio processor is running
803:         if (!audio_service_.IsAudioProcessorRunning())
804:         {
805:             // Send the start listening command
806:             protocol_->SendStartListening(listening_mode_);
807:             audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(true);
808:             audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(false);
809:         }
810: 
811:         // Play popup sound after ResetDecoder (in EnableVoiceProcessing) has been called
812:         if (play_popup_on_listening_)
813:         {
814:             play_popup_on_listening_ = false;
815:             audio_service_.PlaySound(Lang::Sounds::OGG_POPUP);
816:         }
817:         break;
818:     case kDeviceStateSpeaking:
819:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::SPEAKING);
820: 
821:         if (listening_mode_ != kListeningModeRealtime)
822:         {
823:             audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
824:             // Only AFE wake word can be detected in speaking mode
825:             audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(audio_service_.IsAfeWakeWord());
826:         }
827:         audio_service_.ResetDecoder();
828:         break;
829:     case kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring:
830:         audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
831:         audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(false);
832:         break;
833:     default:
834:         // Do nothing
835:         break;
836:     }
837: }

839: void Application::Schedule(std::function<void()> &&callback)
840: {
841:     {
842:         std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);
843:         main_tasks_.push_back(std::move(callback));
844:     }
845:     xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_SCHEDULE);
846: }

848: void Application::AbortSpeaking(AbortReason reason)
849: {
850:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Abort speaking");
851:     aborted_ = true;
852:     if (protocol_)
853:     {
854:         protocol_->SendAbortSpeaking(reason);
855:     }

864: void Application::Reboot()
865: {
866:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Rebooting...");
867:     // Disconnect the audio channel
868:     if (protocol_ && protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
869:     {
870:         protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
871:     }
872:     protocol_.reset();
873:     audio_service_.Stop();
874: 
875:     vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
876:     esp_restart();
877: }
878: 
879: bool Application::UpgradeFirmware(const std::string &url, const std::string &version)
880: {
881:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
882:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
883: 
884:     std::string upgrade_url = url;
885:     std::string version_info = version.empty() ? "(Manual upgrade)" : version;
886: 
887:     // Close audio channel if it's open
888:     if (protocol_ && protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
889:     {
890:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Closing audio channel before firmware upgrade");
891:         protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
892:     }
893:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Starting firmware upgrade from URL: %s", upgrade_url.c_str());
894: 
895:     Alert(Lang::Strings::OTA_UPGRADE, Lang::Strings::UPGRADING, "download", Lang::Sounds::OGG_UPGRADE);
896:     vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
897: 
898:     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateUpgrading);
899: 
900:     std::string message = std::string(Lang::Strings::NEW_VERSION) + version_info;
901:     display->SetChatMessage("system", message.c_str());
902: 
903:     board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
904:     audio_service_.Stop();
905:     vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
906: 
907:     bool upgrade_success = Ota::Upgrade(upgrade_url, [display](int progress, size_t speed)
908:                                         { std::thread([display, progress, speed]()
909:                                                       {
910:             char buffer[32];
911:             snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%d%% %uKB/s", progress, speed / 1024);
912:             display->SetChatMessage("system", buffer); })
913:                                               .detach(); });
914: 
915:     if (!upgrade_success)
916:     {
917:         // Upgrade failed, restart audio service and continue running
918:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Firmware upgrade failed, restarting audio service and continuing operation...");
919:         audio_service_.Start();                             // Restart audio service
920:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER); // Restore power save level
921:         Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, Lang::Strings::UPGRADE_FAILED, "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
922:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
923:         return false;
924:     }
925:     else
926:     {
927:         // Upgrade success, reboot immediately
928:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Firmware upgrade successful, rebooting...");
929:         display->SetChatMessage("system", "Upgrade successful, rebooting...");
930:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000)); // Brief pause to show message
931:         Reboot();
932:         return true;
933:     }
934: }

962:         while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopWakeWordPacket())
963:         {

966:         // Set the chat state to wake word detected

974: #endif

976:     else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
977:     {

The function manages device activation and firmware updates by checking for new versions, performing upgrades if available, and handling activation codes or challenges. If no update is needed, it marks the current firmware as valid and proceeds to activate the device if required. The protocol initialization sets up communication channels (MQTT/WebSocket) and handles audio streaming with power management based on connection states.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code captures the core logic of checking for a new firmware version and performing an upgrade if available.
- It correctly marks the current version as valid when no update is found.
- However, it misses the activation loop that repeatedly attempts activation up to 10 times or until success/failure conditions are met, which is critical behavior in the ground truth.
- The early return based on `new_version_checked_` introduces different semantics than the looping construct in the original.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches (`void Application::CheckNewVersion()`).
- Uses similar APIs like `ota_->HasNewVersion()`, `ota_->GetFirmwareUrl()`, `ota_->GetFirmwareVersion()`, and `UpgradeFirmware(...)`.
- UI/status updates such as `display->SetStatus(...)` and `ShowActivationCode(...)` are included.
- Missing the for-loop structure used in activation process and several logging/error handling constructs (`ESP_LOGI`, `vTaskDelay`, etc.).
- The use of `snprintf`, `Alert`, and delays shows some structural creativity but deviates from the exact pattern in ground truth.

Functional Completeness:
- Core firmware check and upgrade path is covered.
- Marking current version as valid is implemented.
- Activation initiation via showing code/message is partially handled.
- Critical missing elements include: retry mechanism during activation, proper error handling with delays, event group signaling, protocol initialization part (which seems to be continuation in GT), and full JSON processing logic.
- Lacks integration points with other components such as protocol setup and audio stream handling found in the broader context of ground truth.

results.description: This function processes incoming JSON messages to control device behavior, manage audio and display states, and handle system commands. It updates the device state based on "play", "stop", or other triggers, displays chat messages and emotions, and executes system-level actions like rebooting. The function serves as a central message router that translates remote commands into local hardware and UI responses.

172: void Application::Run()
173: {
174:     const EventBits_t ALL_EVENTS =
175:         MAIN_EVENT_SCHEDULE |
176:         MAIN_EVENT_SEND_AUDIO |
177:         MAIN_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_DETECTED |
178:         MAIN_EVENT_VAD_CHANGE |
179:         MAIN_EVENT_CLOCK_TICK |
180:         MAIN_EVENT_ERROR |
181:         MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_CONNECTED |
182:         MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED |
183:         MAIN_EVENT_TOGGLE_CHAT |
184:         MAIN_EVENT_START_LISTENING |
185:         MAIN_EVENT_STOP_LISTENING |
186:         MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE |
187:         MAIN_EVENT_STATE_CHANGED;
188: 
189:     while (true)
190:     {
191:         auto bits = xEventGroupWaitBits(event_group_, ALL_EVENTS, pdTRUE, pdFALSE, portMAX_DELAY);
192: 
193:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_ERROR)
194:         {
195:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
196:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, last_error_message_.c_str(), "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
197:         }
198: 
199:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_CONNECTED)
200:         {
201:             HandleNetworkConnectedEvent();
202:         }
203: 
204:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED)
205:         {
206:             HandleNetworkDisconnectedEvent();
207:         }
208: 
209:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE)
210:         {
211:             HandleActivationDoneEvent();
212:         }
213: 
214:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_STATE_CHANGED)
215:         {
216:             HandleStateChangedEvent();
217:         }
218: 
219:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_TOGGLE_CHAT)
220:         {
221:             HandleToggleChatEvent();
222:         }
223: 
224:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_START_LISTENING)
225:         {
226:             HandleStartListeningEvent();
227:         }
228: 
229:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_STOP_LISTENING)
230:         {
231:             HandleStopListeningEvent();
232:         }
233: 
234:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_SEND_AUDIO)
235:         {
236:             while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopPacketFromSendQueue())
237:             {
238:                 if (protocol_ && !protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet)))
239:                 {
240:                     break;
241:                 }
242:             }
243:         }
244: 
245:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_DETECTED)
246:         {
247:             HandleWakeWordDetectedEvent();
248:         }
249: 
250:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_VAD_CHANGE)
251:         {
252:             if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateListening)
253:             {
254:                 auto led = Board::GetInstance().GetLed();
255:                 led->OnStateChanged();
256:             }
257:         }
258: 
259:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_SCHEDULE)
260:         {
261:             std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);
262:             auto tasks = std::move(main_tasks_);
263:             lock.unlock();
264:             for (auto &task : tasks)
265:             {
266:                 task();
267:             }
268:         }
269: 
270:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_CLOCK_TICK)
271:         {
272:             clock_ticks_++;
273:             auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
274:             display->UpdateStatusBar();
275: 
276:             // Print debug info every 10 seconds
277:             if (clock_ticks_ % 10 == 0)
278:             {
279:                 SystemInfo::PrintHeapStats();
280:             }
281:         }
282:     }
283: }

442:     else if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "sentence_start") == 0)
443:     {
444:         auto text = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "text");
445:         if (cJSON_IsString(text))
446:         {
447:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "<< %s", text->valuestring);
448:             Schedule([this, display, message = std::string(text->valuestring)]()
449:                      { display->SetChatMessage("assistant", message.c_str()); });
450:         }
451:     }
452: }
453: else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "stt") == 0)
454: {
455:     auto text = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "text");
456:     if (cJSON_IsString(text))
457:     {
458:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, ">> %s", text->valuestring);
459:         Schedule([this, display, message = std::string(text->valuestring)]()
460:                  { display->SetChatMessage("user", message.c_str()); });
461:     }
462: }
463: else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "llm") == 0)
464: {
465:     auto emotion = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "emotion");
466:     if (cJSON_IsString(emotion))
467:     {
468:         Schedule([this, display, emotion_str = std::string(emotion->valuestring)]()
469:                  { display->SetEmotion(emotion_str.c_str()); });
470:     }
471: }
472: else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "mcp") == 0)
473: {
474:     auto payload = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "payload");
475:     if (cJSON_IsObject(payload))
476:     {
477:         McpServer::GetInstance().ParseMessage(payload);
478:     }
479: }
480: else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "system") == 0)
481: {
482:     auto command = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "command");
483:     if (cJSON_IsString(command))
484:     {
485:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "System command: %s", command->valuestring);
486:         if (strcmp(command->valuestring, "reboot") == 0)
487:         {
488:             // Do a reboot if user requests a OTA update
489:             Schedule([this]()
490:                      { Reboot(); });
491:         }
492:         else
493:         {
494:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Unknown system command: %s", command->valuestring);
495:         }
496:     }
497: }
498: else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "alert") == 0)
499: {
500:     auto status = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "status");
501:     auto message = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "message");
502:     auto emotion = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "emotion");
503:     if (cJSON_IsString(status) && cJSON_IsString(message) && cJSON_IsString(emotion))
504:     {
505:         Alert(status->valuestring, message->valuestring, emotion->valuestring, Lang::Sounds::OGG_VIBRATION);
506:     }
507:     else
508:     {
509:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Alert command requires status, message and emotion");
510:     }
511: #if CONFIG_RECEIVE_CUSTOM_MESSAGE
512: }
513: else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "custom") == 0)
514: {
515:     auto payload = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "payload");
516:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Received custom message: %s", cJSON_PrintUnformatted(root));
517:     if (cJSON_IsObject(payload))
518:     {
519:         Schedule([this, display, payload_str = std::string(cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload))]()
520:                  { display->SetChatMessage("system", payload_str.c_str()); });
521:     }
522:     else
523:     {
524:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Invalid custom message format: missing payload");
525:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
526:         return;
527:     }
528:     else if (state == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring)
529:     {
530:         audio_service_.EnableAudioTesting(true);
531:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateAudioTesting);
532:         return;
533:     }
534: 
535:     if (!protocol_)
536:     {
537:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Protocol not initialized");
538:         return;
539:     }
540: 
541:     if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
542:     {
543:         if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
544:         {
545:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
546:             if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
547:             {
548:                 return;
549:             }
550:         }
551: 
552:         SetListeningMode(kListeningModeManualStop);
553:     }
554:     else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
555:     {
556:         AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonNone);
557:         SetListeningMode(kListeningModeManualStop);
558:     }

581: void Application::HandleWakeWordDetectedEvent()
582: {
583:     if (!protocol_)
584:     {
585:         return;
586:     }
587: 
588:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
589: 
590:     if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
591:     {
592:         audio_service_.EncodeWakeWord();
593: 
594:         if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
595:         {
596:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
597:             if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
598:             {
599:                 audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(true);
600:                 return;
601:             }
602:         }
603: 
604:         auto wake_word = audio_service_.GetLastWakeWord();
605:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Wake word detected: %s", wake_word.c_str());
606: #if CONFIG_SEND_WAKE_WORD_DATA
607:         // Encode and send the wake word data to the server
608:         while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopWakeWordPacket())
609:         {
610:             protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet));
611:         }
612:         // Set the chat state to wake word detected
613:         protocol_->SendWakeWordDetected(wake_word);
614:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
615: #else
616:         // Set flag to play popup sound after state changes to listening
617:         // (PlaySound here would be cleared by ResetDecoder in EnableVoiceProcessing)
618:         play_popup_on_listening_ = true;
619:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
620: #endif
621:     }
622:     else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
623:     {
624:         AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonWakeWordDetected);
625:     }
626:     else if (state == kDeviceStateActivating)
627:     {
628:         // Restart the activation check if the wake word is detected during activation
629:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
630:     }
631: }

633: void Application::HandleStateChangedEvent()
634: {
635:     DeviceState new_state = state_machine_.GetState();
636:     clock_ticks_ = 0;
637: 
638:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
639:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
640:     auto led = board.GetLed();
641:     led->OnStateChanged();
642: 
643:     switch (new_state)
644:     {
645:     case kDeviceStateUnknown:
646:     case kDeviceStateIdle:
647:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::STANDBY);
648:         display->SetEmotion("neutral");
649:         audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
650:         audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(true);
651:         break;
652:     case kDeviceStateConnecting:
653:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::CONNECTING);
654:         display->SetEmotion("neutral");
655:         display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
656:         break;
657:     case kDeviceStateListening:
658:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::LISTENING);
659:         display->SetEmotion("neutral");
660: 
661:         // Make sure the audio processor is running
662:         if (!audio_service_.IsAudioProcessorRunning())
663:         {
664:             // Send the start listening command
665:             protocol_->SendStartListening(listening_mode_);
666:             audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(true);
667:             audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(false);
668:         }
669: 
670:         // Play popup sound after ResetDecoder (in EnableVoiceProcessing) has been called
671:         if (play_popup_on_listening_)
672:         {
673:             play_popup_on_listening_ = false;
674:             audio_service_.PlaySound(Lang::Sounds::OGG_POPUP);
675:         }
676:         break;
677:     case kDeviceStateSpeaking:
678:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::SPEAKING);
679: 
680:         if (listening_mode_ != kListeningModeRealtime)
681:         {
682:             audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
683:             // Only AFE wake word can be detected in speaking mode
684:             audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(audio_service_.IsAfeWakeWord());
685:         }
686:         audio_service_.ResetDecoder();
687:         break;
688:     case kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring:
689:         audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
690:         audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(false);
691:         break;
692:     default:
693:         // Do nothing
694:         break;
695:     }
696: }

723: void Application::Reboot()
724: {

738: bool Application::UpgradeFirmware(const std::string &url, const std::string &version)
739: {
740:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
741:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
742: 
743:     std::string upgrade_url = url;
744:     std::string version_info = version.empty() ? "(Manual upgrade)" : version;
745: 
746:     // Close audio channel if it's open
747:     if (protocol_ && protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
748:     {
749:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Closing audio channel before firmware upgrade");
750:         protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
751:     }
752:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Starting firmware upgrade from URL: %s", upgrade_url.c_str());
753: 
754:     Alert(Lang::Strings::OTA_UPGRADE, Lang::Strings::UPGRADING, "download", Lang::Sounds::OGG_UPGRADE);
755:     vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
756: 
757:     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateUpgrading);
758: 
759:     std::string message = std::string(Lang::Strings::NEW_VERSION) + version_info;
760:     display->SetChatMessage("system", message.c_str());
761: 
762:     board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
763:     audio_service_.Stop();
764:     vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
765: 
766:     bool upgrade_success = Ota::Upgrade(upgrade_url, [display](int progress, size_t speed)
767:                                         { std::thread([display, progress, speed]()
768:                                                       {
769:             char buffer[32];
770:             snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%d%% %uKB/s", progress, speed / 1024);
771:             display->SetChatMessage("system", buffer); })
772:                                               .detach(); });
773: 
774:     if (!upgrade_success)
775:     {
776:         // Upgrade failed, restart audio service and continue running
777:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Firmware upgrade failed, restarting audio service and continuing operation...");
778:         audio_service_.Start();                             // Restart audio service
779:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER); // Restore power save level
780:         Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, Lang::Strings::UPGRADE_FAILED, "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
781:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
782:         return false;
783:     }
784:     else
785:     {
786:         // Upgrade success, reboot immediately
787:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Firmware upgrade successful, rebooting...");
788:         display->SetChatMessage("system", "Upgrade successful, rebooting...");
789:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000)); // Brief pause to show message
790:         Reboot();
791:         return true;
792:     }
793: }

803:

819: #if CONFIG_USE_AFE_WAKE_WORD || CONFIG_USE_CUSTOM_WAKE_WORD
820:         // Encode and send the wake word data to the server
821:         while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopWakeWordPacket())
822:         {
823:             protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet));
824:         }
825:         // Set the chat state to wake word detected
826:         protocol_->SendWakeWordDetected(wake_word);
827:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
828: #else

835:     else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)

881: void Application::SetAecMode(AecMode mode)

887:         auto display = board.GetDisplay();
888:         switch (aec_mode_) {

895:             display->ShowNotification(Lang::Strings::RTC_MODE_ON);
896:             break;

899:             display->ShowNotification(Lang::Strings::RTC_MODE_ON);
