This function processes incoming JSON messages to control device behavior, manage audio and display states, and handle system commands. It updates the device state based on "play", "stop", or other triggers, displays chat messages and emotions, and executes system-level actions like rebooting. The function serves as a central message router that translates remote commands into local hardware and UI responses.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code captures most of the core logic from the ground truth, such as handling different message types ("play", "stop", "sentence_start", "stt", "llm", "mcp", "system", "alert", and optionally "custom").
- It correctly parses JSON messages using cJSON and handles various fields like "type", "text", "emotion", "command", etc.
- Callback mechanisms for protocol events (on_message, on_connected, on_disconnected) are implemented semantically similar to what's implied in the ground truth.
- However, some nuances differ: e.g., in the ground truth, state transitions for "stop" depend on listening_mode_, but this conditional logic is absent in the generated version. Also, scheduling patterns vary slightly in capture-by-value semantics and scope usage.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature differs significantly: ground truth appears to be completing a constructor or setup routine ending with `protocol_->Start();`, whereas generated code defines a full method named `InitializeProtocol()` that includes initialization, configuration, and connection steps.
- Control flow is broadly similar — both parse JSON, switch over message type, and dispatch actions accordingly.
- Use of lambdas and scheduling via `Schedule(...)` is preserved, though variable capturing styles show minor differences.
- Protocol selection through preprocessor directives matches conceptually.
- Missing elements include final call to `protocol_->Start()` in generated code (present in ground truth), suggesting structural mismatch around lifecycle management.

Functional Completeness:
- Core messaging functionality is covered: JSON parsing, type-based routing, UI updates via display interface, audio service interaction, system commands, alerts, MCP support, and optional custom messages.
- Some features are simplified or omitted: device state management during stop/play operations lacks context-aware behavior seen in ground truth (`listening_mode_` handling); activation code presentation and alert dismissal functions aren't part of generated code since they're external to the snippet being compared.
- Initialization and connection procedures for protocols (MQTT/WebSocket) are more detailed in generated code than implied by ground truth, indicating possible expansion beyond original scope.
- Minor omissions like missing log outputs for certain branches reduce completeness compared to ground truth verbosity.

The function serves as a central control point for user-initiated chat interactions and configuration modes.

128:                 break;
129:             }
130:             case NetworkEvent::Connected: {
131:                 std::string msg = Lang::Strings::CONNECTED_TO;
132:                 msg += data;
133:                 display->ShowNotification(msg.c_str(), 30000);
134:                 xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_CONNECTED);
135:                 break;
136:             }
137:             case NetworkEvent::Disconnected:
138:                 xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED);
139:                 break;
140:             case NetworkEvent::WifiConfigModeEnter:
141:                 // WiFi config mode enter is handled by WifiBoard internally
142:                 break;
143:             case NetworkEvent::WifiConfigModeExit:
144:                 // WiFi config mode exit is handled by WifiBoard internally
145:                 break;
146:             // Cellular modem specific events
147:             case NetworkEvent::ModemDetecting:
148:                 display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::DETECTING_MODULE);
149:                 break;
150:             case NetworkEvent::ModemErrorNoSim:
151:                 Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, Lang::Strings::PIN_ERROR, "triangle_exclamation", Lang::Sounds::OGG_ERR_PIN);
152:                 break;
153:             case NetworkEvent::ModemErrorRegDenied:
154:                 Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, Lang::Strings::REG_ERROR, "triangle_exclamation", Lang::Sounds::OGG_ERR_REG);
155:                 break;
156:             case NetworkEvent::ModemErrorInitFailed:
157:                 display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::DETECTING_MODULE);
158:                 display->SetChatMessage("system", Lang::Strings::DETECTING_MODULE);
159:                 break;
160:             case NetworkEvent::ModemErrorTimeout:
161:                 display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::REGISTERING_NETWORK);
162:                 break;
163:         } });
164: 
165:     // Start network asynchronously
166:     board.StartNetwork();
167: 
168:     // Update the status bar immediately to show the network state
169:     display->UpdateStatusBar(true);
170: }
171: 
172: void Application::Run()
173: {
174:     const EventBits_t ALL_EVENTS =
175:         MAIN_EVENT_SCHEDULE |
176:         MAIN_EVENT_SEND_AUDIO |
177:         MAIN_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_DETECTED |
178:         MAIN_EVENT_VAD_CHANGE |
179:         MAIN_EVENT_CLOCK_TICK |
180:         MAIN_EVENT_ERROR |
181:         MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_CONNECTED |
182:         MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED |
183:         MAIN_EVENT_TOGGLE_CHAT |
184:         MAIN_EVENT_START_LISTENING |
185:         MAIN_EVENT_STOP_LISTENING |
186:         MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE |
187:         MAIN_EVENT_STATE_CHANGED;
188: 
189:     while (true)
190:     {
191:         auto bits = xEventGroupWaitBits(event_group_, ALL_EVENTS, pdTRUE, pdFALSE, portMAX_DELAY);
192: 
193:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_ERROR)
194:         {
195:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
196:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, last_error_message_.c_str(), "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
197:         }
198: 
199:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_CONNECTED)
200:         {
201:             HandleNetworkConnectedEvent();
202:         }
203: 
204:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED)
205:         {
206:             HandleNetworkDisconnectedEvent();
207:         }
208: 
209:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE)
210:         {
211:             HandleActivationDoneEvent();
212:         }
213: 
214:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_STATE_CHANGED)
215:         {
216:             HandleStateChangedEvent();
217:         }
218: 
219:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_TOGGLE_CHAT)
220:         {
221:             HandleToggleChatEvent();
222:         }
223: 
224:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_START_LISTENING)
225:         {
226:             HandleStartListeningEvent();
227:         }
228: 
229:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_STOP_LISTENING)
230:         {
231:             HandleStopListeningEvent();
232:         }
233: 
234:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_SEND_AUDIO)
235:         {
236:             while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopPacketFromSendQueue())
237:             {
238:                 if (protocol_ && !protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet)))
239:                 {
240:                     break;
241:                 }
242:             }
243:         }
244: 
245:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_DETECTED)
246:         {
247:             HandleWakeWordDetectedEvent();
248:         }
249: 
250:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_VAD_CHANGE)
251:         {
252:             if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateListening)
253:             {
254:                 auto led = Board::GetInstance().GetLed();
255:                 led->OnStateChanged();
256:             }
257:         }
258: 
259:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_SCHEDULE)
260:         {
261:             std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);
262:             auto tasks = std::move(main_tasks_);
263:             lock.unlock();
264:             for (auto &task : tasks)
265:             {
266:                 task();
267:             }
268:         }
269: 
270:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_CLOCK_TICK)
271:         {
272:             clock_ticks_++;
273:             auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
274:             display->UpdateStatusBar();
275: 
276:             // Print debug info every 10 seconds
277:             if (clock_ticks_ % 10 == 0)
278:             {
279:                 SystemInfo::PrintHeapStats();
280:             }
281:         }
282:     }
283: }

285: void Application::HandleNetworkConnectedEvent()
286: {
287:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Network connected");
288:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
289: 
290:     if (state == kDeviceStateStarting || state == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring)
291:     {
292:         // Network is ready, start activation
293:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateActivating);
294:         if (activation_task_handle_ != nullptr)
295:         {
296:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Activation task already running");
297:             return;
298:         }
299: 
300:         xTaskCreate([](void *arg)
301:                     {
302:             Application* app = static_cast<Application*>(arg);
303:             app->ActivationTask();
304:             app->activation_task_handle_ = nullptr;
305:             vTaskDelete(NULL); }, "activation", 4096 * 2, this, 2, &activation_task_handle_);
306:     }
307: 
308:     // Update the status bar immediately to show the network state
309:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
310:     display->UpdateStatusBar(true);
311: }
312: 
313: void Application::HandleNetworkDisconnectedEvent()
314: {
315:     // Close current conversation when network disconnected
316:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
317:     if (state == kDeviceStateConnecting || state == kDeviceStateListening || state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
318:     {
319:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Closing audio channel due to network disconnection");
320:         protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
321:     }
322: 
323:     // Update the status bar immediately to show the network state
324:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
325:     display->UpdateStatusBar(true);
326: }
327: 
328: void Application::HandleActivationDoneEvent()
329: {
330:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation done");
331: 
332:     SystemInfo::PrintHeapStats();
333:     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
334: 
335:     has_server_time_ = ota_->HasServerTime();
336: 
337:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
338:     std::string message = std::string(Lang::Strings::VERSION) + ota_->GetCurrentVersion();
339:     display->ShowNotification(message.c_str());
340:     display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
341: 
342:     // Play the success sound to indicate the device is ready
343:     audio_service_.PlaySound(Lang::Sounds::OGG_SUCCESS);
344: 
345:     // Release OTA object after activation is complete
346:     ota_.reset();
347:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
348:     board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER);
349: }

351: void Application::ActivationTask()
352: {
353:     // Create OTA object for activation process
354:     ota_ = std::make_unique<Ota>();
355: 
356:     // Check for new assets version
357:     CheckAssetsVersion();
358: 
359:     // Check for new firmware version
360:     CheckNewVersion();
361: 
362:     // Initialize the protocol
363:     InitializeProtocol();
364: 
365:     // Signal completion to main loop
366:     xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE);
367: }

369: void Application::CheckAssetsVersion()
370: {
371:     // Only allow CheckAssetsVersion to be called once
372:     if (assets_version_checked_)
373:     {
374:         return;
375:     }
376:     assets_version_checked_ = true;
377: 
378:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
379:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
380:     auto &assets = Assets::GetInstance();
381: 
382:     if (!assets.partition_valid())
383:     {
384:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Assets partition is disabled for board %s", BOARD_NAME);
385:         return;
386:     }
387: 
388:     Settings settings("assets", true);
389:     // Check if there is a new assets need to be downloaded
390:     std::string download_url = settings.GetString("download_url");
391: 
392:     if (!download_url.empty())
393:     {
394:         settings.EraseKey("download_url");
395: 
396:         char message[256];
397:         snprintf(message, sizeof(message), Lang::Strings::FOUND_NEW_ASSETS, download_url.c_str());
398:         Alert(Lang::Strings::LOADING_ASSETS, message, "cloud_arrow_down", Lang::Sounds::OGG_UPGRADE);
399: 
400:         // Wait for the audio service to be idle for 3 seconds
401:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
402:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateUpgrading);
403:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
404:         display->SetChatMessage("system", Lang::Strings::PLEASE_WAIT);
405: 
406:         bool success = assets.Download(download_url, [display](int progress, size_t speed) -> void
407:                                        { std::thread([display, progress, speed]()
408:                                                      {
409:                 char buffer[32];
410:                 snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%d%% %uKB/s", progress, speed / 1024);
411:                 display->SetChatMessage("system", buffer); })
412:                                              .detach(); });
413: 
414:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER);
415:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
416: 
417:         if (!success)
418:         {
419:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, Lang::Strings::DOWNLOAD_ASSETS_FAILED, "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
420:             vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(2000));
421:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateActivating);
422:             return;
423:         }
424:     }
425: 
426:     // Apply assets
427:     assets.Apply();
428:     display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
429:     display->SetEmotion("microchip_ai");
430: }
431: 
432: void Application::CheckNewVersion()
433: {
434:     const int MAX_RETRY = 10;
435:     int retry_count = 0;
436:     int retry_delay = 10; // Initial retry delay in seconds
437: 
438:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
439:     while (true)
440:     {
441:         auto display = board.GetDisplay();
442:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::CHECKING_NEW_VERSION);
443: 
444:         esp_err_t err = ota_->CheckVersion();
445:         if (err != ESP_OK)
446:         {
447:             retry_count++;
448:             if (retry_count >= MAX_RETRY)
449:             {
450:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Too many retries, exit version check");
451:                 return;
452:             }
453: 
454:             char error_message[128];
455:             snprintf(error_message, sizeof(error_message), "code=%d, url=%s", err, ota_->GetCheckVersionUrl().c_str());
456:             char buffer[256];
457:             snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), Lang::Strings::CHECK_NEW_VERSION_FAILED, retry_delay, error_message);
458:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, buffer, "cloud_slash", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
459: 
460:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Check new version failed, retry in %d seconds (%d/%d)", retry_delay, retry_count, MAX_RETRY);
461:             for (int i = 0; i < retry_delay; i++)
462:             {
463:                 vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
464:                 if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateIdle)
465:                 {
466:                     break;
467:                 }
468:             }
469:             retry_delay *= 2; // Double the retry delay
470:             continue;
471:         }
472:         retry_count = 0;
473:         retry_delay = 10; // Reset retry delay
474: 
475:         if (ota_->HasNewVersion())
476:         {
477:             if (UpgradeFirmware(ota_->GetFirmwareUrl(), ota_->GetFirmwareVersion()))
478:             {
479:                 return; // This line will never be reached after reboot
480:             }
481:             // If upgrade failed, continue to normal operation
482:         }
483: 
484:         // No new version, mark the current version as valid
485:         ota_->MarkCurrentVersionValid();
486:         if (!ota_->HasActivationCode() && !ota_->HasActivationChallenge())
487:         {
488:             // Exit the loop if done checking new version
489:             break;
490:         }
491: 
492:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::ACTIVATION);
493:         // Activation code is shown to the user and waiting for the user to input
494:         if (ota_->HasActivationCode())
495:         {
496:             ShowActivationCode(ota_->GetActivationCode(), ota_->GetActivationMessage());
497:         }
498: 
499:         // This will block the loop until the activation is done or timeout
500:         for (int i = 0; i < 10; ++i)
501:         {
502:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activating... %d/%d", i + 1, 10);
503:             esp_err_t err = ota_->Activate();
504:             if (err == ESP_OK)
505:             {
506:                 break;
507:             }
508:             else if (err == ESP_ERR_TIMEOUT)
509:             {
510:                 vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
511:             }
512:             else
513:             {
514:                 vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(10000));
515:             }
516:             if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateIdle)
517:             {
518:                 break;
519:             }
520:         }
521:     }
522: }

524: void Application::InitializeProtocol()
525: {
526:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
527:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
528:     auto codec = board.GetAudioCodec();
529: 
530:     display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::LOADING_PROTOCOL);
531: 
532:     if (ota_->HasMqttConfig())
533:     {
534:         protocol_ = std::make_unique<MqttProtocol>();
535:     }
536:     else if (ota_->HasWebsocketConfig())
537:     {
538:         protocol_ = std::make_unique<WebsocketProtocol>();
539:     }
540:     else
541:     {
542:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No protocol specified in the OTA config, using MQTT");
543:         protocol_ = std::make_unique<MqttProtocol>();
544:     }
545: 
546:     protocol_->OnConnected([this]()
547:                            { DismissAlert(); });
548: 
549:     protocol_->OnNetworkError([this](const std::string &message)
550:                               {
551:         last_error_message_ = message;
552:         xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_ERROR); });
553: 
554:     protocol_->OnIncomingAudio([this](std::unique_ptr<AudioStreamPacket> packet)
555:                                {
556:         if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateSpeaking) {
557:             audio_service_.PushPacketToDecodeQueue(std::move(packet));
558:         } });
559: 
560:     protocol_->OnAudioChannelOpened([this, codec, &board]()
561:                                     {
562:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
563:         if (protocol_->server_sample_rate() != codec->output_sample_rate()) {
564:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Server sample rate %d does not match device output sample rate %d, resampling may cause distortion",
565:                 protocol_->server_sample_rate(), codec->output_sample_rate());
566:         } });
567: 
568:     protocol_->OnAudioChannelClosed([this, &board]()
569:                                     {
570:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER);
571:         Schedule([this]() {
572:             auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
573:             display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
574:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
575:         }); });
576: 
577:     protocol_->OnIncomingJson([this, display](const cJSON *root)
578:                               {
579:         // Parse JSON data
580:         auto type = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "type");
581:         if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "tts") == 0) {
582:             auto state = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "state");
583:             if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "start") == 0) {
584:                 Schedule([this]() {
585:                     aborted_ = false;
586:                     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateSpeaking);
587:                 });
588:             } else if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "stop") == 0) {
589:                 Schedule([this]() {
590:                     if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateSpeaking) {
591:                         if (listening_mode_ == kListeningModeManualStop) {
592:                             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
593:                         } else {
594:                             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateListening);
595:                         }
596:                     }
597:                 });
598:             } else if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "sentence_start") == 0) {
599:                 auto text = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "text");
600:                 if (cJSON_IsString(text)) {
601:                     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "<< %s", text->valuestring);
602:                     Schedule([this, display, message = std::string(text->valuestring)]() {
603:                         display->SetChatMessage("assistant", message.c_str());
604:                     });
605:                 }
606:             }
607:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "stt") == 0) {
608:             auto text = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "text");
609:             if (cJSON_IsString(text)) {
610:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, ">> %s", text->valuestring);
611:                 Schedule([this, display, message = std::string(text->valuestring)]() {
612:                     display->SetChatMessage("user", message.c_str());
613:                 });
614:             }
615:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "llm") == 0) {
616:             auto emotion = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "emotion");
617:             if (cJSON_IsString(emotion)) {
618:                 Schedule([this, display, emotion_str = std::string(emotion->valuestring)]() {
619:                     display->SetEmotion(emotion_str.c_str());
620:                 });
621:             }
622:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "mcp") == 0) {
623:             auto payload = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "payload");
624:             if (cJSON_IsObject(payload)) {
625:                 McpServer::GetInstance().ParseMessage(payload);
626:             }
627:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "system") == 0) {
628:             auto command = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "command");
629:             if (cJSON_IsString(command)) {
630:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "System command: %s", command->valuestring);
631:                 if (strcmp(command->valuestring, "reboot") == 0) {
632:                     // Do a reboot if user requests a OTA update
633:                     Schedule([this]() {
634:                         Reboot();
635:                     });
636:                 } else {
637:                     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Unknown system command: %s", command->valuestring);
638:                 }
639:             }
640:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "alert") == 0) {
641:             auto status = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "status");
642:             auto message = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "message");
643:             auto emotion = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "emotion");
644:             if (cJSON_IsString(status) && cJSON_IsString(message) && cJSON_IsString(emotion)) {
645:                 Alert(status->valuestring, message->valuestring, emotion->valuestring, Lang::Sounds::OGG_VIBRATION);
646:             } else {
647:                 ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Alert command requires status, message and emotion");
648:             }
649: #if CONFIG_RECEIVE_CUSTOM_MESSAGE
650:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "custom") == 0) {
651:             auto payload = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "payload");
652:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Received custom message: %s", cJSON_PrintUnformatted(root));
653:             if (cJSON_IsObject(payload)) {
654:                 Schedule([this, display, payload_str = std::string(cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload))]() {
655:                     display->SetChatMessage("system", payload_str.c_str());
656:                 });
657:             } else {
658:                 ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Invalid custom message format: missing payload");
659:             }
660: #endif
661:         } else {
662:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Unknown message type: %s", type->valuestring);
663:         } });
664: 
665:     protocol_->Start();
666: }

668: void Application::ShowActivationCode(const std::string &code, const std::string &message)
669: {
670:     struct digit_sound
671:     {
672:         char digit;
673:         const std::string_view &sound;
674:     };
675:     static const std::array<digit_sound, 10> digit_sounds{{digit_sound{'0', Lang::Sounds::OGG_0},
676:                                                            digit_sound{'1', Lang::Sounds::OGG_1},
677:                                                            digit_sound{'2', Lang::Sounds::OGG_2},
678:                                                            digit_sound{'3', Lang::Sounds::OGG_3},
679:                                                            digit_sound{'4', Lang::Sounds::OGG_4},
680:                                                            digit_sound{'5', Lang::Sounds::OGG_5},
681:                                                            digit_sound{'6', Lang::Sounds::OGG_6},
682:                                                            digit_sound{'7', Lang::Sounds::OGG_7},
683:                                                            digit_sound{'8', Lang::Sounds::OGG_8},
684:                                                            digit_sound{'9', Lang::Sounds::OGG_9}}};
685: 
686:     // This sentence uses 9KB of SRAM, so we need to wait for it to finish
687:     Alert(Lang::Strings::ACTIVATION, message.c_str(), "link", Lang::Sounds::OGG_ACTIVATION);
688: 
689:     for (const auto &digit : code)
690:     {
691:         auto it = std::find_if(digit_sounds.begin(), digit_sounds.end(),
692:                                [digit](const digit_sound &ds)
693:                                { return ds.digit == digit; });
694:         if (it != digit_sounds.end())
695:         {
696:             audio_service_.PlaySound(it->sound);
697:         }
698:     }
699: }

701: void Application::Alert(const char *status, const char *message, const char *emotion, const std::string_view &sound)
702: {
703:     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Alert [%s] %s: %s", emotion, status, message);
704:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
705:     display->SetStatus(status);
706:     display->SetEmotion(emotion);
707:     display->SetChatMessage("system", message);
708:     if (!sound.empty())
709:     {
710:         audio_service_.PlaySound(sound);
711:     }
712: }

751: void Application::HandleStartListeningEvent()
752: {
753:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
754: 
755:     if (state == kDeviceStateActivating)
756:     {
757:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
758:         return;
759:     }
760:     else if (state == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring)
761:     {
762:         audio_service_.EnableAudioTesting(true);
763:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateAudioTesting);
764:         return;
765:     }
766: 
767:     if (!protocol_)
768:     {
769:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Protocol not initialized");
770:         return;
771:     }
772: 
773:     if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
774:     {
775:         if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
776:         {
777:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);

779:             {
780:                 return;
781:             }
782:         }
783: 
784:         SetListeningMode(kListeningModeManualStop);

813: void Application::HandleWakeWordDetectedEvent()
814: {
815:     if (!protocol_)
816:     {
817:         return;
818:     }
819: 
820:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
821: 
822:     if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
823:     {
824:         audio_service_.EncodeWakeWord();
825: 
826:         if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
827:         {
828:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
829:             if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
830:             {
831:                 audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(true);
832:                 return;
833:             }
834:         }
835: 
836:         auto wake_word = audio_service_.GetLastWakeWord();
837:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Wake word detected: %s", wake_word.c_str());
838: #if CONFIG_SEND_WAKE_WORD_DATA
839:         // Encode and send the wake word data to the server
840:         while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopWakeWordPacket())
841:         {
842:             protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet));
843:         }
844:         // Set the chat state to wake word detected
845:         protocol_->SendWakeWordDetected(wake_word);
846:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
847: #else
848:         // Set flag to play popup sound after state changes to listening
849:         // (PlaySound here would be cleared by ResetDecoder in EnableVoiceProcessing)
850:         play_popup_on_listening_ = true;
851:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
852: #endif
853:     }
854:     else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
855:     {
856:         AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonWakeWordDetected);
857:     }
858:     else if (state == kDeviceStateActivating)
859:     {
860:         // Restart the activation check if the wake word is detected during activation
861:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
862:     }
863: }

865: void Application::HandleStateChangedEvent()
866: {
867:     DeviceState new_state = state_machine_.GetState();
868:     clock_ticks_ = 0;
869: 
870:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
871:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
872:     auto led = board.GetLed();
873:     led->OnStateChanged();
874: 
875:     switch (new_state)
876:     {
877:     case kDeviceStateUnknown:
878:     case kDeviceStateIdle:
879:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::STANDBY);
880:         display->SetEmotion("neutral");
881:         audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
882:         audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(true);
883:         break;
884:     case kDeviceStateConnecting:
885:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::CONNECTING);
886:         display->SetEmotion("neutral");
887:         display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
888:         break;
889:     case kDeviceStateListening:
890:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::LISTENING);
891:         display->SetEmotion("neutral");
892: 
893:         // Make sure the audio processor is running
894:         if (!audio_service_.IsAudioProcessorRunning())
895:         {
896:             // Send the start listening command
897:             protocol_->SendStartListening(listening_mode_);
898:             audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(true);
899:             audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(false);
900:         }
901: 
902:         // Play popup sound after ResetDecoder (in EnableVoiceProcessing) has been called
903:         if (play_popup_on_listening_)
904:         {
905:             play_popup_on_listening_ = false;
906:             audio_service_.PlaySound(Lang::Sounds::OGG_POPUP);
907:         }
908:         break;
909:     case kDeviceStateSpeaking:
910:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::SPEAKING);
911: 
912:         if (listening_mode_ != kListeningModeRealtime)
913:         {
914:             audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
915:             // Only AFE wake word can be detected in speaking mode
916:             audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(audio_service_.IsAfeWakeWord());
917:         }
918:         audio_service_.ResetDecoder();
919:         break;
920:     case kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring:
921:         audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
922:         audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(false);
923:         break;
924:     default:
925:         // Do nothing
926:         break;
927:     }
928: }

970: bool Application::UpgradeFirmware(const std::string &url, const std::string &version)
971: {
972:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
973:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
974: 
975:     std::string upgrade_url = url;
976:     std::string version_info = version.empty() ? "(Manual upgrade)" : version;
977: 
978:     // Close audio channel if it's open
979:     if (protocol_ && protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
980:     {
981:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Closing audio channel before firmware upgrade");
982:         protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
983:     }
984:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Starting firmware upgrade from URL: %s", upgrade_url.c_str());
985: 
986:     Alert(Lang::Strings::OTA_UPGRADE, Lang::Strings::UPGRADING, "download", Lang::Sounds::OGG_UPGRADE);
987:     vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
988: 
989:     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateUpgrading);
990: 
991:     std::string message = std::string(Lang::Strings::NEW_VERSION) + version_info;
992:     display->SetChatMessage("system", message.c_str());
993: 
994:     board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
995:     audio_service_.Stop();
996:     vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
997: 
998:     bool upgrade_success = Ota::Upgrade(upgrade_url, [display](int progress, size_t speed)
999:                                         { std::thread([display, progress, speed]()
1000:                                                       {
1001:             char buffer[32];
1002:             snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%d%% %uKB/s", progress, speed / 1024);
1003:             display->SetChatMessage("system", buffer); })
1004:                                               .detach(); });
1005: 
1006:     if (!upgrade_success)
1007:     {
1008:         // Upgrade failed, restart audio service and continue running
1009:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Firmware upgrade failed, restarting audio service and continuing operation...");
1010:         audio_service_.Start();                             // Restart audio service
1011:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER); // Restore power save level
1012:         Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, Lang::Strings::UPGRADE_FAILED, "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
1013:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
1014:         return false;
1015:     }
1016:     else
1017:     {
1018:         // Upgrade success, reboot immediately
1019:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Firmware upgrade successful, rebooting...");
1020:         display->SetChatMessage("system", "Upgrade successful, rebooting...");
1021:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000)); // Brief pause to show message
1022:         Reboot();
1023:         return true;
1024:     }
1025: }

1052:         // Encode and send the wake word data to the server
1053:         while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopWakeWordPacket())
1054:         {

1057:         // Set the chat state to wake word detected

The `HandleToggleChatEvent` function manages device state transitions related to chat functionality, toggling between idle, activating, Wi-Fi configuring, and audio testing states. It also handles opening the audio channel when transitioning from idle to connecting, ensuring proper initialization of the protocol before proceeding. The function serves as a central control point for user-initiated chat interactions and configuration modes.

qualitative.analysis:
The generated code and ground truth implement entirely different functions. The ground truth handles toggling chat events and managing device states, including protocol initialization, audio channel management, and state transitions. In contrast, the generated code implements a function that plays activation codes using audio services, which involves string handling and audio playback but no state management or protocol interactions.

Semantic correctness is low because the two functions serve completely different purposes and operate on different domains (device state management vs. audio-based activation code presentation). There's no overlap in core logic or intended behavior beyond both being part of an application class.

Structure similarity is also very low since the control flows are fundamentally different: the ground truth uses multiple conditional branches for state checking while the generated code uses loops and lookups for playing sounds. Function signatures differ significantly as well - one takes no parameters and manages internal state, while the other accepts code and message strings.

Functional completeness is poor because the generated code does not address any of the key functionalities found in the ground truth such as device state management, protocol handling, audio testing enablement, or error logging. However, it does implement its own complete functionality related to activation code announcement via audio.

results.description: The function handles device state transitions and audio processing when a wake word is detected. It encodes the wake word, opens an audio channel if necessary, and sends the wake word data to the server while updating the device's listening mode based on acoustic echo cancellation settings. If the device is already speaking, it defers the wake word handling until the current audio task completes.

172: void Application::Run()
173: {
174:     const EventBits_t ALL_EVENTS =
175:         MAIN_EVENT_SCHEDULE |
176:         MAIN_EVENT_SEND_AUDIO |
177:         MAIN_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_DETECTED |
178:         MAIN_EVENT_VAD_CHANGE |
179:         MAIN_EVENT_CLOCK_TICK |
180:         MAIN_EVENT_ERROR |
181:         MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_CONNECTED |
182:         MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED |
183:         MAIN_EVENT_TOGGLE_CHAT |
184:         MAIN_EVENT_START_LISTENING |
185:         MAIN_EVENT_STOP_LISTENING |
186:         MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE |
187:         MAIN_EVENT_STATE_CHANGED;
188: 
189:     while (true)
190:     {
191:         auto bits = xEventGroupWaitBits(event_group_, ALL_EVENTS, pdTRUE, pdFALSE, portMAX_DELAY);
192: 
193:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_ERROR)
194:         {
195:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
196:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, last_error_message_.c_str(), "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
197:         }
198: 
199:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_CONNECTED)
200:         {
201:             HandleNetworkConnectedEvent();
202:         }
203: 
204:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED)
205:         {
206:             HandleNetworkDisconnectedEvent();
207:         }
208: 
209:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE)
210:         {
211:             HandleActivationDoneEvent();
212:         }
213: 
214:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_STATE_CHANGED)
215:         {
216:             HandleStateChangedEvent();
217:         }
218: 
219:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_TOGGLE_CHAT)
220:         {
221:             HandleToggleChatEvent();
222:         }
223: 
224:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_START_LISTENING)
225:         {
226:             HandleStartListeningEvent();
227:         }
228: 
229:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_STOP_LISTENING)
230:         {
231:             HandleStopListeningEvent();
232:         }
233: 
234:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_SEND_AUDIO)
235:         {
236:             while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopPacketFromSendQueue())
237:             {
238:                 if (protocol_ && !protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet)))
239:                 {
240:                     break;
241:                 }
242:             }
243:         }
244: 
245:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_DETECTED)
246:         {
247:             HandleWakeWordDetectedEvent();
248:         }
249: 
250:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_VAD_CHANGE)
251:         {
252:             if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateListening)
253:             {
254:                 auto led = Board::GetInstance().GetLed();
255:                 led->OnStateChanged();
256:             }
257:         }
258: 
259:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_SCHEDULE)
260:         {
261:             std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);
262:             auto tasks = std::move(main_tasks_);
263:             lock.unlock();
264:             for (auto &task : tasks)
265:             {
266:                 task();
267:             }
268:         }
269: 
270:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_CLOCK_TICK)
271:         {
272:             clock_ticks_++;
273:             auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
274:             display->UpdateStatusBar();
275: 
276:             // Print debug info every 10 seconds
277:             if (clock_ticks_ % 10 == 0)
278:             {
279:                 SystemInfo::PrintHeapStats();
280:             }
281:         }
282:     }
283: }
284: 
285: void Application::HandleNetworkConnectedEvent()
286: {
287:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Network connected");
288:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
289: 
290:     if (state == kDeviceStateStarting || state == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring)
291:     {
292:         // Network is ready, start activation
293:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateActivating);
294:         if (activation_task_handle_ != nullptr)
295:         {
296:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Activation task already running");
297:             return;
298:         }
299: 
300:         xTaskCreate([](void *arg)
301:                     {
302:             Application* app = static_cast<Application*>(arg);
303:             app->ActivationTask();
304:             app->activation_task_handle_ = nullptr;
305:             vTaskDelete(NULL); }, "activation", 4096 * 2, this, 2, &activation_task_handle_);
306:     }
307: 
308:     // Update the status bar immediately to show the network state
309:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
310:     display->UpdateStatusBar(true);
311: }
312: 
313: void Application::HandleNetworkDisconnectedEvent()
314: {
315:     // Close current conversation when network disconnected
316:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
317:     if (state == kDeviceStateConnecting || state == kDeviceStateListening || state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
318:     {
319:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Closing audio channel due to network disconnection");
320:         protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
321:     }
322: 
323:     // Update the status bar immediately to show the network state
324:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
325:     display->UpdateStatusBar(true);
326: }
327: 
328: void Application::HandleActivationDoneEvent()
329: {
330:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation done");
331: 
332:     SystemInfo::PrintHeapStats();
333:     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
334: 
335:     has_server_time_ = ota_->HasServerTime();
336: 
337:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
338:     std::string message = std::string(Lang::Strings::VERSION) + ota_->GetCurrentVersion();
339:     display->ShowNotification(message.c_str());
340:     display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
341: 
342:     // Play the success sound to indicate the device is ready
343:     audio_service_.PlaySound(Lang::Sounds::OGG_SUCCESS);
344: 
345:     // Release OTA object after activation is complete
346:     ota_.reset();
347:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
348:     board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER);
349: }

351: void Application::ActivationTask()
352: {
353:     // Create OTA object for activation process
354:     ota_ = std::make_unique<Ota>();
355: 
356:     // Check for new assets version
357:     CheckAssetsVersion();
358: 
359:     // Check for new firmware version
360:     CheckNewVersion();
361: 
362:     // Initialize the protocol
363:     InitializeProtocol();
364: 
365:     // Signal completion to main loop
366:     xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE);
367: }

369: void Application::CheckAssetsVersion()
370: {
371:     // Only allow CheckAssetsVersion to be called once
372:     if (assets_version_checked_)
373:     {
374:         return;
375:     }
376:     assets_version_checked_ = true;
377: 
378:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
379:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
380:     auto &assets = Assets::GetInstance();
381: 
382:     if (!assets.partition_valid())
383:     {
384:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Assets partition is disabled for board %s", BOARD_NAME);
385:         return;
386:     }
387: 
388:     Settings settings("assets", true);
389:     // Check if there is a new assets need to be downloaded
390:     std::string download_url = settings.GetString("download_url");
391: 
392:     if (!download_url.empty())
393:     {
394:         settings.EraseKey("download_url");
395: 
396:         char message[256];
397:         snprintf(message, sizeof(message), Lang::Strings::FOUND_NEW_ASSETS, download_url.c_str());
398:         Alert(Lang::Strings::LOADING_ASSETS, message, "cloud_arrow_down", Lang::Sounds::OGG_UPGRADE);
399: 
400:         // Wait for the audio service to be idle for 3 seconds
401:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
402:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateUpgrading);
403:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
404:         display->SetChatMessage("system", Lang::Strings::PLEASE_WAIT);
405: 
406:         bool success = assets.Download(download_url, [display](int progress, size_t speed) -> void
407:                                        { std::thread([display, progress, speed]()
408:                                                      {
409:                 char buffer[32];
410:                 snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%d%% %uKB/s", progress, speed / 1024);
411:                 display->SetChatMessage("system", buffer); })
412:                                              .detach(); });
413: 
414:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER);
415:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
416: 
417:         if (!success)
418:         {
419:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, Lang::Strings::DOWNLOAD_ASSETS_FAILED, "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
420:             vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(2000));
421:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateActivating);
422:             return;
423:         }
424:     }
425: 
426:     // Apply assets
427:     assets.Apply();
428:     display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
429:     display->SetEmotion("microchip_ai");
430: }
431: 
432: void Application::CheckNewVersion()
433: {
434:     const int MAX_RETRY = 10;
435:     int retry_count = 0;
436:     int retry_delay = 10; // Initial retry delay in seconds
437: 
438:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
439:     while (true)
440:     {
441:         auto display = board.GetDisplay();
442:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::CHECKING_NEW_VERSION);
443: 
444:         esp_err_t err = ota_->CheckVersion();
445:         if (err != ESP_OK)
446:         {
447:             retry_count++;
448:             if (retry_count >= MAX_RETRY)
449:             {
450:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Too many retries, exit version check");
451:                 return;
452:             }
453: 
454:             char error_message[128];
455:             snprintf(error_message, sizeof(error_message), "code=%d, url=%s", err, ota_->GetCheckVersionUrl().c_str());
456:             char buffer[256];
457:             snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), Lang::Strings::CHECK_NEW_VERSION_FAILED, retry_delay, error_message);
458:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, buffer, "cloud_slash", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
459: 
460:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Check new version failed, retry in %d seconds (%d/%d)", retry_delay, retry_count, MAX_RETRY);
461:             for (int i = 0; i < retry_delay; i++)
462:             {
463:                 vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
464:                 if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateIdle)
465:                 {
466:                     break;
467:                 }
468:             }
469:             retry_delay *= 2; // Double the retry delay
470:             continue;
471:         }
472:         retry_count = 0;
473:         retry_delay = 10; // Reset retry delay
474: 
475:         if (ota_->HasNewVersion())
476:         {
477:             if (UpgradeFirmware(ota_->GetFirmwareUrl(), ota_->GetFirmwareVersion()))
478:             {
479:                 return; // This line will never be reached after reboot
480:             }
481:             // If upgrade failed, continue to normal operation
482:         }
483: 
484:         // No new version, mark the current version as valid
485:         ota_->MarkCurrentVersionValid();
486:         if (!ota_->HasActivationCode() && !ota_->HasActivationChallenge())
487:         {
488:             // Exit the loop if done checking new version
489:             break;
490:         }
491: 
492:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::ACTIVATION);
493:         // Activation code is shown to the user and waiting for the user to input
494:         if (ota_->HasActivationCode())
495:         {
496:             ShowActivationCode(ota_->GetActivationCode(), ota_->GetActivationMessage());
497:         }
498: 
499:         // This will block the loop until the activation is done or timeout
500:         for (int i = 0; i < 10; ++i)
501:         {
502:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activating... %d/%d", i + 1, 10);
503:             esp_err_t err = ota_->Activate();
504:             if (err == ESP_OK)
505:             {
506:                 break;
507:             }
508:             else if (err == ESP_ERR_TIMEOUT)
509:             {
510:                 vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
511:             }
512:             else
513:             {
514:                 vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(10000));
515:             }
516:             if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateIdle)
517:             {
518:                 break;
519:             }
520:         }
521:     }
522: }

524: void Application::InitializeProtocol()
525: {
526:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
527:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
528:     auto codec = board.GetAudioCodec();
529: 
530:     display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::LOADING_PROTOCOL);
531: 
532:     if (ota_->HasMqttConfig())
533:     {
534:         protocol_ = std::make_unique<MqttProtocol>();
535:     }
536:     else if (ota_->HasWebsocketConfig())
537:     {
538:         protocol_ = std::make_unique<WebsocketProtocol>();
539:     }
540:     else
541:     {
542:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No protocol specified in the OTA config, using MQTT");
543:         protocol_ = std::make_unique<MqttProtocol>();
544:     }
545: 
546:     protocol_->OnConnected([this]()
547:                            { DismissAlert(); });
548: 
549:     protocol_->OnNetworkError([this](const std::string &message)
550:                               {
551:         last_error_message_ = message;
552:         xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_ERROR); });
553: 
554:     protocol_->OnIncomingAudio([this](std::unique_ptr<AudioStreamPacket> packet)
555:                                {
556:         if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateSpeaking) {
557:             audio_service_.PushPacketToDecodeQueue(std::move(packet));
558:         } });
559: 
560:     protocol_->OnAudioChannelOpened([this, codec, &board]()
561:                                     {
562:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
563:         if (protocol_->server_sample_rate() != codec->output_sample_rate()) {
564:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Server sample rate %d does not match device output sample rate %d, resampling may cause distortion",
565:                 protocol_->server_sample_rate(), codec->output_sample_rate());
566:         } });
567: 
568:     protocol_->OnAudioChannelClosed([this, &board]()
569:                                     {
570:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER);
571:         Schedule([this]() {
572:             auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
573:             display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
574:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
575:         }); });
576: 
577:     protocol_->OnIncomingJson([this, display](const cJSON *root)
578:                               {
579:         // Parse JSON data
580:         auto type = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "type");
581:         if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "tts") == 0) {
582:             auto state = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "state");
583:             if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "start") == 0) {
584:                 Schedule([this]() {
585:                     aborted_ = false;
586:                     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateSpeaking);
587:                 });
588:             } else if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "stop") == 0) {
589:                 Schedule([this]() {
590:                     if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateSpeaking) {
591:                         if (listening_mode_ == kListeningModeManualStop) {
592:                             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
593:                         } else {
594:                             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateListening);
595:                         }
596:                     }
597:                 });
598:             } else if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "sentence_start") == 0) {
599:                 auto text = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "text");
600:                 if (cJSON_IsString(text)) {
601:                     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "<< %s", text->valuestring);
602:                     Schedule([this, display, message = std::string(text->valuestring)]() {
603:                         display->SetChatMessage("assistant", message.c_str());
604:                     });
605:                 }
606:             }
607:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "stt") == 0) {
608:             auto text = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "text");
609:             if (cJSON_IsString(text)) {
610:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, ">> %s", text->valuestring);
611:                 Schedule([this, display, message = std::string(text->valuestring)]() {
612:                     display->SetChatMessage("user", message.c_str());
613:                 });
614:             }
615:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "llm") == 0) {
616:             auto emotion = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "emotion");
617:             if (cJSON_IsString(emotion)) {
618:                 Schedule([this, display, emotion_str = std::string(emotion->valuestring)]() {
619:                     display->SetEmotion(emotion_str.c_str());
620:                 });
621:             }
622:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "mcp") == 0) {
623:             auto payload = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "payload");
624:             if (cJSON_IsObject(payload)) {
625:                 McpServer::GetInstance().ParseMessage(payload);
626:             }
627:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "system") == 0) {
628:             auto command = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "command");
629:             if (cJSON_IsString(command)) {
630:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "System command: %s", command->valuestring);
631:                 if (strcmp(command->valuestring, "reboot") == 0) {
632:                     // Do a reboot if user requests a OTA update
633:                     Schedule([this]() {
634:                         Reboot();
635:                     });
636:                 } else {
637:                     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Unknown system command: %s", command->valuestring);
638:                 }
639:             }
640:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "alert") == 0) {
641:             auto status = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "status");
642:             auto message = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "message");
643:             auto emotion = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "emotion");
644:             if (cJSON_IsString(status) && cJSON_IsString(message) && cJSON_IsString(emotion)) {
645:                 Alert(status->valuestring, message->valuestring, emotion->valuestring, Lang::Sounds::OGG_VIBRATION);
646:             } else {
647:                 ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Alert command requires status, message and emotion");
648:             }
649: #if CONFIG_RECEIVE_CUSTOM_MESSAGE
650:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "custom") == 0) {
651:             auto payload = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "payload");
652:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Received custom message: %s", cJSON_PrintUnformatted(root));
653:             if (cJSON_IsObject(payload)) {
654:                 Schedule([this, display, payload_str = std::string(cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload))]() {
655:                     display->SetChatMessage("system", payload_str.c_str());
656:                 });
657:             } else {
658:                 ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Invalid custom message format: missing payload");
659:             }
660: #endif
661:         } else {
662:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Unknown message type: %s", type->valuestring);
663:         } });
664: 
665:     protocol_->Start();
666: }
667: 
668: void Application::ShowActivationCode(const std::string &code, const std::string &message)
669: {
670:     struct digit_sound
671:     {
672:         char digit;
673:         const std::string_view &sound;
674:     };
675:     static const std::array<digit_sound, 10> digit_sounds{{digit_sound{'0', Lang::Sounds::OGG_0},
676:                                                            digit_sound{'1', Lang::Sounds::OGG_1},
677:                                                            digit_sound{'2', Lang::Sounds::OGG_2},
678:                                                            digit_sound{'3', Lang::Sounds::OGG_3},
679:                                                            digit_sound{'4', Lang::Sounds::OGG_4},
680:                                                            digit_sound{'5', Lang::Sounds::OGG_5},
681:                                                            digit_sound{'6', Lang::Sounds::OGG_6},
682:                                                            digit_sound{'7', Lang::Sounds::OGG_7},
683:                                                            digit_sound{'8', Lang::Sounds::OGG_8},
684:                                                            digit_sound{'9', Lang::Sounds::OGG_9}}};
685: 
686:     // This sentence uses 9KB of SRAM, so we need to wait for it to finish
687:     Alert(Lang::Strings::ACTIVATION, message.c_str(), "link", Lang::Sounds::OGG_ACTIVATION);
688: 
689:     for (const auto &digit : code)
690:     {
691:         auto it = std::find_if(digit_sounds.begin(), digit_sounds.end(),
692:                                [digit](const digit_sound &ds)
693:                                { return ds.digit == digit; });
694:         if (it != digit_sounds.end())
695:         {
696:             audio_service_.PlaySound(it->sound);
697:         }
698:     }
699: }

701: void Application::Alert(const char *status, const char *message, const char *emotion, const std::string_view &sound)
702: {
703:     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Alert [%s] %s: %s", emotion, status, message);
704:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
705:     display->SetStatus(status);
706:     display->SetEmotion(emotion);
707:     display->SetChatMessage("system", message);
708:     if (!sound.empty())
709:     {
710:         audio_service_.PlaySound(sound);
711:     }
712: }

740: void Application::HandleToggleChatEvent()
741: {
742:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
743: 
744:     if (state == kDeviceStateActivating)
745:     {
746:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
747:         return;
748:     }
749:     else if (state == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring)
750:     {
751:         audio_service_.EnableAudioTesting(true);
752:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateAudioTesting);
753:         return;
754:     }
755:     else if (state == kDeviceStateAudioTesting)
756:     {
757:         audio_service_.EnableAudioTesting(false);
758:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring);
759:         return;
760:     }
761: 
762:     if (!protocol_)
763:     {
764:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Protocol not initialized");
765:         return;
766:     }
767: 
768:     if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
769:     {
770:         if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
771:         {
772:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
773:             if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
774:             {
775:                 return;
776:             }
777:         }
778: 
779:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
780:     }
781:     else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
782:     {
783:         AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonNone);
784:     }
785:     else if (state == kDeviceStateListening)
786:     {
787:         protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
788:     }
789: }

791: void Application::HandleStartListeningEvent()
792: {
793:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
794: 
795:     if (state == kDeviceStateActivating)
796:     {
797:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
798:         return;
799:     }
800:     else if (state == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring)
801:     {
802:         audio_service_.EnableAudioTesting(true);
803:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateAudioTesting);
804:         return;
805:     }
806: 
807:     if (!protocol_)
808:     {
809:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Protocol not initialized");
810:         return;
811:     }
812: 
813:     if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
814:     {
815:         if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
816:         {
817:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
818:             if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
819:             {
820:                 return;
821:             }
822:         }
823: 
824:         SetListeningMode(kListeningModeManualStop);
825:     }
826:     else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
827:     {
828:         AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonNone);
829:         SetListeningMode(kListeningModeManualStop);
830:     }
831: }

833: void Application::HandleStopListeningEvent()
834: {
835:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
836:     AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonWakeWordDetected);
837: }
838: else if (state == kDeviceStateActivating)
839: {
840:     // Restart the activation check if the wake word is detected during activation
841:     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
842: }
843: }
844: 
845: void Application::HandleStateChangedEvent()
846: {
847:     DeviceState new_state = state_machine_.GetState();
848:     clock_ticks_ = 0;
849: 
850:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
851:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
852:     auto led = board.GetLed();
853:     led->OnStateChanged();
854: 
855:     switch (new_state)
856:     {
857:     case kDeviceStateUnknown:
858:     case kDeviceStateIdle:
859:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::STANDBY);
860:         display->SetEmotion("neutral");
861:         audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
862:         audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(true);
863:         break;
864:     case kDeviceStateConnecting:
865:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::CONNECTING);
866:         display->SetEmotion("neutral");
867:         display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
868:         break;
869:     case kDeviceStateListening:
870:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::LISTENING);
871:         display->SetEmotion("neutral");
872: 
873:         // Make sure the audio processor is running
874:         if (!audio_service_.IsAudioProcessorRunning())
875:         {
876:             // Send the start listening command
877:             protocol_->SendStartListening(listening_mode_);
878:             audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(true);
879:             audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(false);
880:         }
881: 
882:         // Play popup sound after ResetDecoder (in EnableVoiceProcessing) has been called
883:         if (play_popup_on_listening_)
884:         {
885:             play_popup_on_listening_ = false;
886:             audio_service_.PlaySound(Lang::Sounds::OGG_POPUP);
887:         }
888:         break;
889:     case kDeviceStateSpeaking:
890:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::SPEAKING);
891: 
892:         if (listening_mode_ != kListeningModeRealtime)
893:         {
894:             audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
895:             // Only AFE wake word can be detected in speaking mode
896:             audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(audio_service_.IsAfeWakeWord());
897:         }
898:         audio_service_.ResetDecoder();
899:         break;
900:     case kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring:
901:         audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
902:         audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(false);
903:         break;
904:     default:
905:         // Do nothing
906:         break;
907:     }
908: }

950: bool Application::UpgradeFirmware(const std::string &url, const std::string &version)
951: {
952:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
953:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
954: 
955:     std::string upgrade_url = url;
956:     std::string version_info = version.empty() ? "(Manual upgrade)" : version;
957: 
958:     // Close audio channel if it's open
959:     if (protocol_ && protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
960:     {
961:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Closing audio channel before firmware upgrade");
962:         protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
963:     }
964:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Starting firmware upgrade from URL: %s", upgrade_url.c_str());
965: 
966:     Alert(Lang::Strings::OTA_UPGRADE, Lang::Strings::UPGRADING, "download", Lang::Sounds::OGG_UPGRADE);
967:     vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
968: 
969:     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateUpgrading);
970: 
971:     std::string message = std::string(Lang::Strings::NEW_VERSION) + version_info;
972:     display->SetChatMessage("system", message.c_str());
973: 
974:     board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
975:     audio_service_.Stop();
976:     vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
977: 
978:     bool upgrade_success = Ota::Upgrade(upgrade_url, [display](int progress, size_t speed)
979:                                         { std::thread([display, progress, speed]()
980:                                                       {
981:             char buffer[32];
982:             snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%d%% %uKB/s", progress, speed / 1024);
983:             display->SetChatMessage("system", buffer); })
984:                                               .detach(); });
985: 
986:     if (!upgrade_success)
987:     {
988:         // Upgrade failed, restart audio service and continue running
989:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Firmware upgrade failed, restarting audio service and continuing operation...");
990:         audio_service_.Start();                             // Restart audio service
991:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER); // Restore power save level
992:         Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, Lang::Strings::UPGRADE_FAILED, "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
993:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
994:         return false;
995:     }
996:     else
997:     {
998:         // Upgrade success, reboot immediately
999:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Firmware upgrade successful, rebooting...");
1000:         display->SetChatMessage("system", "Upgrade successful, rebooting...");
1001:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000)); // Brief pause to show message
1002:         Reboot();
1003:         return true;
1004:     }
1005: }

1007: void Application::WakeWordInvoke(const std::string &wake_word)
1008: {
1009:     if (!protocol_)
1010:     {
1011:         return;
1012:     }
1013: 
1014:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
1015: 
1016:     if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
1017:     {
1018:         audio_service_.EncodeWakeWord();
1019: 
1020:         if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
1021:         {
1022:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
1023:             if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
1024:             {
1025:                 audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(true);
1026:                 return;
1027:             }
1028:         }
1029: 
1030:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Wake word detected: %s", wake_word.c_str());
1031: #if CONFIG_USE_AFE_WAKE_WORD || CONFIG_USE_CUSTOM_WAKE_WORD
1032:         // Encode and send the wake word data to the server
1033:         while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopWakeWordPacket())
1034:         {
1035:             protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet));
1036:         }
1037:         // Set the chat state to wake word detected
1038:         protocol_->SendWakeWordDetected(wake_word);
1039:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
1040: #else
1041:         // Set flag to play popup sound after state changes to listening
1042:         // (PlaySound here would be cleared by ResetDecoder in EnableVoiceProcessing)
1043:         play_popup_on_listening_ = true;
1044:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
1045: #endif
1046:     }
1047:     else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
1048:     {
1049:         Schedule([this]()
1050:                  { AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonNone); });
1051:     }
1052:     else if (state == kDeviceStateListening)
1053:     {
1054:         Schedule([this]()
1055:                  {
1056:             if (protocol_) {
1057:                 protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
1058:             } });
1059:     }
1060: }
1061: 
1062: bool Application::CanEnterSleepMode()
1063: {
1064:     if (GetDeviceState() != kDeviceStateIdle)

1066:         return false;
1067:     }
1068: 
1069:     if (protocol_ && protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
1070:     {
1071:         return false;
1072:     }
1073: 
1074:     if (!audio_service_.IsIdle())
1075:     {
1076:         return false;
1077:     }
1078: 
1079:     // Now it is safe to enter sleep mode
1080:     return true;
1081: }

1087:              {

1089:             protocol_->SendMcpMessage(payload);
1090:         } });
1091: }

1093: void Application::SetAecMode(AecMode mode)
1094: {
1095:     aec_mode_ = mode;
1096:     Schedule([this]()
1097:              {
1098:         auto& board = Board::GetInstance();
1099:         auto display = board.GetDisplay();
1100:         switch (aec_mode_) {
1101:         case kAecOff:
1102:             audio_service_.EnableDeviceAec(false);
1103:             display->ShowNotification(Lang::Strings::RTC_MODE_OFF);
1104:             break;
1105:         case kAecOnServerSide:
1106:             audio_service_.EnableDeviceAec(false);
1107:             display->ShowNotification(Lang::Strings::RTC_MODE_ON);
1108:             break;
1109:         case kAecOnDeviceSide:
1110:             audio_service_.EnableDeviceAec(true);
1111:             display->ShowNotification(Lang::Strings::RTC_MODE_ON);
1112:             break;
1113:         }
1114: 
1115:         // If the AEC mode is changed, close the audio channel
1116:         if (protocol_ && protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened()) {
1117:             protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
1118:         } });
1119: }

1127: {
1128:     Schedule([this]()
1129:              {

1131:         if (protocol_ && protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened()) {
1132:             protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
1133:         }
1134:         // Reset protocol
1135:         protocol_.reset(); });
1136: }

</file>
The function handles device state transitions and audio processing when a wake word is detected. It encodes the wake word, opens an audio channel if necessary, and sends the wake word data to the server while updating the device's listening mode based on acoustic echo cancellation settings. If the device is already speaking, it defers the wake word handling until the current audio task completes.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code implements a ToggleChat event handler that transitions between various device states, which aligns with the general intent.
- However, it does not correctly replicate the logic for handling wake-word-related behavior from the ground truth (which seems to be part of a larger context involving `HandleWakeWordDetectedEvent`).
- It includes additional logic such as handling `kDeviceStateActivating`, which is not present in the ground truth's toggle logic.
- There are discrepancies in how certain states like `kDeviceStateListening` are handled; the ground truth sends a stop command via protocol before transitioning to idle, whereas the generated code simply closes the audio channel without changing state or sending commands.

Structure Similarity:
- Both functions follow a similar pattern of retrieving the current device state and branching based on its value.
- Function signature matches exactly (`void Application::HandleToggleChatEvent()`).
- Use of `SetDeviceState`, checks for `protocol_`, and conditional blocks based on enumerated states show structural similarity.
- However, some expected control flows (e.g., calling `protocol_->SendStopListening()` when leaving listening mode) are missing in the generated version.

Functional Completeness:
- Several critical functionalities from the ground truth are omitted:
  * No handling of wake-word encoding or sending (`audio_service_.EncodeWakeWord`, `protocol_->SendWakeWordDetected`).
  * Missing support for playing popup sounds conditionally using `play_popup_on_listening_`.
  * Lacks detailed packet-based audio transmission under #ifdef CONFIG_SEND_WAKE_WORD_DATA.
- Some behaviors appear over-implemented or misplaced, such as toggling into/out of `kDeviceStateAudioTesting` which doesn't seem relevant to a typical "toggle chat" action.
- State transition logic deviates significantly from what would be expected in a proper toggle mechanism between idle/listening/speaking modes.

Overall, while there is partial overlap in structure and naming conventions, the semantic meaning diverges due to incorrect or incomplete implementation of core interactions and workflows found in the reference code.

results.description: The `HandleStateChangedEvent` function updates the device's behavior and user interface in response to state changes. It manages audio processing modes (such as enabling or disabling wake word detection and voice processing) and updates the display status and emotion based on the current device state. Additionally, it triggers specific actions like sending a "start listening" command to the protocol manager when entering the listening state.

172: void Application::Run()
173: {
174:     const EventBits_t ALL_EVENTS =
175:         MAIN_EVENT_SCHEDULE |
176:         MAIN_EVENT_SEND_AUDIO |
177:         MAIN_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_DETECTED |
178:         MAIN_EVENT_VAD_CHANGE |
179:         MAIN_EVENT_CLOCK_TICK |
180:         MAIN_EVENT_ERROR |
181:         MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_CONNECTED |
182:         MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED |
183:         MAIN_EVENT_TOGGLE_CHAT |
184:         MAIN_EVENT_START_LISTENING |
185:         MAIN_EVENT_STOP_LISTENING |
186:         MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE |
187:         MAIN_EVENT_STATE_CHANGED;
188: 
189:     while (true)
190:     {
191:         auto bits = xEventGroupWaitBits(event_group_, ALL_EVENTS, pdTRUE, pdFALSE, portMAX_DELAY);
192: 
193:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_ERROR)
194:         {
195:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
196:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, last_error_message_.c_str(), "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
197:         }
198: 
199:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_CONNECTED)
200:         {
201:             HandleNetworkConnectedEvent();
202:         }
203: 
204:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED)
205:         {
206:             HandleNetworkDisconnectedEvent();
207:         }
208: 
209:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE)
210:         {
211:             HandleActivationDoneEvent();
212:         }
213: 
214:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_STATE_CHANGED)
215:         {
216:             HandleStateChangedEvent();
217:         }
218: 
219:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_TOGGLE_CHAT)
220:         {
221:             HandleToggleChatEvent();
222:         }
223: 
224:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_START_LISTENING)
225:         {
226:             HandleStartListeningEvent();
227:         }
228: 
229:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_STOP_LISTENING)
230:         {
231:             HandleStopListeningEvent();
232:         }
233: 
234:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_SEND_AUDIO)
235:         {
236:             while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopPacketFromSendQueue())
237:             {
238:                 if (protocol_ && !protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet)))
239:                 {
240:                     break;
241:                 }
242:             }
243:         }
244: 
245:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_DETECTED)
246:         {
247:             HandleWakeWordDetectedEvent();
248:         }
249: 
250:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_VAD_CHANGE)
251:         {
252:             if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateListening)
253:             {
254:                 auto led = Board::GetInstance().GetLed();
255:                 led->OnStateChanged();
256:             }
257:         }
258: 
259:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_SCHEDULE)
260:         {
261:             std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);
262:             auto tasks = std::move(main_tasks_);
263:             lock.unlock();
264:             for (auto &task : tasks)
265:             {
266:                 task();
267:             }
268:         }
269: 
270:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_CLOCK_TICK)
271:         {
272:             clock_ticks_++;
273:             auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
274:             display->UpdateStatusBar();
275: 
276:             // Print debug info every 10 seconds
277:             if (clock_ticks_ % 10 == 0)
278:             {
279:                 SystemInfo::PrintHeapStats();
280:             }
281:         }
282:     }
283: }
