1143: void Application::SendMcpMessage(const std::string &payload)
1144: {
1145:     // Always schedule to run in main task for thread safety
1146:     Schedule([this, payload = std::move(payload)]()
1147:              {
1148:         if (protocol_) {
1149:             protocol_->SendMcpMessage(payload);
1150:         } });
1151: }

1153: void Application::SetAecMode(AecMode mode)
1154: {
1155:     aec_mode_ = mode;
1156:     Schedule([this]()
1157:              {
1158:         auto& board = Board::GetInstance();
1159:         auto display = board.GetDisplay();
1160:         switch (aec_mode_) {
1161:         case kAecOff:
1162:             audio_service_.EnableDeviceAec(false);
1163:             display->ShowNotification(Lang::Strings::RTC_MODE_OFF);
1164:             break;
1165:         case kAecOnServerSide:
1166:             audio_service_.EnableDeviceAec(false);
1167:             display->ShowNotification(Lang::Strings::RTC_MODE_ON);
1168:             break;
1169:         case kAecOnDeviceSide:

403: bool Assets::Download(std::string url, std::function<void(int progress, size_t speed)> progress_callback)
404: {
405:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Downloading new version of assets from %s", url.c_str());
406: 
407:     // 取消当前资源分区的内存映射
408:     if (mmap_handle_ != 0)
409:     {
410:         esp_partition_munmap(mmap_handle_);
411:         mmap_handle_ = 0;
412:         mmap_root_ = nullptr;
413:     }
414:     checksum_valid_ = false;
415:     assets_.clear();
416: 
417:     // 下载新的资源文件
418:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
419:     auto http = network->CreateHttp(0);
420: 
421:     if (!http->Open("GET", url))
422:     {
423:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
424:         return false;
425:     }
426: 
427:     if (http->GetStatusCode() != 200)
428:     {
429:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get assets, status code: %d", http->GetStatusCode());
430:         return false;
431:     }
432: 
433:     size_t content_length = http->GetBodyLength();
434:     if (content_length == 0)
435:     {
436:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get content length");
437:         return false;
438:     }
439: 
440:     if (content_length > partition_->size)
441:     {
442:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Assets file size (%u) is larger than partition size (%lu)", content_length, partition_->size);
443:         return false;
444:     }
445: 
446:     // 定义扇区大小为4KB（ESP32的标准扇区大小）
447:     const size_t SECTOR_SIZE = esp_partition_get_main_flash_sector_size();
448: 
449:     // 计算需要擦除的扇区数量
450:     size_t sectors_to_erase = (content_length + SECTOR_SIZE - 1) / SECTOR_SIZE; // 向上取整
451:     size_t total_erase_size = sectors_to_erase * SECTOR_SIZE;
452: 
453:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Sector size: %u, content length: %u, sectors to erase: %u, total erase size: %u",
454:              SECTOR_SIZE, content_length, sectors_to_erase, total_erase_size);
455: 
456:     // 写入新的资源文件到分区，一边erase一边写入
457:     char buffer[512];
458:     size_t total_written = 0;
459:     size_t recent_written = 0;
460:     size_t current_sector = 0;
461:     auto last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
462: 
463:     while (true)
464:     {
465:         int ret = http->Read(buffer, sizeof(buffer));
466:         if (ret < 0)
467:         {
468:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to read HTTP data: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
469:             return false;
470:         }
471: 
472:         if (ret == 0)
473:         {
474:             break;
475:         }
476: 
477:         // 检查是否需要擦除新的扇区
478:         size_t write_end_offset = total_written + ret;
479:         size_t needed_sectors = (write_end_offset + SECTOR_SIZE - 1) / SECTOR_SIZE;
480: 
481:         // 擦除需要的新扇区
482:         while (current_sector < needed_sectors)
483:         {
484:             size_t sector_start = current_sector * SECTOR_SIZE;
485:             size_t sector_end = (current_sector + 1) * SECTOR_SIZE;
486: 
487:             // 确保擦除范围不超过分区大小
488:             if (sector_end > partition_->size)
489:             {
490:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Sector end (%u) exceeds partition size (%lu)", sector_end, partition_->size);
491:                 return false;
492:             }
493: 
494:             ESP_LOGD(TAG, "Erasing sector %u (offset: %u, size: %u)", current_sector, sector_start, SECTOR_SIZE);
495:             esp_err_t err = esp_partition_erase_range(partition_, sector_start, SECTOR_SIZE);
496:             if (err != ESP_OK)
497:             {
498:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to erase sector %u at offset %u: %s", current_sector, sector_start, esp_err_to_name(err));
499:                 return false;
500:             }
501: 
502:             current_sector++;
503:         }
504: 
505:         // 写入数据到分区
506:         esp_err_t err = esp_partition_write(partition_, total_written, buffer, ret);
507:         if (err != ESP_OK)
508:         {
509:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to write to assets partition at offset %u: %s", total_written, esp_err_to_name(err));
510:             return false;
511:         }
512: 
513:         total_written += ret;
514:         recent_written += ret;
515: 
516:         // 计算进度和速度
517:         if (esp_timer_get_time() - last_calc_time >= 1000000 || total_written == content_length || ret == 0)
518:         {
519:             size_t progress = total_written * 100 / content_length;
520:             size_t speed = recent_written; // 每秒的字节数
521:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Progress: %u%% (%u/%u), Speed: %u B/s, Sectors erased: %u",
522:                      progress, total_written, content_length, speed, current_sector);
523:             if (progress_callback)
524:             {
525:                 progress_callback(progress, speed);
526:             }
527:             last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
528:             recent_written = 0; // 重置最近写入的字节数
529:         }
530:     }
531: 
532:     http->Close();
533: 
534:     if (total_written != content_length)
535:     {
536:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Downloaded size (%u) does not match expected size (%u)", total_written, content_length);
537:         return false;
538:     }
539: 
540:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Assets download completed, total written: %u bytes, total sectors erased: %u",
541:              total_written, current_sector);
542: 
543:     // 重新初始化资源分区
544:     if (!InitializePartition())
545:     {
546:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to re-initialize assets partition");
547:         return false;
548:     }
549: 
550:     return true;
551: }

553: bool Assets::GetAssetData(const std::string &name, void *&ptr, size_t &size)
554: {
555:     auto asset = assets_.find(name);
556:     if (asset == assets_.end())
557:     {
558:         return false;
559:     }
560:     auto data = (const char *)(mmap_root_ + asset->second.offset);
561:     if (data[0] != 'Z' || data[1] != 'Z')
562:     {
563:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "The asset %s is not valid with magic %02x%02x", name.c_str(), data[0], data[1]);
564:         return false;
565:     }
566: 
567:     ptr = static_cast<void *>(const_cast<char *>(data + 2));
568:     size = asset->second.size;
569:     return true;
570: }

The function initializes and validates an assets partition in flash memory, ensuring it exists, has sufficient space, and passes checksum verification. It maps the partition into memory and builds an index of assets with their sizes and offsets for efficient access. Returns true if the partition is successfully initialized and validated, false otherwise.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The core idea of initializing a partition and validating its contents is preserved.
- However, the method of validation differs significantly: ground truth uses checksum comparison with stored length/data, while generated code uses a magic number and per-asset validation.
- The generated code assumes a different data layout (magic number, asset count, asset entries, then checksum), which diverges from the ground truth's approach (file count, checksum, total length, followed by asset table).
- Error handling and logging are present but target different failure points.

Structure Similarity:
- Both functions follow a general flow of finding a partition, mapping it, performing validations, and building an asset index.
- Differences include:
  * Partition search parameters (generated uses DATA/SPIFFS subtype; ground truth uses ANY/ANY).
  * Memory mapping logic is similar but applied differently due to assumed data layouts.
  * Loop structures differ in purpose: ground truth reads a fixed-size table, while generated processes variable-sized entries.
  * Use of timers/checksums occurs at different stages and for different purposes.

Functional Completeness:
- Partial overlap in functionality: both initialize a partition, validate integrity, and populate an asset map.
- Missing elements in generated version:
  * No check against available flash memory pages (`spi_flash_mmap_get_free_pages`).
  * No timing measurement of checksum calculation.
  * Different asset metadata (adds width/height not present in ground truth).
  * Assumes presence of magic number and specific data layout not found in ground truth.
- Additional features in generated version that aren't in ground truth:
  * Per-entry validation and skipping of empty assets.
  * Magic number verification.
  * Detailed string-based asset naming with null termination enforcement.

Overall, while there is conceptual alignment, implementation details and assumptions about data format cause significant divergence in behavior and correctness relative to the reference.

121: bool Assets::Download(std::string url, std::function<void(int progress, size_t speed)> progress_callback)
122: {
123:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Downloading new version of assets from %s", url.c_str());
124: 
125:     // 取消当前资源分区的内存映射
126:     if (mmap_handle_ != 0)
127:     {
128:         esp_partition_munmap(mmap_handle_);
129:         mmap_handle_ = 0;
130:         mmap_root_ = nullptr;
131:     }
132:     checksum_valid_ = false;
133:     assets_.clear();
134: 
135:     // 下载新的资源文件
136:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
137:     auto http = network->CreateHttp(0);
138: 
139:     if (!http->Open("GET", url))
140:     {
141:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
142:         return false;
143:     }
144: 
145:     if (http->GetStatusCode() != 200)
146:     {
147:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get assets, status code: %d", http->GetStatusCode());
148:         return false;
149:     }
150: 
151:     size_t content_length = http->GetBodyLength();
152:     if (content_length == 0)
153:     {
154:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get content length");
155:         return false;
156:     }
157: 
158:     if (content_length > partition_->size)
159:     {
160:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Assets file size (%u) is larger than partition size (%lu)", content_length, partition_->size);
161:         return false;
162:     }
163: 
164:     // 定义扇区大小为4KB（ESP32的标准扇区大小）
165:     const size_t SECTOR_SIZE = esp_partition_get_main_flash_sector_size();
166: 
167:     // 计算需要擦除的扇区数量
168:     size_t sectors_to_erase = (content_length + SECTOR_SIZE - 1) / SECTOR_SIZE; // 向上取整
169:     size_t total_erase_size = sectors_to_erase * SECTOR_SIZE;
170: 
171:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Sector size: %u, content length: %u, sectors to erase: %u, total erase size: %u",
172:              SECTOR_SIZE, content_length, sectors_to_erase, total_erase_size);
173: 
174:     // 写入新的资源文件到分区，一边erase一边写入
175:     char buffer[512];
176:     size_t total_written = 0;
177:     size_t recent_written = 0;
178:     size_t current_sector = 0;
179:     auto last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
180: 
181:     while (true)
182:     {
183:         int ret = http->Read(buffer, sizeof(buffer));
184:         if (ret < 0)
185:         {
186:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to read HTTP data: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
187:             return false;
188:         }
189: 
190:         if (ret == 0)
191:         {
192:             break;
193:         }
194: 
195:         // 检查是否需要擦除新的扇区
196:         size_t write_end_offset = total_written + ret;
197:         size_t needed_sectors = (write_end_offset + SECTOR_SIZE - 1) / SECTOR_SIZE;
198: 
199:         // 擦除需要的新扇区
200:         while (current_sector < needed_sectors)
201:         {
202:             size_t sector_start = current_sector * SECTOR_SIZE;
203:             size_t sector_end = (current_sector + 1) * SECTOR_SIZE;
204: 
205:             // 确保擦除范围不超过分区大小
206:             if (sector_end > partition_->size)
207:             {
208:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Sector end (%u) exceeds partition size (%lu)", sector_end, partition_->size);
209:                 return false;
210:             }
211: 
212:             ESP_LOGD(TAG, "Erasing sector %u (offset: %u, size: %u)", current_sector, sector_start, SECTOR_SIZE);
213:             esp_err_t err = esp_partition_erase_range(partition_, sector_start, SECTOR_SIZE);
214:             if (err != ESP_OK)
215:             {
216:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to erase sector %u at offset %u: %s", current_sector, sector_start, esp_err_to_name(err));
217:                 return false;
218:             }
219: 
220:             current_sector++;
221:         }
222: 
223:         // 写入数据到分区
224:         esp_err_t err = esp_partition_write(partition_, total_written, buffer, ret);
225:         if (err != ESP_OK)
226:         {
227:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to write to assets partition at offset %u: %s", total_written, esp_err_to_name(err));
228:             return false;
229:         }
230: 
231:         total_written += ret;
232:         recent_written += ret;
233: 
234:         // 计算进度和速度
235:         if (esp_timer_get_time() - last_calc_time >= 1000000 || total_written == content_length || ret == 0)
236:         {
237:             size_t progress = total_written * 100 / content_length;
238:             size_t speed = recent_written; // 每秒的字节数
239:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Progress: %u%% (%u/%u), Speed: %u B/s, Sectors erased: %u",
240:                      progress, total_written, content_length, speed, current_sector);
241:             if (progress_callback)
242:             {
243:                 progress_callback(progress, speed);
244:             }
245:             last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
246:             recent_written = 0; // 重置最近写入的字节数
247:         }
248:     }
249: 
250:     http->Close();
251: 
252:     if (total_written != content_length)
253:     {
254:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Downloaded size (%u) does not match expected size (%u)", total_written, content_length);
255:         return false;
256:     }
257: 
258:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Assets download completed, total written: %u bytes, total sectors erased: %u",
259:              total_written, current_sector);
260: 
261:     // 重新初始化资源分区
262:     if (!InitializePartition())
263:     {
264:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to re-initialize assets partition");
265:         return false;
266:     }
267: 
268:     return true;
269: }

271: bool Assets::GetAssetData(const std::string &name, void *&ptr, size_t &size)
272: {
273:     auto asset = assets_.find(name);
274:     if (asset == assets_.end())
275:     {
276:         return false;
277:     }
278:     auto data = (const char *)(mmap_root_ + asset->second.offset);
279:     if (data[0] != 'Z' || data[1] != 'Z')
280:     {
281:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "The asset %s is not valid with magic %02x%02x", name.c_str(), data[0], data[1]);
282:         return false;
283:     }
284: 
285:     ptr = static_cast<void *>(const_cast<char *>(data + 2));
286:     size = asset->second.size;
287:     return true;
288: }

The `Assets::Apply()` function loads and applies system assets from an "index.json" configuration file, including speech recognition models and UI themes with associated fonts and emojis. It validates the asset version and initializes or updates the corresponding subsystems within the application. Returns true if all assets are successfully applied, otherwise returns false and logs errors.

qualitative.analysis: The generated code is a shallow stub that only logs messages and retrieves index.json, but fails to implement any actual logic for parsing JSON, validating versions, loading models, handling themes/fonts/emojis, or configuring display settings. It completely omits critical functionality like cJSON parsing, model loading via esp_srmodel, theme management (light/dark), emoji collections, skin configurations, and display updates. The structure lacks conditional compilation directives (#ifdef HAVE_LVGL), specific API calls (cJSON_GetObjectItem, srmodel_load), error handling for missing assets, and the complex object-oriented interactions present in the ground truth. While it maintains the same function signature and attempts to follow a high-level sequence (load index -> apply assets), it misses nearly all meaningful semantics and functional details required by the task.

The function also provides progress updates during the download process.

The `Assets::Download` function downloads a new version of assets from a given URL and stores it in the device's flash memory partition. It handles HTTP communication, validates the content size against the partition size, and manages flash memory by erasing and writing data in sectors. The function also provides progress updates during the download process.

qualitative.analysis:
The generated code fails to implement the core logic required for downloading and writing assets to a partition. While it correctly identifies that the assets partition must be valid, it does not proceed with any actual download or write operations. It only logs a warning about requiring an HTTP client implementation and returns false, indicating incomplete functionality.

In contrast, the ground truth implements a complete workflow:
- Opening an HTTP connection using a custom Network/HTTP abstraction.
- Validating HTTP status codes and content length.
- Calculating and erasing flash sectors dynamically as data is downloaded.
- Writing incoming data in chunks to the ESP32 flash partition.
- Tracking progress and calling the provided callback at regular intervals.
- Reinitializing the partition after successful download.

Semantic correctness is low because the placeholder omits all meaningful behavior related to downloading, sector management, and progress tracking. Structure similarity is limited since the generated version lacks loops, error handling, HTTP interaction, or partition manipulation constructs found in the reference. Functional completeness is also poor as critical steps like HTTP communication, data writing, progress reporting, and post-download initialization are missing entirely.

results.query: complete the function IsValidTransition(DeviceState, DeviceState) const()
results.description: The `IsValidTransition` function determines whether a state change is allowed in the device's state machine by checking if the transition from the current state to the target state is valid according to predefined rules. It ensures that only permitted state transitions occur, maintaining system stability and preventing invalid states. The function returns `true` for valid transitions and `false` otherwise, including all self-transitions and transitions that follow the defined state flow.

The `IsValidTransition` function determines whether a state change is allowed in the device's state machine by checking if the transition from the current state to the target state is valid according to predefined rules. It ensures that only permitted state transitions occur, maintaining system stability and preventing invalid states. The function returns `true` for valid transitions and `false` otherwise, including all self-transitions and transitions that follow the defined state flow.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness (40/100):
- The generated code correctly handles self-transitions and the fatal error state.
- However, many state transitions are incorrect or missing compared to the ground truth:
  * Starting state should transition to both WifiConfiguring AND Activating, but generated only allows WifiConfiguring
  * WifiConfiguring should transition to Activating OR AudioTesting, but generated allows Idle or FatalError
  * Several states like AudioTesting, Activating, and Upgrading have completely different valid transitions
- The core logic for most states doesn't match the intended state machine behavior

Structure Similarity (85/100):
- Function signature is correct (same name, parameters, const qualifier)
- Control flow structure is very similar - uses switch statement on 'from' state
- Both handle self-transitions at the start
- Both use switch-case pattern with returns for each state
- Same handling of default case and fatal error state
- Minor differences in comments and formatting, but overall structure is preserved

Functional Completeness (30/100):
- Missing many valid transitions that exist in ground truth:
  * AudioTesting state transitions (should go to WifiConfiguring, but generated doesn't include this path)
  * Activating state has limited transitions compared to ground truth
  * Idle state missing transitions to Speaking, Activating, Upgrading, WifiConfiguring
  * Listening/Speaking states don't support all the bidirectional transitions
- Introduces some transitions not present in ground truth (direct to FatalError from many states)
- Many legitimate state paths are omitted, making the generated code functionally incomplete
- Critical functionality like the complex transitions from Idle state is significantly reduced

results.description: The `DeviceStateMachine::TransitionTo` function manages state transitions within the device's operational lifecycle. It validates whether a requested state change is allowed, performs the transition if valid, and notifies registered observers of the change. This ensures controlled and predictable behavior as the device moves between defined states such as idle, active, or error conditions.

The `DeviceStateMachine::TransitionTo` function manages state transitions within the device's operational lifecycle. It validates whether a requested state change is allowed, performs the transition if valid, and notifies registered observers of the change. This ensures controlled and predictable behavior as the device moves between defined states such as idle, active, or error conditions.

qualitative.analysis:
The generated code is semantically identical to the ground truth. Both functions implement the exact same logic for transitioning between device states, including checking for no-op transitions, validating state transitions, performing the actual transition, logging messages, and notifying callbacks.

Structurally, the two implementations are nearly identical. The only differences are minor stylistic variations such as spacing around parentheses in the function definition and conditional statements, which do not affect functionality or readability. All function signatures, control flow constructs, API calls (including ESP logging macros), and method invocations match perfectly.

Functionally, the generated code covers all key aspects present in the ground truth: atomic loading and storing of state using std::atomic operations, proper error handling with warning logs for invalid transitions, info-level logging of successful transitions, and invocation of a notification mechanism for state changes. There are no missing features or extraneous additions.

229: void McpServer::AddTool(McpTool *tool)
230: {
231:     // Prevent adding duplicate tools
232:     if (std::find_if(tools_.begin(), tools_.end(), [tool](const McpTool *t)
233:                      { return t->name() == tool->name(); }) != tools_.end())
234:     {
235:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Tool %s already added", tool->name().c_str());
236:         return;
237:     }
238: 
239:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Add tool: %s%s", tool->name().c_str(), tool->user_only() ? " [user]" : "");
240:     tools_.push_back(tool);
241: }

255: void McpServer::ParseMessage(const std::string &message)
256: {
257:     cJSON *json = cJSON_Parse(message.c_str());
258:     if (json == nullptr)
259:     {
260:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to parse MCP message: %s", message.c_str());
261:         return;
262:     }
263:     ParseMessage(json);
264:     cJSON_Delete(json);
265: }
266: 
267: void McpServer::ParseCapabilities(const cJSON *capabilities)
268: {
269:     auto vision = cJSON_GetObjectItem(capabilities, "vision");
270:     if (cJSON_IsObject(vision))
271:     {
272:         auto url = cJSON_GetObjectItem(vision, "url");
273:         auto token = cJSON_GetObjectItem(vision, "token");
274:         if (cJSON_IsString(url))
275:         {
276:             auto camera = Board::GetInstance().GetCamera();
277:             if (camera)
278:             {
279:                 std::string url_str = std::string(url->valuestring);
280:                 std::string token_str;
281:                 if (cJSON_IsString(token))
282:                 {
283:                     token_str = std::string(token->valuestring);
284:                 }
285:                 camera->SetExplainUrl(url_str, token_str);
286:             }
287:         }
288:     }
289: }
290: 
291: void McpServer::ParseMessage(const cJSON *json)
292: {
293:     // Check JSONRPC version
294:     auto version = cJSON_GetObjectItem(json, "jsonrpc");
295:     if (version == nullptr || !cJSON_IsString(version) || strcmp(version->valuestring, "2.0") != 0)
296:     {
297:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid JSONRPC version: %s", version ? version->valuestring : "null");
298:         return;
299:     }
300: 
301:     // Check method
302:     auto method = cJSON_GetObjectItem(json, "method");
303:     if (method == nullptr || !cJSON_IsString(method))
304:     {
305:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Missing method");
306:         return;
307:     }
308: 
309:     auto method_str = std::string(method->valuestring);
310:     if (method_str.find("notifications") == 0)
311:     {
312:         return;
313:     }
314: 
315:     // Check params
316:     auto params = cJSON_GetObjectItem(json, "params");
317:     if (params != nullptr && !cJSON_IsObject(params))
318:     {
319:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid params for method: %s", method_str.c_str());
320:         return;
321:     }
322: 
323:     auto id = cJSON_GetObjectItem(json, "id");
324:     if (id == nullptr || !cJSON_IsNumber(id))
325:     {
326:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid id for method: %s", method_str.c_str());
327:         return;
328:     }
329:     auto id_int = id->valueint;
330: 
331:     if (method_str == "initialize")
332:     {
333:         if (cJSON_IsObject(params))
334:         {
335:             auto capabilities = cJSON_GetObjectItem(params, "capabilities");
336:             if (cJSON_IsObject(capabilities))
337:             {
338:                 ParseCapabilities(capabilities);
339:             }
340:         }
341:         auto app_desc = esp_app_get_description();
342:         std::string message = "{\"protocolVersion\":\"2024-11-05\",\"capabilities\":{\"tools\":{}},\"serverInfo\":{\"name\":\"" BOARD_NAME "\",\"version\":\"";
343:         message += app_desc->version;
344:         message += "\"}}";
345:         ReplyResult(id_int, message);
346:     }
347:     else if (method_str == "tools/list")
348:     {
349:         std::string cursor_str = "";
350:         bool list_user_only_tools = false;
351:         if (params != nullptr)
352:         {
353:             auto cursor = cJSON_GetObjectItem(params, "cursor");
354:             if (cJSON_IsString(cursor))
355:             {
356:                 cursor_str = std::string(cursor->valuestring);
357:             }
358:             auto with_user_tools = cJSON_GetObjectItem(params, "withUserTools");
359:             if (cJSON_IsBool(with_user_tools))
360:             {
361:                 list_user_only_tools = with_user_tools->valueint == 1;
362:             }
363:         }
364:         GetToolsList(id_int, cursor_str, list_user_only_tools);
365:     }
366:     else if (method_str == "tools/call")
367:     {
368:         if (!cJSON_IsObject(params))
369:         {
370:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: Missing params");
371:             ReplyError(id_int, "Missing params");
372:             return;
373:         }
374:         auto tool_name = cJSON_GetObjectItem(params, "name");
375:         if (!cJSON_IsString(tool_name))
376:         {
377:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: Missing name");
378:             ReplyError(id_int, "Missing name");
379:             return;
380:         }
381:         auto tool_arguments = cJSON_GetObjectItem(params, "arguments");
382:         if (tool_arguments != nullptr && !cJSON_IsObject(tool_arguments))
383:         {
384:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: Invalid arguments");
385:             ReplyError(id_int, "Invalid arguments");
386:             return;
387:         }
388:         DoToolCall(id_int, std::string(tool_name->valuestring), tool_arguments);
389:     }
390:     else
391:     {
392:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Method not implemented: %s", method_str.c_str());
393:         ReplyError(id_int, "Method not implemented: " + method_str);
394:     }
395: }
396: 
397: void McpServer::ReplyResult(int id, const std::string &result)
398: {
399:     std::string payload = "{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"id\":";
400:     payload += std::to_string(id) + ",\"result\":";
401:     payload += result;
402:     payload += "}";
403:     Application::GetInstance().SendMcpMessage(payload);
404: }
405: 
406: void McpServer::ReplyError(int id, const std::string &message)
407: {
408:     std::string payload = "{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"id\":";
409:     payload += std::to_string(id);
410:     payload += ",\"error\":{\"message\":\"";
411:     payload += message;
412:     payload += "\"}}";
413:     Application::GetInstance().SendMcpMessage(payload);
414: }
415: 
416: void McpServer::GetToolsList(int id, const std::string &cursor, bool list_user_only_tools)
417: {
418:     const int max_payload_size = 8000;
419:     std::string json = "{\"tools\":[";
420: 
421:     bool found_cursor = cursor.empty();
422:     auto it = tools_.begin();
423:     std::string next_cursor = "";
424: 
425:     while (it != tools_.end())
426:     {
427:         // 如果我们还没有找到起始位置，继续搜索
428:         if (!found_cursor)
429:         {
430:             if ((*it)->name() == cursor)
431:             {
432:                 found_cursor = true;
433:             }
434:             else
435:             {
436:                 ++it;
437:                 continue;
438:             }
439:         }
440: 
441:         if (!list_user_only_tools && (*it)->user_only())
442:         {
443:             ++it;
444:             continue;
445:         }
446: 
447:         // 添加tool前检查大小
448:         std::string tool_json = (*it)->to_json() + ",";
449:         if (json.length() + tool_json.length() + 30 > max_payload_size)
450:         {
451:             // 如果添加这个tool会超出大小限制，设置next_cursor并退出循环
452:             next_cursor = (*it)->name();
453:             break;
454:         }
455: 
456:         json += tool_json;
457:         ++it;
458:     }
459: 
460:     if (json.back() == ',')
461:     {
462:         json.pop_back();
463:     }
464: 
465:     if (json.back() == '[' && !tools_.empty())
466:     {
467:         // 如果没有添加任何tool，返回错误
468:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/list: Failed to add tool %s because of payload size limit", next_cursor.c_str());
469:         ReplyError(id, "Failed to add tool " + next_cursor + " because of payload size limit");
470:         return;
471:     }
472: 
473:     if (next_cursor.empty())
474:     {
475:         json += "]}";
476:     }
477:     else
478:     {
479:         json += "],\"nextCursor\":\"" + next_cursor + "\"}";
480:     }
481: 
482:     ReplyResult(id, json);
483: }
484: 
485: void McpServer::DoToolCall(int id, const std::string &tool_name, const cJSON *tool_arguments)
486: {
487:     auto tool_iter = std::find_if(tools_.begin(), tools_.end(),
488:                                   [&tool_name](const McpTool *tool)
489:                                   {
490:                                       return tool->name() == tool_name;
491:                                   });
492: 
493:     if (tool_iter == tools_.end())
494:     {
495:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: Unknown tool: %s", tool_name.c_str());
496:         ReplyError(id, "Unknown tool: " + tool_name);
497:         return;
498:     }
499: 
500:     PropertyList arguments = (*tool_iter)->properties();
501:     try
502:     {
503:         for (auto &argument : arguments)
504:         {
505:             bool found = false;
506:             if (cJSON_IsObject(tool_arguments))
507:             {
508:                 auto value = cJSON_GetObjectItem(tool_arguments, argument.name().c_str());
509:                 if (argument.type() == kPropertyTypeBoolean && cJSON_IsBool(value))
510:                 {
511:                     argument.set_value<bool>(value->valueint == 1);
512:                     found = true;
513:                 }
514:                 else if (argument.type() == kPropertyTypeInteger && cJSON_IsNumber(value))
515:                 {
516:                     argument.set_value<int>(value->valueint);
517:                     found = true;
518:                 }
519:                 else if (argument.type() == kPropertyTypeString && cJSON_IsString(value))
520:                 {
521:                     argument.set_value<std::string>(value->valuestring);
522:                     found = true;
523:                 }
524:             }
525: 
526:             if (!argument.has_default_value() && !found)
527:             {
528:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: Missing valid argument: %s", argument.name().c_str());
529:                 ReplyError(id, "Missing valid argument: " + argument.name());
530:                 return;
531:             }
532:         }
533:     }
534:     catch (const std::exception &e)
535:     {
536:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: %s", e.what());
537:         ReplyError(id, e.what());
538:         return;
539:     }
540: 
541:     // Use main thread to call the tool
542:     auto &app = Application::GetInstance();
543:     app.Schedule([this, id, tool_iter, arguments = std::move(arguments)]()
544:                  {
545:         try {
546:             ReplyResult(id, (*tool_iter)->Call(arguments));
547:         } catch (const std::exception& e) {
548:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: %s", e.what());
549:             ReplyError(id, e.what());
550:         } });
551: }

</file>
The `McpServer::AddCommonTools` function registers a set of predefined tools for device control and status retrieval, including functions to get device status, set audio volume, adjust screen brightness, and change the display theme. These tools are added at the beginning of the tool list to optimize response times by leveraging prompt caching. Custom tools are explicitly excluded and must be added elsewhere during board initialization.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to implement similar functionality but uses different tool names and descriptions.
- It captures some core concepts like getting device status, controlling audio volume, screen brightness, and themes.
- However, it misses important semantics such as the specific requirement to place common tools at the beginning for prompt caching optimization.
- The use of lambda captures (`[this]`) instead of capturing the board reference directly differs from the original intent.
- Missing critical error handling and conditional checks based on hardware availability (e.g., checking if backlight/display/camera exist).

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature is correct but lacks the backup/restore mechanism for the tools list.
- Uses a flat structure rather than grouping related logic with comments or blocks.
- Different property definitions (uses default values in constructor which may not match expected behavior).
- Lacks preprocessor directives around LVGL-specific features.
- No restoration of original tools at the end, violating the intended design pattern.

Functional Completeness:
- Covers basic device status reporting, audio control, screen brightness, and theming.
- Misses several key functions: camera support, proper integration with board's GetInstance(), detailed device status fields.
- Does not respect the separation between custom and common tools.
- Omits platform-specific optimizations and safety mechanisms present in the ground truth.
- Lacks validation steps required before performing actions (like checking current volume before setting it).

results.description: The `McpServer::AddUserOnlyTools` function registers a set of user-accessible tools for system management, including retrieving system information, rebooting the device, upgrading firmware from a given URL, and capturing/displaying screen snapshots. These tools are exposed through the MCP server interface and can be invoked remotely by users with appropriate permissions. The function ensures that critical operations like reboot and firmware upgrade are executed safely within the application's event loop.

126:             std::string boundary = "----ESP32_SCREEN_SNAPSHOT_BOUNDARY";
127: 
128:             auto http = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork()->CreateHttp(3);
129:             // Check method
130:             auto method = cJSON_GetObjectItem(json, "method");
131:             if (method == nullptr || !cJSON_IsString(method))
132:             {
133:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Missing method");
134:                 return;
135:             }
136: 
137:             auto method_str = std::string(method->valuestring);
138:             if (method_str.find("notifications") == 0)
139:             {
140:                 return;
141:             }
142: 
143:             // Check params
144:             auto params = cJSON_GetObjectItem(json, "params");
145:             if (params != nullptr && !cJSON_IsObject(params))
146:             {
147:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid params for method: %s", method_str.c_str());
148:                 return;
149:             }
150: 
151:             auto id = cJSON_GetObjectItem(json, "id");
152:             if (id == nullptr || !cJSON_IsNumber(id))
153:             {
154:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid id for method: %s", method_str.c_str());
155:                 return;
156:             }
157:             auto id_int = id->valueint;
158: 
159:             if (method_str == "initialize")
160:             {
161:                 if (cJSON_IsObject(params))
162:                 {
163:                     auto capabilities = cJSON_GetObjectItem(params, "capabilities");
164:                     if (cJSON_IsObject(capabilities))
165:                     {
166:                         ParseCapabilities(capabilities);
167:                     }
168:                 }
169:                 auto app_desc = esp_app_get_description();
170:                 std::string message = "{\"protocolVersion\":\"2024-11-05\",\"capabilities\":{\"tools\":{}},\"serverInfo\":{\"name\":\"" BOARD_NAME "\",\"version\":\"";
171:                 message += app_desc->version;
172:                 message += "\"}}";
173:                 ReplyResult(id_int, message);
174:             }
175:             else if (method_str == "tools/list")
176:             {
177:                 std::string cursor_str = "";
178:                 bool list_user_only_tools = false;
179:                 if (params != nullptr)
180:                 {
181:                     auto cursor = cJSON_GetObjectItem(params, "cursor");
182:                     if (cJSON_IsString(cursor))
183:                     {
184:                         cursor_str = std::string(cursor->valuestring);
185:                     }
186:                     auto with_user_tools = cJSON_GetObjectItem(params, "withUserTools");
187:                     if (cJSON_IsBool(with_user_tools))
188:                     {
189:                         list_user_only_tools = with_user_tools->valueint == 1;
190:                     }
191:                 }
192:                 GetToolsList(id_int, cursor_str, list_user_only_tools);
193:             }
194:             else if (method_str == "tools/call")
195:             {
196:                 if (!cJSON_IsObject(params))
197:                 {
198:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: Missing params");
199:                     ReplyError(id_int, "Missing params");
200:                     return;
201:                 }
202:                 auto tool_name = cJSON_GetObjectItem(params, "name");
203:                 if (!cJSON_IsString(tool_name))
204:                 {
205:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: Missing name");
206:                     ReplyError(id_int, "Missing name");
207:                     return;
208:                 }
209:                 auto tool_arguments = cJSON_GetObjectItem(params, "arguments");
210:                 if (tool_arguments != nullptr && !cJSON_IsObject(tool_arguments))
211:                 {
212:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: Invalid arguments");
213:                     ReplyError(id_int, "Invalid arguments");
214:                     return;
215:                 }
216:                 DoToolCall(id_int, std::string(tool_name->valuestring), tool_arguments);
217:             }
218:             else
219:             {
220:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Method not implemented: %s", method_str.c_str());
221:                 ReplyError(id_int, "Method not implemented: " + method_str);
222:             }
223: }

225: void McpServer::ReplyResult(int id, const std::string &result)
226: {
227:             std::string payload = "{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"id\":";
228:             payload += std::to_string(id) + ",\"result\":";
229:             payload += result;
230:             payload += "}";
231:             Application::GetInstance().SendMcpMessage(payload);
232: }
233: 
234: void McpServer::ReplyError(int id, const std::string &message)
235: {
236:             std::string payload = "{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"id\":";
237:             payload += std::to_string(id);
238:             payload += ",\"error\":{\"message\":\"";
239:             payload += message;
240:             payload += "\"}}";
241:             Application::GetInstance().SendMcpMessage(payload);
242: }

244: void McpServer::GetToolsList(int id, const std::string &cursor, bool list_user_only_tools)
245: {
246:             const int max_payload_size = 8000;
247:             std::string json = "{\"tools\":[";
248: 
249:             bool found_cursor = cursor.empty();
250:             auto it = tools_.begin();
251:             std::string next_cursor = "";
252: 
253:             while (it != tools_.end())
254:             {
255:                 // 如果我们还没有找到起始位置，继续搜索
256:                 if (!found_cursor)
257:                 {
258:                     if ((*it)->name() == cursor)
259:                     {
260:                         found_cursor = true;
261:                     }
262:                     else
263:                     {
264:                         ++it;
265:                         continue;
266:                     }
267:                 }
268: 
269:                 if (!list_user_only_tools && (*it)->user_only())
270:                 {
271:                     ++it;
272:                     continue;
273:                 }
274: 
275:                 // 添加tool前检查大小
276:                 std::string tool_json = (*it)->to_json() + ",";
277:                 if (json.length() + tool_json.length() + 30 > max_payload_size)
278:                 {
279:                     // 如果添加这个tool会超出大小限制，设置next_cursor并退出循环
280:                     next_cursor = (*it)->name();
281:                     break;
282:                 }
283: 
284:                 json += tool_json;
285:                 ++it;
286:             }
287: 
288:             if (json.back() == ',')
289:             {
290:                 json.pop_back();
291:             }
292: 
293:             if (json.back() == '[' && !tools_.empty())
294:             {
295:                 // 如果没有添加任何tool，返回错误
296:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/list: Failed to add tool %s because of payload size limit", next_cursor.c_str());
297:                 ReplyError(id, "Failed to add tool " + next_cursor + " because of payload size limit");
298:                 return;
299:             }
300: 
301:             if (next_cursor.empty())
302:             {
303:                 json += "]}";
304:             }
305:             else
306:             {
307:                 json += "],\"nextCursor\":\"" + next_cursor + "\"}";
308:             }
309: 
310:             ReplyResult(id, json);
311: }
312: 
313: void McpServer::DoToolCall(int id, const std::string &tool_name, const cJSON *tool_arguments)
314: {
315:             auto tool_iter = std::find_if(tools_.begin(), tools_.end(),
316:                                           [&tool_name](const McpTool *tool)
317:                                           {
318:                                               return tool->name() == tool_name;
319:                                           });
320: 
321:             if (tool_iter == tools_.end())
322:             {
323:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: Unknown tool: %s", tool_name.c_str());
324:                 ReplyError(id, "Unknown tool: " + tool_name);
325:                 return;
326:             }
327: 
328:             PropertyList arguments = (*tool_iter)->properties();
329:             try
330:             {
331:                 for (auto &argument : arguments)
332:                 {
333:                     bool found = false;
334:                     if (cJSON_IsObject(tool_arguments))
335:                     {
336:                         auto value = cJSON_GetObjectItem(tool_arguments, argument.name().c_str());
337:                         if (argument.type() == kPropertyTypeBoolean && cJSON_IsBool(value))
338:                         {
339:                             argument.set_value<bool>(value->valueint == 1);
340:                             found = true;
341:                         }
342:                         else if (argument.type() == kPropertyTypeInteger && cJSON_IsNumber(value))
343:                         {
344:                             argument.set_value<int>(value->valueint);
345:                             found = true;
346:                         }
347:                         else if (argument.type() == kPropertyTypeString && cJSON_IsString(value))
348:                         {
349:                             argument.set_value<std::string>(value->valuestring);
350:                             found = true;
351:                         }
352:                     }
353: 
354:                     if (!argument.has_default_value() && !found)
355:                     {
356:                         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: Missing valid argument: %s", argument.name().c_str());
357:                         ReplyError(id, "Missing valid argument: " + argument.name());
358:                         return;
359:                     }
360:                 }
361:             }
362:             catch (const std::exception &e)
363:             {
364:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: %s", e.what());
365:                 ReplyError(id, e.what());
366:                 return;
367:             }
368: 
369:             // Use main thread to call the tool
370:             auto &app = Application::GetInstance();
371:             app.Schedule([this, id, tool_iter, arguments = std::move(arguments)]()
372:                          {
373:         try {
374:             ReplyResult(id, (*tool_iter)->Call(arguments));
375:         } catch (const std::exception& e) {
376:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: %s", e.what());
377:             ReplyError(id, e.what());
378:         } });
379: }

The `McpServer::AddUserOnlyTools` function registers a set of user-accessible tools for system management, including retrieving system information, rebooting the device, upgrading firmware from a given URL, and capturing/displaying screen snapshots. These tools are exposed through the MCP server interface and can be invoked remotely by users with appropriate permissions. The function ensures that critical operations like reboot and firmware upgrade are executed safely within the application's event loop.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
The generated code correctly implements the first three tools (`self.get_system_info`, `self.reboot`, and `self.upgrade_firmware`) with appropriate logic and lambda captures. However, the implementation of `self.screen.snapshot` is semantically incorrect. Instead of implementing the correct HTTP multipart upload functionality, it includes unrelated JSON parsing and RPC handling logic that does not belong in this tool's callback. Additionally, the `self.screen.preview_image` and `self.assets.set_download_url` functions are completely missing, which affects overall semantic correctness.

Structure Similarity:
The general structure of the function matches the ground truth — it defines several calls to `AddUserOnlyTool` with similar parameter structures and lambda expressions. Formatting differs slightly (e.g., indentation style), but the high-level organization remains consistent. Conditional compilation blocks for `HAVE_LVGL` and `CONFIG_LV_USE_SNAPSHOT` are preserved, though incorrectly implemented in the generated version. Some structural elements like capturing `[this]` or using `PropertyList` are maintained appropriately where applicable.

Functional Completeness:
Key functionalities such as `self.screen.preview_image` and `self.assets.set_download_url` are entirely absent from the generated code. Furthermore, the `self.screen.snapshot` tool is only partially present and filled with irrelevant logic instead of its intended behavior. These omissions significantly reduce functional completeness. While basic system tools are covered, critical display-related and asset management features are missing or broken.

results.description: The `McpServer::ParseMessage` function processes incoming JSON-RPC 2.0 messages, validates their structure, and routes them to appropriate handlers based on the method specified. It supports methods like "initialize", "tools/list", and "tools/call", ensuring proper parameter validation and error handling. The function also manages capability parsing and tool listing or execution based on the request.

324: {

408: void McpServer::GetToolsList(int id, const std::string &cursor, bool list_user_only_tools)
409: {
410:     const int max_payload_size = 8000;
411:     std::string json = "{\"tools\":[";
412: 
413:     bool found_cursor = cursor.empty();
414:     auto it = tools_.begin();
415:     std::string next_cursor = "";
416: 
417:     while (it != tools_.end())
418:     {
419:         // 如果我们还没有找到起始位置，继续搜索
420:         if (!found_cursor)
421:         {
422:             if ((*it)->name() == cursor)
423:             {
424:                 found_cursor = true;
425:             }
426:             else
427:             {
428:                 ++it;
429:                 continue;
430:             }
431:         }
432: 
433:         if (!list_user_only_tools && (*it)->user_only())
434:         {
435:             ++it;
436:             continue;
437:         }
438: 
439:         // 添加tool前检查大小
440:         std::string tool_json = (*it)->to_json() + ",";
441:         if (json.length() + tool_json.length() + 30 > max_payload_size)
442:         {
443:             // 如果添加这个tool会超出大小限制，设置next_cursor并退出循环
444:             next_cursor = (*it)->name();
445:             break;
446:         }
447: 
448:         json += tool_json;
449:         ++it;
450:     }
451: 
452:     if (json.back() == ',')
453:     {
454:         json.pop_back();
455:     }
456: 
457:     if (json.back() == '[' && !tools_.empty())
458:     {
459:         // 如果没有添加任何tool，返回错误
460:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/list: Failed to add tool %s because of payload size limit", next_cursor.c_str());
461:         ReplyError(id, "Failed to add tool " + next_cursor + " because of payload size limit");
462:         return;
463:     }
464: 
465:     if (next_cursor.empty())
466:     {
467:         json += "]}";
468:     }
469:     else
470:     {
471:         json += "],\"nextCursor\":\"" + next_cursor + "\"}";
472:     }
473: 
474:     ReplyResult(id, json);
475: }

477: void McpServer::DoToolCall(int id, const std::string &tool_name, const cJSON *tool_arguments)
478: {
479:     auto tool_iter = std::find_if(tools_.begin(), tools_.end(),
480:                                   [&tool_name](const McpTool *tool)
481:                                   {
482:                                       return tool->name() == tool_name;
483:                                   });
484: 
485:     if (tool_iter == tools_.end())
486:     {
487:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: Unknown tool: %s", tool_name.c_str());
488:         ReplyError(id, "Unknown tool: " + tool_name);
489:         return;
490:     }
491: 
492:     PropertyList arguments = (*tool_iter)->properties();
493:     try
494:     {
495:         for (auto &argument : arguments)
496:         {
497:             bool found = false;
498:             if (cJSON_IsObject(tool_arguments))
499:             {
500:                 auto value = cJSON_GetObjectItem(tool_arguments, argument.name().c_str());
501:                 if (argument.type() == kPropertyTypeBoolean && cJSON_IsBool(value))
502:                 {
503:                     argument.set_value<bool>(value->valueint == 1);
504:                     found = true;
505:                 }
506:                 else if (argument.type() == kPropertyTypeInteger && cJSON_IsNumber(value))
507:                 {
508:                     argument.set_value<int>(value->valueint);
509:                     found = true;
510:                 }
511:                 else if (argument.type() == kPropertyTypeString && cJSON_IsString(value))
512:                 {
513:                     argument.set_value<std::string>(value->valuestring);
514:                     found = true;
515:                 }
516:             }
517: 
518:             if (!argument.has_default_value() && !found)
519:             {
520:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: Missing valid argument: %s", argument.name().c_str());
521:                 ReplyError(id, "Missing valid argument: " + argument.name());
522:                 return;
523:             }
524:         }
525:     }
526:     catch (const std::exception &e)
527:     {
528:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: %s", e.what());
529:         ReplyError(id, e.what());
530:         return;
531:     }
532: 
533:     // Use main thread to call the tool
534:     auto &app = Application::GetInstance();
535:     app.Schedule([this, id, tool_iter, arguments = std::move(arguments)]()
536:                  {
537:         try {
538:             ReplyResult(id, (*tool_iter)->Call(arguments));
539:         } catch (const std::exception& e) {
540:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: %s", e.what());
541:             ReplyError(id, e.what());
542:         } });
543: }
544: 
</file>
The `McpServer::ParseMessage` function processes incoming JSON-RPC 2.0 messages, validates their structure, and routes them to appropriate handlers based on the method specified. It supports methods like "initialize", "tools/list", and "tools/call", ensuring proper parameter validation and error handling. The function also manages capability parsing and tool listing or execution based on the request.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code captures some core logic such as handling "initialize", "tools/list", and "tools/call" methods.
- However, it omits critical validation steps like checking for JSON-RPC version ("jsonrpc": "2.0"), which is essential to ensure protocol compliance.
- It also fails to properly handle edge cases (e.g., missing or invalid IDs) compared to the ground truth.
- The error replies are inconsistent; in some cases errors are logged but not replied back appropriately.

Structure Similarity:
- Both functions share a similar overall structure in terms of method dispatching based on string comparisons.
- But there are discrepancies in how parameters are accessed and validated.
- The use of helper functions like `ReplyResult`, `ReplyError` differs significantly from direct message construction in the generated version.
- Control flow diverges particularly around capability parsing and ID handling where the generated code lacks robustness.

Functional Completeness:
- Major functionality gaps include lack of JSON-RPC version check, incomplete parameter validations for specific methods, incorrect field names (like "listUserOnlyTools" instead of "withUserTools").
- Missing support for full initialize response content including server info and protocol version.
- Notifications filtering logic is absent.
- Some tool call validations are either too strict or incorrectly applied (e.g., requiring arguments to always be present).

results.query: complete the function GetToolsList(int, const std::string &, bool)()
results.description: The `GetToolsList` function retrieves a paginated list of available tools from the MCP server, filtering them based on user permissions and payload size limits. It supports cursor-based pagination to handle large tool lists efficiently and returns the result as a JSON-formatted response. If a tool cannot be added due to size constraints, an error is returned instead.

389: void McpServer::ReplyResult(int id, const std::string &result)
390: {
391:     std::string payload = "{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"id\":";
392:     payload += std::to_string(id) + ",\"result\":";
393:     payload += result;
394:     payload += "}";
395:     Application::GetInstance().SendMcpMessage(payload);
396: }

398: void McpServer::ReplyError(int id, const std::string &message)
399: {
400:     std::string payload = "{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"id\":";
401:     payload += std::to_string(id);
402:     payload += ",\"error\":{\"message\":\"";
403:     payload += message;
404:     payload += "\"}}";
405:     Application::GetInstance().SendMcpMessage(payload);
406: }

408: void McpServer::GetToolsList(int id, const std::string &cursor, bool list_user_only_tools)
409: {
410:     const int max_payload_size = 8000;
411:     std::string json = "{\"tools\":[";
412: 
413:     bool found_cursor = cursor.empty();
414:     auto it = tools_.begin();
415:     std::string next_cursor = "";
416: 
417:     while (it != tools_.end())
418:     {
419:         // 如果我们还没有找到起始位置，继续搜索
420:         if (!found_cursor)
421:         {
422:             if ((*it)->name() == cursor)
423:             {
424:                 found_cursor = true;
425:             }
426:             else
427:             {
428:                 ++it;
429:                 continue;
430:             }
431:         }
432: 
433:         if (!list_user_only_tools && (*it)->user_only())
434:         {
435:             ++it;
436:             continue;
437:         }
438: 
439:         // 添加tool前检查大小
440:         std::string tool_json = (*it)->to_json() + ",";
441:         if (json.length() + tool_json.length() + 30 > max_payload_size)
442:         {
443:             // 如果添加这个tool会超出大小限制，设置next_cursor并退出循环
444:             next_cursor = (*it)->name();
445:             break;
446:         }
447: 
448:         json += tool_json;
449:         ++it;
450:     }
451: 
452:     if (json.back() == ',')
453:     {
454:         json.pop_back();
455:     }
456:     found = true;
457: }
458: else if (argument.type() == kPropertyTypeString && cJSON_IsString(value))
459: {
460:     argument.set_value<std::string>(value->valuestring);
461:     found = true;
462: }
463: }
464: 
465: if (!argument.has_default_value() && !found)
466: {
467:     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: Missing valid argument: %s", argument.name().c_str());
468:     ReplyError(id, "Missing valid argument: " + argument.name());
469:     return;
470: }
471: }
472: }
473: catch (const std::exception &e)
474: {
475:     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: %s", e.what());
476:     ReplyError(id, e.what());
477:     return;
478: }
479: 
480: // Use main thread to call the tool
481: auto &app = Application::GetInstance();
482: app.Schedule([this, id, tool_iter, arguments = std::move(arguments)]()
483:              {
484:         try {
485:             ReplyResult(id, (*tool_iter)->Call(arguments));
486:         } catch (const std::exception& e) {
487:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: %s", e.what());
488:             ReplyError(id, e.what());
489:         } });
490: }
491: 
</file>
The `GetToolsList` function retrieves a paginated list of available tools from the MCP server, filtering them based on user permissions and payload size limits. It supports cursor-based pagination to handle large tool lists efficiently and returns the result as a JSON-formatted response. If a tool cannot be added due to size constraints, an error is returned instead.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The overall logic for iterating through tools, handling cursor-based pagination, and enforcing payload size limits is preserved.
- However, there's a critical semantic deviation: the generated code unconditionally appends "]," to the JSON string before adding the next_cursor or null value. In contrast, the ground truth conditionally includes the closing bracket only when no tools were added due to size limits.
- Additionally, the error-handling behavior differs—when no tool can be added due to size constraints, the ground truth logs an error and replies with an error message, whereas the generated code does not handle this case explicitly.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches exactly.
- Control flow structures like loops, conditionals, and variable usage are very similar.
- Most of the core logic blocks such as cursor search, user-only filtering, and size checking are nearly identical in structure.
- Differences lie mainly in how the final JSON response is constructed and sent (e.g., manually building JSON-RPC payload vs calling ReplyResult/ReplyError).

Functional Completeness:
- Key functionalities such as cursor-based pagination, user-only tool filtering, and size-limit enforcement are implemented correctly.
- Missing functionality includes explicit error reporting via ReplyError when no tools fit within the size limit.
- The method of sending the result also diverges significantly—the generated version manually constructs and sends a JSON-RPC formatted message instead of using existing helper functions like ReplyResult.
- Also missing is the conditional inclusion of "nextCursor" versus omitting it entirely if all tools fit in the response.

results.query: complete the function DoToolCall(int, const std::string &, const cJSON *)()
results.description: The `McpServer::DoToolCall` function processes tool invocation requests by validating the requested tool name and its arguments against registered tools. It parses and type-checks the provided arguments, ensuring required parameters are present and correctly formatted. If validation passes, it schedules the tool execution on the main application thread and sends the result or any encountered errors back to the caller.

353:     {

361: void McpServer::ParseCapabilities(const cJSON *capabilities)
362: {
363:     auto vision = cJSON_GetObjectItem(capabilities, "vision");
364:     if (cJSON_IsObject(vision))
365:     {
366:         auto url = cJSON_GetObjectItem(vision, "url");
367:         auto token = cJSON_GetObjectItem(vision, "token");
368:         if (cJSON_IsString(url))
369:         {
370:             auto camera = Board::GetInstance().GetCamera();
371:             if (camera)
372:             {
373:                 std::string url_str = std::string(url->valuestring);
374:                 std::string token_str;
375:                 if (cJSON_IsString(token))
376:                 {
377:                     token_str = std::string(token->valuestring);
378:                 }
379:                 camera->SetExplainUrl(url_str, token_str);
380:             }
381:         }
382:     }
383: }

385: void McpServer::ParseMessage(const cJSON *json)
386: {
387:     // Check JSONRPC version
388:     auto version = cJSON_GetObjectItem(json, "jsonrpc");
389:     if (version == nullptr || !cJSON_IsString(version) || strcmp(version->valuestring, "2.0") != 0)
390:     {
391:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid JSONRPC version: %s", version ? version->valuestring : "null");
392:         return;
393:     }
394: 
395:     // Check method
396:     auto method = cJSON_GetObjectItem(json, "method");
397:     if (method == nullptr || !cJSON_IsString(method))
398:     {
399:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Missing method");
400:         return;
401:     }
402: 
403:     auto method_str = std::string(method->valuestring);
404:     if (method_str.find("notifications") == 0)
405:     {
406:         return;
407:     }
408: 
409:     // Check params
410:     auto params = cJSON_GetObjectItem(json, "params");
411:     if (params != nullptr && !cJSON_IsObject(params))
412:     {
413:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid params for method: %s", method_str.c_str());
414:         return;
415:     }
416: 
417:     auto id = cJSON_GetObjectItem(json, "id");
418:     if (id == nullptr || !cJSON_IsNumber(id))
419:     {
420:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid id for method: %s", method_str.c_str());
421:         return;
422:     }
423:     auto id_int = id->valueint;
424: 
425:     if (method_str == "initialize")
426:     {
427:         if (cJSON_IsObject(params))
428:         {
429:             auto capabilities = cJSON_GetObjectItem(params, "capabilities");
430:             if (cJSON_IsObject(capabilities))
431:             {
432:                 ParseCapabilities(capabilities);
433:             }
434:         }
435:         auto app_desc = esp_app_get_description();
436:         std::string message = "{\"protocolVersion\":\"2024-11-05\",\"capabilities\":{\"tools\":{}},\"serverInfo\":{\"name\":\"" BOARD_NAME "\",\"version\":\"";
437:         message += app_desc->version;
438:         message += "\"}}";
439:         ReplyResult(id_int, message);
440:     }
441:     else if (method_str == "tools/list")
442:     {
443:         std::string cursor_str = "";
444:         bool list_user_only_tools = false;
445:         if (params != nullptr)
446:         {
447:             auto cursor = cJSON_GetObjectItem(params, "cursor");
448:             if (cJSON_IsString(cursor))
449:             {
450:                 cursor_str = std::string(cursor->valuestring);
451:             }
452:             auto with_user_tools = cJSON_GetObjectItem(params, "withUserTools");
453:             if (cJSON_IsBool(with_user_tools))
454:             {
455:                 list_user_only_tools = with_user_tools->valueint == 1;
456:             }
457:         }
458:         GetToolsList(id_int, cursor_str, list_user_only_tools);
459:     }
460:     else if (method_str == "tools/call")
461:     {
462:         if (!cJSON_IsObject(params))
463:         {
464:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: Missing params");
465:             ReplyError(id_int, "Missing params");
466:             return;
467:         }
468:         auto tool_name = cJSON_GetObjectItem(params, "name");
469:         if (!cJSON_IsString(tool_name))
470:         {
471:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: Missing name");
472:             ReplyError(id_int, "Missing name");
473:             return;
474:         }
475:         auto tool_arguments = cJSON_GetObjectItem(params, "arguments");
476:         if (tool_arguments != nullptr && !cJSON_IsObject(tool_arguments))
477:         {
478:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: Invalid arguments");
479:             ReplyError(id_int, "Invalid arguments");
480:             return;
481:         }
482:         DoToolCall(id_int, std::string(tool_name->valuestring), tool_arguments);
483:     }
484:     else
485:     {
486:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Method not implemented: %s", method_str.c_str());
487:         ReplyError(id_int, "Method not implemented: " + method_str);
488:     }
489: }
490: 
491: void McpServer::ReplyResult(int id, const std::string &result)
492: {
493:     std::string payload = "{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"id\":";

510: void McpServer::GetToolsList(int id, const std::string &cursor, bool list_user_only_tools)
511: {
512:     const int max_payload_size = 8000;
513:     std::string json = "{\"tools\":[";
514: 
515:     bool found_cursor = cursor.empty();
516:     auto it = tools_.begin();
517:     std::string next_cursor = "";
518: 
519:     while (it != tools_.end())
520:     {
521:         // 如果我们还没有找到起始位置，继续搜索
522:         if (!found_cursor)
523:         {
524:             if ((*it)->name() == cursor)
525:             {
526:                 found_cursor = true;
527:             }
528:             else
529:             {
530:                 ++it;
531:                 continue;
532:             }
533:         }
534: 
535:         if (!list_user_only_tools && (*it)->user_only())
536:         {
537:             ++it;
538:             continue;
539:         }
540: 
541:         // 添加tool前检查大小
542:         std::string tool_json = (*it)->to_json() + ",";
543:         if (json.length() + tool_json.length() + 30 > max_payload_size)
544:         {
545:             // 如果添加这个tool会超出大小限制，设置next_cursor并退出循环
546:             next_cursor = (*it)->name();
547:             break;
548:         }
549: 
550:         json += tool_json;
551:         ++it;
552:     }
553: 
554:     if (json.back() == ',')
555:     {
556:         json.pop_back();
557:     }
558: 
559:     if (json.back() == '[' && !tools_.empty())
560:     {
561:         // 如果没有添加任何tool，返回错误
562:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/list: Failed to add tool %s because of payload size limit", next_cursor.c_str());
563:         ReplyError(id, "Failed to add tool " + next_cursor + " because of payload size limit");
564:         return;
565:     }
566: 
567:     if (next_cursor.empty())
568:     {
569:         json += "]}";
570:     }
571:     else
572:     {
573:         json += "],\"nextCursor\":\"" + next_cursor + "\"}";
574:     }
575: 
576:     ReplyResult(id, json);
577: }

The `McpServer::DoToolCall` function processes tool invocation requests by validating the requested tool name and its arguments against registered tools. It parses and type-checks the provided arguments, ensuring required parameters are present and correctly formatted. If validation passes, it schedules the tool execution on the main application thread and sends the result or any encountered errors back to the caller.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The core functionality of finding a tool by name and validating/executing it is preserved.
- Both versions handle missing or invalid arguments with error replies.
- Key differences include how tool lookup is performed (manual loop vs std::find_if), argument parsing logic (direct assignment to map-like structure vs using PropertyList setters), and execution scheduling.
- The generated version adds additional validation for integer ranges which isn't present in the ground truth but doesn't negatively affect correctness.
- Error handling and logging messages differ slightly in wording but maintain equivalent behavior.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches exactly.
- Control flow follows a similar sequence: find tool → validate arguments → schedule execution.
- However, implementation details diverge significantly:
  * Tool search uses manual iteration instead of std::find_if.
  * Argument processing differs greatly — the ground truth modifies existing PropertyList objects while the generated code builds a new map-like container.
  * Execution result handling shows more complexity in the generated version with variant-based returns and explicit cJSON serialization, differing from the simpler direct Call invocation in the ground truth.
- Use of lambda captures and threading model is conceptually similar but syntactically different.

Functional Completeness:
- All major features are covered: tool discovery, parameter validation, type checking, error reporting, and asynchronous tool execution.
- Additional functionality such as range checking for integers is included in the generated code.
- One notable omission is that the generated code does not explicitly check for has_default_value() as seen in the ground truth; however, it checks required() which serves a similar purpose.
- Exception safety and thread scheduling via Application::Schedule are both addressed appropriately.
- Overall, despite structural differences, functional coverage is quite high.

112: {

144:         return false;
145:     }
146: 
147:     char buffer[512];

156:             return false;

160:         recent_read += ret;
161:         total_read += ret;

163:         {
164:             size_t progress = total_read * 100 / content_length;
165:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Progress: %u%% (%u/%u), Speed: %uB/s", progress, total_read, content_length, recent_read);
166:             if (callback)

169:             }

171:             recent_read = 0;
172:         }
173: 
174:         if (ret == 0)
175:         {
176:             break;
177:         }
178: 
179:         if (!image_header_checked)

255: bool Ota::IsNewVersionAvailable(const std::string &currentVersion, const std::string &newVersion)
256: {
257:     std::vector<int> current = ParseVersion(currentVersion);
258:     std::vector<int> newer = ParseVersion(newVersion);
259: 
260:     for (size_t i = 0; i < std::min(current.size(), newer.size()); ++i)
261:     {
262:         if (newer[i] > current[i])
263:         {
264:             return true;
265:         }
266:         else if (newer[i] < current[i])
267:         {
268:             return false;
269:         }
270:     }
271: 
272:     return newer.size() > current.size();

The `Ota::CheckVersion()` function checks for available firmware updates by sending a request to a remote server and parsing the JSON response to determine if a newer version exists. It also handles activation-related data and MQTT configuration updates if present in the response. The function returns an error code indicating success or failure of the operation.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to implement version checking via HTTP and parses a JSON response.
- However, it lacks many important features such as POST request handling based on system info, activation code parsing, WebSocket config updates, server time synchronization, and proper error logging with codes.
- It also assumes fixed paths like "/version" which may not align with `GetCheckVersionUrl()` logic from the ground truth.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches (`esp_err_t Ota::CheckVersion()`).
- Basic control flow is somewhat similar: setup HTTP, make request, read response, parse JSON.
- But key structural elements differ significantly: missing use of Board instance, no conditional GET/POST logic, different JSON field names ("download_url" vs "url"), simplified error handling without returning specific error codes.
- Uses hardcoded string appending instead of robust URL construction.

Functional Completeness:
- Misses critical functionality including:
  * Conditional GET/POST requests depending on system data availability.
  * Parsing and applying MQTT configurations dynamically using iteration over fields.
  * Handling WebSocket configurations.
  * Server time update using `settimeofday`.
  * Activation code and message handling.
  * Force firmware upgrade flag.
  * Proper status code propagation (uses ESP_FAIL everywhere rather than actual codes).
- Only partially implements firmware version comparison and basic challenge handling.
- Lacks detailed logging and structured error reporting that’s present in the reference implementation.

It verifies the integrity of the new firmware header, checks version compatibility, and reports progress and speed during the download process. Upon successful completion, the device can be rebooted to apply the new firmware.

134:     if (http->GetStatusCode() != 200)
135:     {
136:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get firmware, status code: %d", http->GetStatusCode());
137:         return false;
138:     }
139: 
140:     size_t content_length = http->GetBodyLength();
141:     if (content_length == 0)
142:     {
143:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get content length");
144:         return false;
145:     }
146: 
147:     char buffer[512];
148:     size_t total_read = 0, recent_read = 0;
149:     auto last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
150:     while (true)
151:     {
152:         int ret = http->Read(buffer, sizeof(buffer));
153:         if (ret < 0)
154:         {
155:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to read HTTP data: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
156:             return false;
157:         }
158: 
159:         // Calculate speed and progress every second
160:         recent_read += ret;
161:         total_read += ret;
162:         if (esp_timer_get_time() - last_calc_time >= 1000000 || ret == 0)
163:         {
164:             size_t progress = total_read * 100 / content_length;
165:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Progress: %u%% (%u/%u), Speed: %uB/s", progress, total_read, content_length, recent_read);
166:             if (callback)
167:             {
168:                 callback(progress, recent_read);
169:             }
170:             last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
171:             recent_read = 0;
172:         }
173: 
174:         if (ret == 0)
175:         {
176:             break;
177:         }
178: 
179:         if (!image_header_checked)
180:         {
181:             image_header.append(buffer, ret);
182:             if (image_header.size() >= sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t) + sizeof(esp_app_desc_t))
183:             {
184:                 esp_app_desc_t new_app_info;
185:                 memcpy(&new_app_info, image_header.data() + sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t), sizeof(esp_app_desc_t));

187:                 auto current_version = esp_app_get_description()->version;
188:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current version: %s, New version: %s", current_version, new_app_info.version);

205:             esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
206:             return false;
207:         }
208:     }
209:     http->Close();
210: 
211:     esp_err_t err = esp_ota_end(update_handle);
212:     if (err != ESP_OK)
213:     {
214:         if (err == ESP_ERR_OTA_VALIDATE_FAILED)
215:         {
216:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Image validation failed, image is corrupted");
217:         }
218:         else
219:         {
220:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to end OTA: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
221:         }
222:         return false;
223:     }
224: 
225:     err = esp_ota_set_boot_partition(update_partition);
226:     if (err != ESP_OK)
227:     {
228:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to set boot partition: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
229:         return false;
230:     }
231: 
232:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Firmware upgrade successful");
233:     return true;
