140: bool AudioService::ReadAudioData(std::vector<int16_t> &data, int sample_rate, int samples)
141: {
142:     if (!codec_->input_enabled())
143:     {
144:         esp_timer_stop(audio_power_timer_);
145:         esp_timer_start_periodic(audio_power_timer_, AUDIO_POWER_CHECK_INTERVAL_MS * 1000);
146:         codec_->EnableInput(true);
147:     }
148: 
149:     if (codec_->input_sample_rate() != sample_rate)
150:     {
151:         data.resize(samples * codec_->input_sample_rate() / sample_rate * codec_->input_channels());
152:         if (!codec_->InputData(data))
153:         {
154:             return false;
155:         }
156:         if (codec_->input_channels() == 2)
157:         {
158:             auto mic_channel = std::vector<int16_t>(data.size() / 2);
159:             auto reference_channel = std::vector<int16_t>(data.size() / 2);
160:             for (size_t i = 0, j = 0; i < mic_channel.size(); ++i, j += 2)
161:             {
162:                 mic_channel[i] = data[j];
163:                 reference_channel[i] = data[j + 1];
164:             }
165:             auto resampled_mic = std::vector<int16_t>(input_resampler_.GetOutputSamples(mic_channel.size()));
166:             auto resampled_reference = std::vector<int16_t>(reference_resampler_.GetOutputSamples(reference_channel.size()));
167:             input_resampler_.Process(mic_channel.data(), mic_channel.size(), resampled_mic.data());
168:             reference_resampler_.Process(reference_channel.data(), reference_channel.size(), resampled_reference.data());
169:             data.resize(resampled_mic.size() + resampled_reference.size());
170:             for (size_t i = 0, j = 0; i < resampled_mic.size(); ++i, j += 2)
171:             {
172:                 data[j] = resampled_mic[i];
173:                 data[j + 1] = resampled_reference[i];
174:             }
175:         }
176:         else
177:         {
178:             auto resampled = std::vector<int16_t>(input_resampler_.GetOutputSamples(data.size()));
179:             input_resampler_.Process(data.data(), data.size(), resampled.data());
180:             data = std::move(resampled);
181:         }
182:     }
183:     else
184:     {
185:         data.resize(samples * codec_->input_channels());
186:         if (!codec_->InputData(data))
187:         {
188:             return false;
189:         }
190:     }
191: 
192:     /* Update the last input time */
193:     last_input_time_ = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
194:     debug_statistics_.input_count++;
195: 
196: #if CONFIG_USE_AUDIO_DEBUGGER
197:     // 音频调试：发送原始音频数据
198:     if (audio_debugger_ == nullptr)
199:     {
200:         audio_debugger_ = std::make_unique<AudioDebugger>();
201:     }
202:     audio_debugger_->Feed(data);
203: #endif
204: 
205:     return true;
206: }

208: void AudioService::AudioInputTask()
209: {
210:     while (true)
211:     {
212:         EventBits_t bits = xEventGroupWaitBits(event_group_, AS_EVENT_AUDIO_TESTING_RUNNING | AS_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_RUNNING | AS_EVENT_AUDIO_PROCESSOR_RUNNING,
213:                                                pdFALSE, pdFALSE, portMAX_DELAY);
214: 
215:         if (service_stopped_)
216:         {
217:             break;
218:         }
219:         if (audio_input_need_warmup_)
220:         {
221:             audio_input_need_warmup_ = false;
222:             vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(120));
223:             continue;
224:         }
225: 
226:         /* Used for audio testing in NetworkConfiguring mode by clicking the BOOT button */
227:         if (bits & AS_EVENT_AUDIO_TESTING_RUNNING)
228:         {
229:             if (audio_testing_queue_.size() >= AUDIO_TESTING_MAX_DURATION_MS / OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS)
230:             {
231:                 ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Audio testing queue is full, stopping audio testing");
232:                 EnableAudioTesting(false);
233:                 continue;
234:             }
235:             std::vector<int16_t> data;
236:             int samples = OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS * 16000 / 1000;
237:             if (ReadAudioData(data, 16000, samples))
238:             {
239:                 // If input channels is 2, we need to fetch the left channel data
240:                 if (codec_->input_channels() == 2)
241:                 {
242:                     auto mono_data = std::vector<int16_t>(data.size() / 2);
243:                     for (size_t i = 0, j = 0; i < mono_data.size(); ++i, j += 2)
244:                     {
245:                         mono_data[i] = data[j];
246:                     }
247:                     data = std::move(mono_data);
248:                 }
249:                 PushTaskToEncodeQueue(kAudioTaskTypeEncodeToTestingQueue, std::move(data));
250:                 continue;
251:             }
252:         }
253: 
254:         /* Feed the wake word */
255:         if (bits & AS_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_RUNNING)
256:         {
257:             std::vector<int16_t> data;
258:             int samples = wake_word_->GetFeedSize();
259:             if (samples > 0)
260:             {
261:                 if (ReadAudioData(data, 16000, samples))
262:                 {
263:                     wake_word_->Feed(data);
264:                     continue;
265:                 }
266:             }
267:         }
268: 
269:         /* Feed the audio processor */
270:         if (bits & AS_EVENT_AUDIO_PROCESSOR_RUNNING)
271:         {
272:             std::vector<int16_t> data;
273:             int samples = audio_processor_->GetFeedSize();
274:             if (samples > 0)
275:             {
276:                 if (ReadAudioData(data, 16000, samples))
277:                 {
278:                     audio_processor_->Feed(std::move(data));
279:                     continue;
280:                 }
281:             }
282:         }
283: 
284:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Should not be here, bits: %lx", bits);
285:         break;
286:     }
287: 
288:     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Audio input task stopped");
289: }

333: void AudioService::OpusCodecTask()
334: {
335:     while (true)
336:     {
337:         std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
338:         audio_queue_cv_.wait(lock, [this]()
339:                              { return service_stopped_ ||
340:                                       (!audio_encode_queue_.empty() && audio_send_queue_.size() < MAX_SEND_PACKETS_IN_QUEUE) ||
341:                                       (!audio_decode_queue_.empty() && audio_playback_queue_.size() < MAX_PLAYBACK_TASKS_IN_QUEUE); });
342:         if (service_stopped_)
343:         {
344:             break;
345:         }
346: 
347:         /* Decode the audio from decode queue */
348:         if (!audio_decode_queue_.empty() && audio_playback_queue_.size() < MAX_PLAYBACK_TASKS_IN_QUEUE)
349:         {
350:             auto packet = std::move(audio_decode_queue_.front());
351:             audio_decode_queue_.pop_front();
352:             audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
353:             lock.unlock();
354: 
355:             auto task = std::make_unique<AudioTask>();
356:             task->type = kAudioTaskTypeDecodeToPlaybackQueue;
357:             task->timestamp = packet->timestamp;
358: 
359:             SetDecodeSampleRate(packet->sample_rate, packet->frame_duration);
360:             if (opus_decoder_->Decode(std::move(packet->payload), task->pcm))
361:             {
362:                 // Resample if the sample rate is different
363:                 if (opus_decoder_->sample_rate() != codec_->output_sample_rate())
364:                 {
365:                     int target_size = output_resampler_.GetOutputSamples(task->pcm.size());
366:                     std::vector<int16_t> resampled(target_size);
367:                     output_resampler_.Process(task->pcm.data(), task->pcm.size(), resampled.data());
368:                     task->pcm = std::move(resampled);
369:                 }
370: 
371:                 lock.lock();
372:                 audio_playback_queue_.push_back(std::move(task));
373:                 audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
374:             }
375:             else
376:             {
377:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to decode audio");
378:                 lock.lock();
379:             }
380:             debug_statistics_.decode_count++;
381:         }
382: 
383:         /* Encode the audio to send queue */
384:         if (!audio_encode_queue_.empty() && audio_send_queue_.size() < MAX_SEND_PACKETS_IN_QUEUE)
385:         {
386:             auto task = std::move(audio_encode_queue_.front());
387:             audio_encode_queue_.pop_front();
388:             audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
389:             lock.unlock();
390: 
391:             auto packet = std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>();
392:             packet->frame_duration = OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS;
393:             packet->sample_rate = 16000;
394:             packet->timestamp = task->timestamp;
395:             if (!opus_encoder_->Encode(std::move(task->pcm), packet->payload))
396:             {
397:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to encode audio");
398:                 continue;
399:             }
400: 
401:             if (task->type == kAudioTaskTypeEncodeToSendQueue)
402:             {
403:                 {
404:                     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
405:                     audio_send_queue_.push_back(std::move(packet));
406:                 }
407:                 if (callbacks_.on_send_queue_available)
408:                 {
409:                     callbacks_.on_send_queue_available();
410:                 }
411:             }
412:             else if (task->type == kAudioTaskTypeEncodeToTestingQueue)
413:             {
414:                 std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
415:                 audio_testing_queue_.push_back(std::move(packet));
416:             }
417:             debug_statistics_.encode_count++;
418:             lock.lock();
419:         }
420:     }
421: 
422:     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Opus codec task stopped");
423: }

426: {

428:     {

443: void AudioService::PushTaskToEncodeQueue(AudioTaskType type, std::vector<int16_t> &&pcm)
444: {
445:     auto task = std::make_unique<AudioTask>();
446:     task->type = type;
447:     task->pcm = std::move(pcm);
448: 
449:     /* Push the task to the encode queue */
450:     std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
451: 
452:     /* If the task is to send queue, we need to set the timestamp */
453:     if (type == kAudioTaskTypeEncodeToSendQueue && !timestamp_queue_.empty())
454:     {
455:         if (timestamp_queue_.size() <= MAX_TIMESTAMPS_IN_QUEUE)
456:         {
457:             task->timestamp = timestamp_queue_.front();
458:         }
459:         else
460:         {
461:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Timestamp queue (%u) is full, dropping timestamp", timestamp_queue_.size());
462:         }
463:         timestamp_queue_.pop_front();
464:     }
465: 
466:     audio_queue_cv_.wait(lock, [this]()
467:                          { return audio_encode_queue_.size() < MAX_ENCODE_TASKS_IN_QUEUE; });
468:     audio_encode_queue_.push_back(std::move(task));
469:     audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
470: }

476:     {
477:         if (wait)
478:         {
479:             audio_queue_cv_.wait(lock, [this]()
480:                                  { return audio_decode_queue_.size() < MAX_DECODE_PACKETS_IN_QUEUE; });
481:         }
482:         else
483:         {
484:             return false;
485:         }
486:     }
487:     audio_decode_queue_.push_back(std::move(packet));

518: std::unique_ptr<AudioStreamPacket> AudioService::PopWakeWordPacket()
519: {
520:     auto packet = std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>();
521:     if (wake_word_->GetWakeWordOpus(packet->payload))
522:     {
523:         return packet;
524:         if (pos == static_cast<size_t>(-1))
525:             break;
526:         offset = pos;
527:         if (offset + 27 > size)
528:             break;
529: 
530:         const uint8_t *page = buf + offset;
531:         uint8_t page_segments = page[26];
532:         size_t seg_table_off = offset + 27;
533:         if (seg_table_off + page_segments > size)
534:             break;
535: 
536:         size_t body_size = 0;
537:         for (size_t i = 0; i < page_segments; ++i)
538:             body_size += page[27 + i];
539: 
540:         size_t body_off = seg_table_off + page_segments;
541:         if (body_off + body_size > size)
542:             break;
543: 
544:         // Parse packets using lacing
545:         size_t cur = body_off;
546:         size_t seg_idx = 0;
547:         while (seg_idx < page_segments)
548:         {
549:             size_t pkt_len = 0;
550:             size_t pkt_start = cur;
551:             bool continued = false;
552:             do
553:             {
554:                 uint8_t l = page[27 + seg_idx++];
555:                 pkt_len += l;
556:                 cur += l;
557:                 continued = (l == 255);
558:             } while (continued && seg_idx < page_segments);
559: 
560:             if (pkt_len == 0)
561:                 continue;
562:             const uint8_t *pkt_ptr = buf + pkt_start;
563: 
564:             if (!seen_head)
565:             {
566:                 // 解析OpusHead包
567:                 if (pkt_len >= 19 && std::memcmp(pkt_ptr, "OpusHead", 8) == 0)
568:                 {
569:                     seen_head = true;
570: 
571:                     // OpusHead结构：[0-7] "OpusHead", [8] version, [9] channel_count, [10-11] pre_skip
572:                     // [12-15] input_sample_rate, [16-17] output_gain, [18] mapping_family
573:                     if (pkt_len >= 12)
574:                     {
575:                         uint8_t version = pkt_ptr[8];
576:                         uint8_t channel_count = pkt_ptr[9];
577: 
578:                         if (pkt_len >= 16)
579:                         {
580:                             // 读取输入采样率 (little-endian)
581:                             sample_rate = pkt_ptr[12] | (pkt_ptr[13] << 8) |
582:                                           (pkt_ptr[14] << 16) | (pkt_ptr[15] << 24);
583:                             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "OpusHead: version=%d, channels=%d, sample_rate=%d",
584:                                      version, channel_count, sample_rate);
585:                         }
586:                     }
587:                 }
588:                 continue;
589:             }
590:             if (!seen_tags)
591:             {
592:                 // Expect OpusTags in second packet
593:                 if (pkt_len >= 8 && std::memcmp(pkt_ptr, "OpusTags", 8) == 0)
594:                 {
595:                     seen_tags = true;
596:                 }
597:                 continue;
598:             }
599: 
600:             // Audio packet (Opus)
601:             auto packet = std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>();
602:             packet->sample_rate = sample_rate;
603:             packet->frame_duration = 60;
604:             packet->payload.resize(pkt_len);
605:             std::memcpy(packet->payload.data(), pkt_ptr, pkt_len);
606:             PushPacketToDecodeQueue(std::move(packet), true);
607:         }
608: 
609:         offset = body_off + body_size;
610:     }
611: }

630: void AudioService::CheckAndUpdateAudioPowerState()
631: {
632:     auto now = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
633:     auto input_elapsed = std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(now - last_input_time_).count();
634:     auto output_elapsed = std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(now - last_output_time_).count();
635:     if (input_elapsed > AUDIO_POWER_TIMEOUT_MS && codec_->input_enabled())
636:     {
637:         codec_->EnableInput(false);
638:     }
639:     if (output_elapsed > AUDIO_POWER_TIMEOUT_MS && codec_->output_enabled())
640:     {
641:         codec_->EnableOutput(false);
642:     }
643:     if (!codec_->input_enabled() && !codec_->output_enabled())
644:     {
645:         esp_timer_stop(audio_power_timer_);
646:     }
647: }

</file>
The `AudioService::PlaySound` function plays an OGG audio file by first enabling the audio output if it is disabled, then parsing the OGG container format to extract and decode Opus audio packets. It reads the Opus header to determine the sample rate and other parameters, ensuring proper playback configuration before sending the decoded audio data to the output device. The function handles basic OGG page structure and packet segmentation to correctly locate and process the audio content.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- Both implementations correctly parse an OGG/Opus stream and extract audio packets.
- The core logic of parsing pages, segments, and reconstructing packets is preserved.
- However, the generated code interprets the input as a filename and reads from a file, whereas the ground truth expects raw data directly in the string_view.
- There are minor differences in default sample rate (48000 vs 16000) and logging detail.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature differs: ground truth takes `const std::string_view& ogg` (raw data), generated takes `const std::string_view &filename` (treated as path).
- Control flow is broadly similar but diverges due to file I/O operations in generated code.
- Core packet parsing loop and handling of OpusHead/OpusTags/audio packets mirrors the ground truth.
- Use of lambdas and direct memory access patterns differ between versions.

Functional Completeness:
- Generated code includes additional robust error checking for file operations which is absent in ground truth.
- Both handle enabling output when needed.
- Lacing value processing and packet reconstruction logic is mostly consistent.
- Missing some metadata logging present in ground truth (e.g., version, channel count).
- Overall functionality overlaps significantly, though assumptions about input type lead to structural divergence.

results.description: The `AudioService::SetModelsList` function sets the list of speech recognition models and initializes the appropriate wake word detection mechanism based on the available models and target hardware. It selects between custom, AFE-based, or ESP-based wake word engines depending on model prefixes and platform configuration. If a valid wake word engine is initialized, it registers a callback to handle wake word detection events.

140: bool AudioService::ReadAudioData(std::vector<int16_t> &data, int sample_rate, int samples)
141: {
142:     if (!codec_->input_enabled())
143:     {
144:         esp_timer_stop(audio_power_timer_);
145:         esp_timer_start_periodic(audio_power_timer_, AUDIO_POWER_CHECK_INTERVAL_MS * 1000);
146:         codec_->EnableInput(true);
147:     }
148: 
149:     if (codec_->input_sample_rate() != sample_rate)
150:     {
151:         data.resize(samples * codec_->input_sample_rate() / sample_rate * codec_->input_channels());
152:         if (!codec_->InputData(data))
153:         {
154:             return false;
155:         }
156:         if (codec_->input_channels() == 2)
157:         {
158:             auto mic_channel = std::vector<int16_t>(data.size() / 2);
159:             auto reference_channel = std::vector<int16_t>(data.size() / 2);
160:             for (size_t i = 0, j = 0; i < mic_channel.size(); ++i, j += 2)
161:             {
162:                 mic_channel[i] = data[j];
163:                 reference_channel[i] = data[j + 1];
164:             }
165:             auto resampled_mic = std::vector<int16_t>(input_resampler_.GetOutputSamples(mic_channel.size()));
166:             auto resampled_reference = std::vector<int16_t>(reference_resampler_.GetOutputSamples(reference_channel.size()));
167:             input_resampler_.Process(mic_channel.data(), mic_channel.size(), resampled_mic.data());
168:             reference_resampler_.Process(reference_channel.data(), reference_channel.size(), resampled_reference.data());
169:             data.resize(resampled_mic.size() + resampled_reference.size());
170:             for (size_t i = 0, j = 0; i < resampled_mic.size(); ++i, j += 2)
171:             {
172:                 data[j] = resampled_mic[i];
173:                 data[j + 1] = resampled_reference[i];
174:             }
175:         }
176:         else
177:         {
178:             auto resampled = std::vector<int16_t>(input_resampler_.GetOutputSamples(data.size()));
179:             input_resampler_.Process(data.data(), data.size(), resampled.data());
180:             data = std::move(resampled);
181:         }
182:     }
183:     else
184:     {
185:         data.resize(samples * codec_->input_channels());
186:         if (!codec_->InputData(data))
187:         {
188:             return false;
189:         }
190:     }
191: 
192:     /* Update the last input time */
193:     last_input_time_ = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
194:     debug_statistics_.input_count++;
195: 
196: #if CONFIG_USE_AUDIO_DEBUGGER
197:     // 音频调试：发送原始音频数据
198:     if (audio_debugger_ == nullptr)
199:     {
200:         audio_debugger_ = std::make_unique<AudioDebugger>();
201:     }
202:     audio_debugger_->Feed(data);
203: #endif
204: 
205:     return true;
206: }

235:             std::vector<int16_t> data;
236:             int samples = OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS * 16000 / 1000;
237:             if (ReadAudioData(data, 16000, samples))
238:             {
239:                 // If input channels is 2, we need to fetch the left channel data
240:                 if (codec_->input_channels() == 2)
241:                 {
242:                     auto mono_data = std::vector<int16_t>(data.size() / 2);
243:                     for (size_t i = 0, j = 0; i < mono_data.size(); ++i, j += 2)
244:                     {
245:                         mono_data[i] = data[j];
246:                     }
247:                     data = std::move(mono_data);
248:                 }
249:                 PushTaskToEncodeQueue(kAudioTaskTypeEncodeToTestingQueue, std::move(data));
250:                 continue;
251:             }
252:         }
253: 
254:         /* Feed the wake word */
255:         if (bits & AS_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_RUNNING)
256:         {
257:             std::vector<int16_t> data;
258:             int samples = wake_word_->GetFeedSize();
259:             if (samples > 0)
260:             {
261:                 if (ReadAudioData(data, 16000, samples))
262:                 {
263:                     wake_word_->Feed(data);
264:                     continue;
265:                 }
266:             }
267:         }
268: 
269:         /* Feed the audio processor */
270:         if (bits & AS_EVENT_AUDIO_PROCESSOR_RUNNING)
271:         {
272:             std::vector<int16_t> data;
273:             int samples = audio_processor_->GetFeedSize();
274:             if (samples > 0)
275:             {
276:                 if (ReadAudioData(data, 16000, samples))
277:                 {
278:                     audio_processor_->Feed(std::move(data));
279:                     continue;
280:                 }
281:             }
282:         }
283: 
284:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Should not be here, bits: %lx", bits);
285:         break;
286:     }
287: 
288:     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Audio input task stopped");
289: }

291: void AudioService::AudioOutputTask()
292: {
293:     while (true)
294:     {
295:         std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
296:         audio_queue_cv_.wait(lock, [this]()
297:                              { return !audio_playback_queue_.empty() || service_stopped_; });
298:         if (service_stopped_)
299:         {
300:             break;
301:         }
302: 
303:         auto task = std::move(audio_playback_queue_.front());
304:         audio_playback_queue_.pop_front();
305:         audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
306:         lock.unlock();
307: 
308:         if (!codec_->output_enabled())
309:         {
310:             esp_timer_stop(audio_power_timer_);
311:             esp_timer_start_periodic(audio_power_timer_, AUDIO_POWER_CHECK_INTERVAL_MS * 1000);
312:             codec_->EnableOutput(true);
313:         }
314:         codec_->OutputData(task->pcm);
315: 
316:         /* Update the last output time */
317:         last_output_time_ = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
318:         debug_statistics_.playback_count++;
319: 
320: #if CONFIG_USE_SERVER_AEC
321:         /* Record the timestamp for server AEC */
322:         if (task->timestamp > 0)
323:         {
324:             lock.lock();
325:             timestamp_queue_.push_back(task->timestamp);
326:         }
327: #endif
328:     }
329: 
330:     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Audio output task stopped");

333: void AudioService::OpusCodecTask()
334: {
335:     while (true)
336:     {
337:         std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
338:         audio_queue_cv_.wait(lock, [this]()
339:                              { return service_stopped_ ||
340:                                       (!audio_encode_queue_.empty() && audio_send_queue_.size() < MAX_SEND_PACKETS_IN_QUEUE) ||
341:                                       (!audio_decode_queue_.empty() && audio_playback_queue_.size() < MAX_PLAYBACK_TASKS_IN_QUEUE); });
342:         if (service_stopped_)
343:         {
344:             break;
345:         }
346: 
347:         /* Decode the audio from decode queue */
348:         if (!audio_decode_queue_.empty() && audio_playback_queue_.size() < MAX_PLAYBACK_TASKS_IN_QUEUE)
349:         {
350:             auto packet = std::move(audio_decode_queue_.front());
351:             audio_decode_queue_.pop_front();
352:             audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
353:             lock.unlock();
354: 
355:             auto task = std::make_unique<AudioTask>();
356:             task->type = kAudioTaskTypeDecodeToPlaybackQueue;
357:             task->timestamp = packet->timestamp;
358: 
359:             SetDecodeSampleRate(packet->sample_rate, packet->frame_duration);
360:             if (opus_decoder_->Decode(std::move(packet->payload), task->pcm))
361:             {
362:                 // Resample if the sample rate is different
363:                 if (opus_decoder_->sample_rate() != codec_->output_sample_rate())
364:                 {
365:                     int target_size = output_resampler_.GetOutputSamples(task->pcm.size());
366:                     std::vector<int16_t> resampled(target_size);
367:                     output_resampler_.Process(task->pcm.data(), task->pcm.size(), resampled.data());
368:                     task->pcm = std::move(resampled);
369:                 }
370: 
371:                 lock.lock();
372:                 audio_playback_queue_.push_back(std::move(task));
373:                 audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
374:             }
375:             else
376:             {
377:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to decode audio");
378:                 lock.lock();
379:             }
380:             debug_statistics_.decode_count++;
381:         }
382: 
383:         /* Encode the audio to send queue */
384:         if (!audio_encode_queue_.empty() && audio_send_queue_.size() < MAX_SEND_PACKETS_IN_QUEUE)
385:         {
386:             auto task = std::move(audio_encode_queue_.front());
387:             audio_encode_queue_.pop_front();
388:             audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
389:             lock.unlock();
390: 
391:             auto packet = std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>();
392:             packet->frame_duration = OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS;
393:             packet->sample_rate = 16000;
394:             packet->timestamp = task->timestamp;
395:             if (!opus_encoder_->Encode(std::move(task->pcm), packet->payload))
396:             {
397:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to encode audio");
398:                 continue;
399:             }
400: 
401:             if (task->type == kAudioTaskTypeEncodeToSendQueue)
402:             {
403:                 {
404:                     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
405:                     audio_send_queue_.push_back(std::move(packet));
406:                 }
407:                 if (callbacks_.on_send_queue_available)
408:                 {
409:                     callbacks_.on_send_queue_available();
410:                 }
411:             }
412:             else if (task->type == kAudioTaskTypeEncodeToTestingQueue)
413:             {
414:                 std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
415:                 audio_testing_queue_.push_back(std::move(packet));
416:             }
417:             debug_statistics_.encode_count++;
418:             lock.lock();
419:         }
420:     }
421: 
422:     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Opus codec task stopped");
423: }

425: void AudioService::SetDecodeSampleRate(int sample_rate, int frame_duration)
426: {
427:     if (opus_decoder_->sample_rate() == sample_rate && opus_decoder_->duration_ms() == frame_duration)
428:     {
429:         return;
430:     }
431: 
432:     opus_decoder_.reset();
433:     opus_decoder_ = std::make_unique<OpusDecoderWrapper>(sample_rate, 1, frame_duration);
434: 
435:     auto codec = Board::GetInstance().GetAudioCodec();
436:     if (opus_decoder_->sample_rate() != codec->output_sample_rate())
437:     {
438:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Resampling audio from %d to %d", opus_decoder_->sample_rate(), codec->output_sample_rate());
439:         output_resampler_.Configure(opus_decoder_->sample_rate(), codec->output_sample_rate());
440:     }
441: }

443: void AudioService::PushTaskToEncodeQueue(AudioTaskType type, std::vector<int16_t> &&pcm)
444: {
445:     auto task = std::make_unique<AudioTask>();
446:     task->type = type;
447:     task->pcm = std::move(pcm);
448: 
449:     /* Push the task to the encode queue */
450:     std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
451: 
452:     /* If the task is to send queue, we need to set the timestamp */
453:     if (type == kAudioTaskTypeEncodeToSendQueue && !timestamp_queue_.empty())
454:     {
455:         if (timestamp_queue_.size() <= MAX_TIMESTAMPS_IN_QUEUE)
456:         {
457:             task->timestamp = timestamp_queue_.front();
458:         }
459:         else
460:         {
461:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Timestamp queue (%u) is full, dropping timestamp", timestamp_queue_.size());
462:         }
463:         timestamp_queue_.pop_front();
464:     }
465: 
466:     audio_queue_cv_.wait(lock, [this]()
467:                          { return audio_encode_queue_.size() < MAX_ENCODE_TASKS_IN_QUEUE; });
468:     audio_encode_queue_.push_back(std::move(task));
469:     audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
470: }

615: void AudioService::PlaySound(const std::string_view &ogg)
616: {
617:     if (!codec_->output_enabled())
618:     {
619:         esp_timer_stop(audio_power_timer_);
620:         esp_timer_start_periodic(audio_power_timer_, AUDIO_POWER_CHECK_INTERVAL_MS * 1000);
621:         codec_->EnableOutput(true);
622:     }
623: 
624:     const uint8_t *buf = reinterpret_cast<const uint8_t *>(ogg.data());
625:     size_t size = ogg.size();
626:     size_t offset = 0;
627: 
628:     auto find_page = [&](size_t start) -> size_t
629:     {
630:         for (size_t i = start; i + 4 <= size; ++i)
631:         {
632:             if (buf[i] == 'O' && buf[i + 1] == 'g' && buf[i + 2] == 'g' && buf[i + 3] == 'S')
633:                 return i;
634:         }
635:         return static_cast<size_t>(-1);
636:     };
637: 
638:     bool seen_head = false;
639:     bool seen_tags = false;
640:     int sample_rate = 16000; // 默认值
641: 
642:     while (true)
643:     {
644:         size_t pos = find_page(offset);
645:         if (pos == static_cast<size_t>(-1))
646:             break;
647:         offset = pos;
648:         if (offset + 27 > size)
649:             break;
650: 
651:         const uint8_t *page = buf + offset;
652:         uint8_t page_segments = page[26];
653:         size_t seg_table_off = offset + 27;
654:         if (seg_table_off + page_segments > size)
655:             break;
656: 
657:         size_t body_size = 0;
658:         for (size_t i = 0; i < page_segments; ++i)
659:             body_size += page[27 + i];
660: 
661:         size_t body_off = seg_table_off + page_segments;
662:         if (body_off + body_size > size)
663:             break;
664: 
665:         // Parse packets using lacing
666:         size_t cur = body_off;
667:         size_t seg_idx = 0;
668:         while (seg_idx < page_segments)
669:         {
670:             size_t pkt_len = 0;
671:             size_t pkt_start = cur;
672:             bool continued = false;
673:             do
674:             {
675:                 uint8_t l = page[27 + seg_idx++];
676:                 pkt_len += l;
677:                 cur += l;
678:                 continued = (l == 255);
679:             } while (continued && seg_idx < page_segments);
680: 
681:             if (pkt_len == 0)
682:                 continue;
683:             const uint8_t *pkt_ptr = buf + pkt_start;
684: 
685:             if (!seen_head)
686:             {
687:                 // 解析OpusHead包
688:                 if (pkt_len >= 19 && std::memcmp(pkt_ptr, "OpusHead", 8) == 0)
689:                 {
690:                     seen_head = true;
691: 
692:                     // OpusHead结构：[0-7] "OpusHead", [8] version, [9] channel_count, [10-11] pre_skip
693:                     // [12-15] input_sample_rate, [16-17] output_gain, [18] mapping_family
694:                     if (pkt_len >= 12)
695:                     {
696:                         uint8_t version = pkt_ptr[8];
697:                         uint8_t channel_count = pkt_ptr[9];
698: 
699:                         if (pkt_len >= 16)
700:                         {
701:                             // 读取输入采样率 (little-endian)
702:                             sample_rate = pkt_ptr[12] | (pkt_ptr[13] << 8) |
703:                                           (pkt_ptr[14] << 16) | (pkt_ptr[15] << 24);
704:                             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "OpusHead: version=%d, channels=%d, sample_rate=%d",
705:                                      version, channel_count, sample_rate);
706:                         }
707:                     }
708:                 }
709:                 continue;
710:             }
711:             if (!seen_tags)
712:             {
713:                 // Expect OpusTags in second packet
714:                 if (pkt_len >= 8 && std::memcmp(pkt_ptr, "OpusTags", 8) == 0)
715:                 {
716:                     seen_tags = true;
717:                 }
718:                 continue;
719:             }
720: 
721:             // Audio packet (Opus)
722:             auto packet = std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>();
723:             packet->sample_rate = sample_rate;
724:             packet->frame_duration = 60;
725:             packet->payload.resize(pkt_len);
726:             std::memcpy(packet->payload.data(), pkt_ptr, pkt_len);
727:             PushPacketToDecodeQueue(std::move(packet), true);
728:         }
729: 
730:         offset = body_off + body_size;
731:     }
732: }

751: void AudioService::CheckAndUpdateAudioPowerState()
752: {
753:     auto now = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
754:     auto input_elapsed = std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(now - last_input_time_).count();
755:     auto output_elapsed = std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(now - last_output_time_).count();
756:     if (input_elapsed > AUDIO_POWER_TIMEOUT_MS && codec_->input_enabled())
757:     {
758:         codec_->EnableInput(false);
759:     }
760:     if (output_elapsed > AUDIO_POWER_TIMEOUT_MS && codec_->output_enabled())
761:     {
762:         codec_->EnableOutput(false);
763:     }
764:     if (!codec_->input_enabled() && !codec_->output_enabled())
765:     {
766:         esp_timer_stop(audio_power_timer_);
767:     }
768: }

The `AudioService::SetModelsList` function sets the list of speech recognition models and initializes the appropriate wake word detection mechanism based on the available models and target hardware. It selects between custom, AFE-based, or ESP-based wake word engines depending on model prefixes and platform configuration. If a valid wake word engine is initialized, it registers a callback to handle wake word detection events.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to implement a similar logic but diverges significantly in how it determines which wake word engine to use.
- Instead of using the `esp_srmodel_filter` API with specific prefixes (`ESP_MN_PREFIX`, `ESP_WN_PREFIX`) as in the ground truth, the generated code manually checks model names for a "custom_" prefix. This is semantically different and may not behave the same across all cases.
- The early return on null or empty list in the generated code alters behavior compared to the ground truth, which always assigns `models_list_`.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches.
- Both versions assign `models_list_` and attempt to initialize a wake word engine based on conditions.
- However, the control flow differs due to added null/empty checks and custom filtering logic that doesn't align with the original API usage pattern.
- Use of preprocessor directives is preserved, but applied differently.
- Callback registration block is nearly identical, showing good similarity there.

Functional Completeness:
- Misses correct usage of `esp_srmodel_filter()` with proper prefixes — critical functionality from the ground truth.
- Introduces new logic (checking for "custom_" prefix) not present in the reference implementation, potentially leading to incorrect initialization paths.
- Lacks distinction between MN (multinet) and WN (wakenet) model types used in conditional wake word engine selection in the ground truth.
- Early exit condition (null or zero count) changes functional behavior and might prevent necessary cleanup or reinitialization steps.

The function also supports optional reference input for acoustic echo cancellation.

58: void Es8388AudioCodec::EnableInput(bool enable)
59: {
60:     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(data_if_mutex_);
61:     if (enable == input_enabled_)
62:     {
63:         return;
64:     }
65:     if (enable)
66:     {
67:         esp_codec_dev_sample_info_t fs = {
68:             .bits_per_sample = 16,
69:             .channel = (uint8_t)input_channels_,
70:             .channel_mask = ESP_CODEC_DEV_MAKE_CHANNEL_MASK(0),
71:             .sample_rate = (uint32_t)input_sample_rate_,
72:             .mclk_multiple = 0,
73:         };
74:         if (input_reference_)
75:         {
76:             fs.channel_mask |= ESP_CODEC_DEV_MAKE_CHANNEL_MASK(1);
77:         }
78:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_open(input_dev_, &fs));
79:         if (input_reference_)
80:         {
81:             uint8_t gain = (11 << 4) + 0;
82:             ctrl_if_->write_reg(ctrl_if_, 0x09, 1, &gain, 1);
83:         }
84:         else
85:         {
86:             ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_set_in_gain(input_dev_, input_gain_));
87:         }
88:     }
89:     else
90:     {
91:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_close(input_dev_));
92:     }
93:     AudioCodec::EnableInput(enable);
94: }

96: void Es8388AudioCodec::EnableOutput(bool enable)
97: {
98:     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(data_if_mutex_);
99:     if (enable == output_enabled_)
100:     {
101:         return;
102:     }
103:     if (enable)
104:     {
105:         esp_codec_dev_sample_info_t fs = {
106:             .bits_per_sample = 16,
107:             .channel = 1,
108:             .channel_mask = 0,
109:             .sample_rate = (uint32_t)output_sample_rate_,
110:             .mclk_multiple = 0,
111:         };
112:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_open(output_dev_, &fs));
113:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_set_out_vol(output_dev_, output_volume_));
114: 
115:         // Set analog output volume to 0dB, default is -45dB
116:         uint8_t reg_val = 30; // 0dB
117:         if (input_reference_)
118:         {
119:             reg_val = 27;

121:         uint8_t regs[] = {46, 47, 48, 49}; // HP_LVOL, HP_RVOL, SPK_LVOL, SPK_RVOL
122:         for (uint8_t reg : regs)

124:             ctrl_if_->write_reg(ctrl_if_, reg, 1, &reg_val, 1);
125:         }
126:

129:             gpio_set_level(pa_pin_, 1);

131:     }
132:     else

135:         if (pa_pin_ != GPIO_NUM_NC)

137:             gpio_set_level(pa_pin_, 0);
138:         }
139:     }
140:     AudioCodec::EnableOutput(enable);
141: }

The `Es8388AudioCodec` constructor initializes the ES8388 audio codec for full-duplex operation, setting up I2S data interfaces, I2C control interface, and GPIO for power amplifier control. It configures both input and output audio devices with specified sample rates and channel settings, enabling simultaneous audio capture and playback. The function also supports optional reference input for acoustic echo cancellation.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to initialize an ES8388 audio codec but uses a different set of APIs and configuration structures than the ground truth.
- It incorrectly assumes fixed channel counts instead of dynamically determining them based on `input_reference`.
- Key logic such as setting up duplex channels via `CreateDuplexChannels` is missing, which affects how input/output devices are handled.
- Initialization sequence differs significantly; e.g., manual register writes are used instead of relying on higher-level abstractions provided by the driver framework in the ground truth.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches mostly except for parameter names and types (`addr` vs `es8388_addr`, initialization list usage).
- Control flow diverges greatly — generated version manually configures registers while ground truth relies on structured codec/device initialization functions like `es8388_codec_new()` and `esp_codec_dev_new()`.
- Use of error-checking macros like `ESP_ERROR_CHECK` is present in generated code but not aligned with actual API usage patterns from ground truth.

Functional Completeness:
- Missing critical parts: PA pin handling beyond assignment, no disabling of auto-disable behavior (`esp_codec_set_disable_when_closed`), lack of master mode setup, voltage gain settings.
- Some elements like GPIO interface creation exist but use incorrect or placeholder configurations (e.g., `.gpio_intr_handle = NULL` without justification).
- Manual register writes indicate misunderstanding of required codec setup steps compared to using standard configuration interfaces.
- Overall, many functional aspects of the original constructor—especially those related to device integration within the ESP audio subsystem—are incomplete or misrepresented.

The `CreateDuplexChannels` function initializes full-duplex audio communication channels for the ES8388 codec, configuring both transmission and reception paths with specified GPIO pins and standard I2S settings. It sets up the I2S peripheral in master mode with stereo 16-bit audio at a configured sample rate, enabling simultaneous audio input and output. This establishes the hardware foundation for real-time audio processing and communication.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to create duplex I2S channels, which aligns with the intent of the ground truth.
- However, it uses an older/deprecated I2S API (`i2s_driver_install`, `i2s_set_pin`) instead of the newer channel-based API (`i2s_new_channel`, `i2s_channel_init_std_mode`), leading to different internal behavior and resource management.
- It incorrectly assumes a single I2S_NUM_0 handle can be used for both TX and RX via ring buffers, whereas the ground truth properly separates TX and RX handles using the new API.
- Sample rate handling differs; the ground truth enforces equality between input/output rates and uses `output_sample_rate_`, while the generated code uses `input_sample_rate_`.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches exactly — same name, class scope, and parameters.
- Control flow is linear in both cases but differs significantly in implementation approach: one uses legacy driver install, the other uses modern channel allocation.
- API usage diverges greatly — old-style I2S setup vs. new-style channel configuration.
- Missing elements like explicit master role declaration, DMA descriptor/frame numbers, GPIO inversion flags, and logging in the generated version reduce structural alignment.

Functional Completeness:
- Core functionality (setting up I2S for full-duplex communication) is addressed.
- Missing features from ground truth include: separate transmit/receive channel initialization, proper clock configuration with MCLK, explicit slot configurations (stereo, bit shifts, endianness), automatic buffer clearing policies, interrupt priority settings, and debug logging.
- Incorrect or incomplete handling of ring buffer assignment suggests functional gaps in duplex stream separation.

results.description: The `EnableInput` function controls the audio input functionality of the ES8388 codec, enabling or disabling microphone input based on the provided boolean parameter. When enabled, it configures and opens the input device with specified sample rate, bit depth, and channel settings, and applies either a fixed gain or adjustable input gain. When disabled, it closes the input device to stop audio capture.

The `EnableInput` function controls the audio input functionality of the ES8388 codec, enabling or disabling microphone input based on the provided boolean parameter. When enabled, it configures and opens the input device with specified sample rate, bit depth, and channel settings, and applies either a fixed gain or adjustable input gain. When disabled, it closes the input device to stop audio capture.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The overall logic of enabling/disabling input is preserved.
- Both versions use a mutex lock and check for redundant state changes.
- However, there are critical semantic differences:
  * In the ground truth, `channel_mask` is computed using `ESP_CODEC_DEV_MAKE_CHANNEL_MASK`, which correctly handles channel mapping based on `input_reference_`. In contrast, the generated code hardcodes `.channel_mask = 0`, which may result in incorrect audio routing or capture behavior.
  * Gain setting differs significantly: Ground truth uses either direct register manipulation with a specific bit-packed gain value (for reference input) or an abstraction (`esp_codec_dev_set_in_gain`). Generated code directly writes to registers 20 and 21 without proper masking or documented correspondence to hardware specs.
  * The condition for applying fixed vs adjustable gain is handled differently — ground truth checks `input_reference_` before opening device and configures accordingly; generated code does so after opening but applies similar logic.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches exactly.
- Control flow (lock, early return, conditional enable/disable branches, call to parent method) is nearly identical.
- Mutex locking pattern and usage of ESP-IDF error checking macros are consistent.
- Use of structs like `esp_codec_dev_sample_info_t` and APIs such as `esp_codec_dev_open/close` aligns well.
- Minor stylistic differences exist in formatting and comments, but these do not affect structural integrity.

Functional Completeness:
- Core functionality (opening/closing input device, managing input state) is covered.
- Missing key behaviors:
  * Proper computation of `channel_mask` that respects multi-channel inputs.
  * Use of abstracted gain-setting API when appropriate (`esp_codec_dev_set_in_gain`) instead of hardcoded register access.
  * Bit manipulation for gain register (e.g., `(11 << 4) + 0`) is lost in favor of simple numeric literals.
  * No handling of `mclk_multiple` beyond zero-initialization, though this might be intentional depending on context.
- Some added complexity in generated version (explicit dual register writes for L/R gains) suggests attempt at completeness but lacks precision compared to ground truth abstractions.

When enabled, it configures the codec's sample rate and volume settings, sets analog output levels, and activates the power amplifier pin if present. When disabled, it closes the codec output device and deactivates the power amplifier pin.

When enabled, it configures the codec's sample rate and volume settings, sets analog output levels, and activates the power amplifier pin if present. When disabled, it closes the codec output device and deactivates the power amplifier pin.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The overall logic of enabling/disabling audio output is preserved.
- Both versions use a mutex lock and check for redundant state changes.
- Key differences include channel configuration (mono vs stereo), handling of analog volume registers, and input reference logic which affects volume settings in the ground truth but not in the generated code.
- The semantic flow for opening/closing the device and controlling the PA pin is consistent.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches exactly.
- Control flow (lock, early return, if-else for enable/disable) is nearly identical.
- Use of ESP-IDF APIs like esp_codec_dev_open/close and error checking via ESP_ERROR_CHECK are maintained.
- Mutex locking pattern and call to parent class method at the end are preserved.

Functional Completeness:
- Missing critical functionality: The generated code omits setting analog output volumes (HP_LVOL, HP_RVOL, SPK_LVOL, SPK_RVOL registers).
- Lacks input reference-based volume adjustment logic.
- Channel configuration differs (stereo vs mono) which may affect compatibility.

- Core device open/close and digital volume control are correctly implemented.

The AudioDebugger initializes a UDP socket connection to a specified server for debugging purposes, parsing the server's IP address and port from configuration settings. It logs the initialization status and handles errors if the socket creation or address parsing fails. This functionality supports real-time audio debugging by enabling UDP-based data transmission to an external server.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
The generated code largely captures the intended behavior of initializing a UDP socket for an audio debugger. However, there are key differences:
- The ground truth parses an "IP:PORT" string from CONFIG_AUDIO_DEBUG_UDP_SERVER, while the generated code assumes separate configuration values for IP and PORT.
- Error handling differs slightly; the ground truth uses ESP_LOGW for warnings, while the generated code uses ESP_LOGE for errors, which changes the semantic meaning of the log level.
- The generated code returns early on socket creation failure, whereas the ground truth lets udp_sockfd_ remain negative.

Structure Similarity:
Both codes follow a similar high-level structure under a preprocessor guard, initialize a UDP socket, configure sockaddr_in, and include error checking/logging. However:
- Functionally different APIs are used: inet_aton() vs inet_pton().
- Control flow diverges due to how IP and PORT are parsed/used.
- Log messages differ in content and severity level.

Functional Completeness:
Most core functionality is preserved:
- UDP socket initialization.
- Server address setup.
- Logging and error handling.

Missing elements:
- No parsing of combined "IP:PORT" string in generated code.
- Lacks validation that the server address contains a colon in the ground truth style.
- Different logging levels may affect downstream monitoring or debugging expectations.

The `AfeWakeWord::Initialize` function sets up the acoustic front-end (AFE) for wake word detection by configuring the audio codec, loading wake word models, and initializing the AFE processing pipeline with appropriate settings for echo cancellation and memory allocation. It also starts a dedicated task for audio detection. The function returns true if initialization is successful, otherwise false.

qualitative.analysis:
The generated code shows a significant divergence from the ground truth in terms of both logic and implementation details:

Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code incorrectly assumes that `codec->Initialize()` must be called, which is not part of the original design.
- It misuses `esp_srmodel_init`, passing `models_list` directly instead of a string path or handling it according to actual API usage shown in the ground truth.
- The AFE configuration process is oversimplified and does not reflect the complex setup involving input format construction, specific AFE types/modes, memory allocation preferences, or core affinity settings found in the ground truth.
- Wake word extraction logic is completely different — the ground truth parses model names for ESP_WN_PREFIX and extracts wake words via `esp_srmodel_get_wake_words`, whereas the generated code naively copies model names assuming a struct layout that doesn't match the real API.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature differs slightly due to added null checks on `codec`.
- Control flow diverges significantly: error handling paths are different, task creation styles vary (lambda capture style vs raw pointer casting), and overall sequencing of operations like model loading and AFE initialization do not align.
- Many key API calls such as `afe_config_init`, `esp_afe_handle_from_config`, custom input format generation based on channel count, and reference mic handling are missing entirely.

Functional Completeness:
- Missing critical functionality including proper AFE configuration with input format derived from codec channels, correct use of `AFE_TYPE_SR` and performance mode settings, explicit assignment of `wakenet_model_`, and parsing wake words correctly using delimiters.
- Incorrect assumptions about data structures (`model_num`, `model[i].name`) that don't correspond to the actual `srmodel_list_t` definition used in the ground truth.
- Lacks support for optional models list parameter handling (i.e., defaulting to built-in "model" when null).
- Task priority and stack size choices differ without justification, potentially affecting system behavior.

Overall, while the generated code attempts to follow a general pattern of initializing components and starting a detection task, it fails to accurately replicate the detailed logic, correct APIs, and nuanced configuration required by the underlying embedded audio framework.

When a wake word is detected, it stops the detection process, stores the relevant audio data, and triggers a callback with the identified wake word. The control flow, conditionals, and state handling are perfectly matched.

Structurally, the two codes are indistinguishable except for minor stylistic differences such as spacing and brace placement, which do not affect functionality or readability. Function names, variable names, API calls, and overall program structure are completely consistent between the two.

The `AfeWakeWord::EncodeWakeWordData` function encodes captured wake word audio data from PCM format to Opus format using a dedicated real-time task. It processes each PCM packet through an Opus encoder with low complexity settings for fast encoding, stores the resulting Opus packets in a thread-safe buffer, and signals completion via a condition variable. The function manages dynamic memory allocation for the task stack and buffer, ensuring efficient use of SPIRAM and internal memory resources.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness (60/100):
The generated code attempts to implement the same functionality but diverges significantly in approach. While both versions create a dedicated task for encoding wake word data using Opus, the generated version uses a continuous loop with condition variable waiting, whereas the ground truth processes all data at once and then terminates the task. The generated code also maintains the encoder alive indefinitely, while the ground truth creates and destroys it per execution. Memory management differs, with the generated version adding error handling and cleanup that wasn't present in the original.

Structure Similarity (45/100):
Functionally, the generated code splits the logic into two methods (EncodeWakeWordData and EncodeWakeWordTask) instead of using a lambda as in the ground truth. Stack allocation is done differently - the ground truth allocates 4096*7 bytes while the generated code uses 4096*sizeof(StackType_t). Task creation parameters differ including priority (5 vs 2) and task names. The generated code includes additional error checking and logging not found in the reference implementation.

Functional Completeness (55/100):
The core functionality of encoding PCM data to Opus is preserved, but several aspects are missing or altered. The generated code omits timing measurements present in the ground truth. It changes how encoded packets are handled - using a deque and continuous processing rather than batch processing followed by termination. Additional features like setting bitrate and different mutex notification patterns are introduced, but critical elements such as clearing PCM data after processing occur at different points. Some important behaviors like pushing an empty vector at the end to signal completion are retained, though implementation details vary.

116: void CustomWakeWord::Feed(const std::vector<int16_t> &data)
117: {
118:     if (multinet_model_data_ == nullptr || !running_)
119:     {
120:         return;
121:     }
122: 
123:     esp_mn_state_t mn_state;
124:     // If input channels is 2, we need to fetch the left channel data
125:     if (codec_->input_channels() == 2)
126:     {
127:         auto mono_data = std::vector<int16_t>(data.size() / 2);
128:         for (size_t i = 0, j = 0; i < mono_data.size(); ++i, j += 2)
129:         {
130:             mono_data[i] = data[j];
131:         }
132: 
133:         StoreWakeWordData(mono_data);
134:         mn_state = multinet_->detect(multinet_model_data_, const_cast<int16_t *>(mono_data.data()));
135:     }
136:     else
137:     {
138:         StoreWakeWordData(data);
139:         mn_state = multinet_->detect(multinet_model_data_, const_cast<int16_t *>(data.data()));
140:     }
141: 
142:     if (mn_state == ESP_MN_STATE_DETECTING)
143:     {
144:         return;
145:     }
146:     else if (mn_state == ESP_MN_STATE_DETECTED)
147:     {
148:         esp_mn_results_t *mn_result = multinet_->get_results(multinet_model_data_);
149:         for (int i = 0; i < mn_result->num && running_; i++)
150:         {
151:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Custom wake word detected: command_id=%d, string=%s, prob=%f",
152:                      mn_result->command_id[i], mn_result->string, mn_result->prob[i]);
153:             auto &command = commands_[mn_result->command_id[i] - 1];
154:             if (command.action == "wake")
155:             {
156:                 last_detected_wake_word_ = command.text;
157:                 running_ = false;
158: 
159:                 if (wake_word_detected_callback_)
160:                 {
161:                     wake_word_detected_callback_(last_detected_wake_word_);
162:                 }
163:             }
164:         }
165:         multinet_->clean(multinet_model_data_);
166:     }
167:     else if (mn_state == ESP_MN_STATE_TIMEOUT)
168:     {
169:         ESP_LOGD(TAG, "Command word detection timeout, cleaning state");
170:         multinet_->clean(multinet_model_data_);
171:     }
172: }
173: 
174: size_t CustomWakeWord::GetFeedSize()
175: {
176:     if (multinet_model_data_ == nullptr)
177:     {
178:         return 0;
179:     }
180:     return multinet_->get_samp_chunksize(multinet_model_data_);
181: }

183: void CustomWakeWord::StoreWakeWordData(const std::vector<int16_t> &data)
184: {
185:     // store audio data to wake_word_pcm_
186:     wake_word_pcm_.push_back(data);
187:     // keep about 2 seconds of data, detect duration is 30ms (sample_rate == 16000, chunksize == 512)
188:     while (wake_word_pcm_.size() > 2000 / 30)
189:     {
190:         wake_word_pcm_.pop_front();
191:     }
192: }
193: 
194: void CustomWakeWord::EncodeWakeWordData()
195: {
196:     const size_t stack_size = 4096 * 7;
197:     wake_word_opus_.clear();
198:     if (wake_word_encode_task_stack_ == nullptr)
199:     {
200:         wake_word_encode_task_stack_ = (StackType_t *)heap_caps_malloc(stack_size, MALLOC_CAP_SPIRAM);
201:         assert(wake_word_encode_task_stack_ != nullptr);
202:     }
203:     if (wake_word_encode_task_buffer_ == nullptr)
204:     {
205:         wake_word_encode_task_buffer_ = (StaticTask_t *)heap_caps_malloc(sizeof(StaticTask_t), MALLOC_CAP_INTERNAL);
206:         assert(wake_word_encode_task_buffer_ != nullptr);
207:     }
208: 
209:     wake_word_encode_task_ = xTaskCreateStatic([](void *arg)
210:                                                {
211:         auto this_ = (CustomWakeWord*)arg;
212:         {
213:             auto start_time = esp_timer_get_time();
214:             auto encoder = std::make_unique<OpusEncoderWrapper>(16000, 1, OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS);
215:             encoder->SetComplexity(0); // 0 is the fastest
216: 
217:             int packets = 0;
218:             for (auto& pcm: this_->wake_word_pcm_) {
219:                 encoder->Encode(std::move(pcm), [this_](std::vector<uint8_t>&& opus) {
220:                     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(this_->wake_word_mutex_);
221:                     this_->wake_word_opus_.emplace_back(std::move(opus));
222:                     this_->wake_word_cv_.notify_all();
223:                 });
224:                 packets++;
225:             }
226:             this_->wake_word_pcm_.clear();
227: 
228:             auto end_time = esp_timer_get_time();
229:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Encode wake word opus %d packets in %ld ms", packets, (long)((end_time - start_time) / 1000));
230: 
231:             std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(this_->wake_word_mutex_);
232:             this_->wake_word_opus_.push_back(std::vector<uint8_t>());
233:             this_->wake_word_cv_.notify_all();
234:         }
235:         vTaskDelete(NULL); }, "encode_wake_word", stack_size, this, 2, wake_word_encode_task_stack_, wake_word_encode_task_buffer_);
236: }
237: 
238: bool CustomWakeWord::GetWakeWordOpus(std::vector<uint8_t> &opus)
239: {
240:     std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(wake_word_mutex_);
241:     wake_word_cv_.wait(lock, [this]()
242:                        { return !wake_word_opus_.empty(); });
243:     opus.swap(wake_word_opus_.front());
244:     wake_word_opus_.pop_front();
245:     return !opus.empty();
246: }
247:

The `CustomWakeWord::ParseWakenetModelConfig` function reads and parses the `index.json` configuration file to extract wake word detection settings, including language, duration, threshold, and command definitions. It populates internal variables with these settings and logs each parsed command for debugging purposes. This enables the system to configure and respond to specific voice commands based on the model's defined parameters.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The core purpose of both functions is the same: to parse a JSON configuration file named "index.json" and extract fields such as language, duration, threshold, and commands.
- However, there are significant semantic differences:
  - The ground truth uses `Assets::GetInstance()` to access asset data in-memory, while the generated code reads the file from disk using `fopen`, `fread`, etc.
  - Field names differ between implementations: ground truth expects nested keys under `"multinet_model"` and uses field names like `"command"`, `"text"`, and `"action"` within each command object, whereas the generated code looks for top-level keys (`"language"`, `"duration"`, etc.) and expects command objects with fields `"name"`, `"display"`, and `"action"`.
  - These mismatches indicate that although both codes attempt to achieve similar goals, they do so assuming different input formats and mechanisms, leading to incorrect behavior when used interchangeably.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches exactly.
- Both use cJSON library for parsing JSON content.
- General flow (read -> parse -> extract values -> store) is preserved.
- Major structural differences include:
  - File reading method differs significantly — one uses an asset manager abstraction, the other uses standard C file I/O.
  - Memory management strategy differs — manual allocation/free vs assumed managed assets.
  - JSON traversal logic differs in terms of expected hierarchy and key names.
  - Logging messages vary slightly but follow a consistent pattern.

Functional Completeness:
- Core functionality to read and parse index.json exists in both versions.
- Extraction of basic config parameters (language, duration, threshold) is present in both.
- Command array parsing is implemented in both, including iteration over elements and storing structured data.
- Missing or mismatched elements:
  - The generated code does not handle the nested `"multinet_model"` object which is central in the ground truth's structure.
  - It incorrectly assumes flat JSON structure instead of nested one.
  - Key naming inconsistencies prevent full compatibility (e.g., `"command"/"text"` vs `"name"/"display"`).
  - No error handling around `esp_srmodel_get_file_path` beyond null check, though this may be acceptable depending on API contract.
Overall, while many building blocks exist, critical parts of the intended functionality would fail due to structural assumptions about the JSON schema and resource loading mechanism.

The `CustomWakeWord::Initialize` function sets up the wake word detection system by configuring the audio codec, loading voice recognition models, and registering custom voice commands. It initializes the multinet model for speech command recognition based on the specified language and threshold settings. The function returns true if initialization succeeds, otherwise false.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
The overall logic and control flow of the generated code closely resemble that of the ground truth. Both versions handle initialization of `codec_`, clearing of commands, conditional handling of `models_list`, fallback logic for language-specific multinet models, and error logging. However, there are semantic discrepancies such as incorrect parameter usage in `multinet_->create()` where the ground truth passes both `mn_name_` and `duration_`, while the generated version only passes a hardcoded `0`. This can lead to different runtime behavior or failure.

Structure Similarity:
The structural similarity is high. Function signature, variable names, conditional blocks, API call sequences (e.g., `esp_srmodel_filter`, `esp_mn_handle_from_name`) match almost exactly. Control structures like if-else branches and loops also align well. Minor formatting differences (e.g., brace placement) do not affect structural integrity.

Functional Completeness:
The generated code omits several important functional components present in the ground truth:
- It does not set detection threshold using `multinet_->set_det_threshold()`.
- It skips registration and updating of speech commands via `esp_mn_commands_add()` and `esp_mn_commands_update()`.
- It lacks printing active speech commands with `multinet_->print_active_speech_commands()`.
These omissions significantly reduce its completeness in replicating full functionality.

results.description: The `CustomWakeWord::Feed` function processes audio data to detect predefined wake words using a multinet model. It handles both mono and stereo input by extracting the left channel if necessary, stores the processed audio data, and runs the wake word detection algorithm. Upon successful detection, it triggers a callback with the identified wake word and stops further processing until reactivated.

64:         cJSON *duration = cJSON_GetObjectItem(multinet_model, "duration");
65:         cJSON *threshold = cJSON_GetObjectItem(multinet_model, "threshold");

69:             language_ = language->valuestring;

73:             duration_ = duration->valueint;
74:         }
75:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(threshold))
76:         {
77:             threshold_ = threshold->valuedouble;
78:         }
79:         if (cJSON_IsArray(commands))
80:         {
81:             for (int i = 0; i < cJSON_GetArraySize(commands); i++)
82:             {

84:                 if (cJSON_IsObject(command))

194: void CustomWakeWord::EncodeWakeWordData()
195: {
196:     const size_t stack_size = 4096 * 7;
197:     wake_word_opus_.clear();
198:     if (wake_word_encode_task_stack_ == nullptr)
199:     {
200:         wake_word_encode_task_stack_ = (StackType_t *)heap_caps_malloc(stack_size, MALLOC_CAP_SPIRAM);
201:         assert(wake_word_encode_task_stack_ != nullptr);
202:     }
203:     if (wake_word_encode_task_buffer_ == nullptr)
204:     {
205:         wake_word_encode_task_buffer_ = (StaticTask_t *)heap_caps_malloc(sizeof(StaticTask_t), MALLOC_CAP_INTERNAL);
206:         assert(wake_word_encode_task_buffer_ != nullptr);
207:     }
208: 
209:     wake_word_encode_task_ = xTaskCreateStatic([](void *arg)
210:                                                {
211:         auto this_ = (CustomWakeWord*)arg;
212:         {
213:             auto start_time = esp_timer_get_time();
214:             auto encoder = std::make_unique<OpusEncoderWrapper>(16000, 1, OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS);
215:             encoder->SetComplexity(0); // 0 is the fastest
216: 
217:             int packets = 0;
218:             for (auto& pcm: this_->wake_word_pcm_) {
219:                 encoder->Encode(std::move(pcm), [this_](std::vector<uint8_t>&& opus) {
220:                     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(this_->wake_word_mutex_);
221:                     this_->wake_word_opus_.emplace_back(std::move(opus));
222:                     this_->wake_word_cv_.notify_all();
223:                 });
224:                 packets++;
225:             }
226:             this_->wake_word_pcm_.clear();
227: 
228:             auto end_time = esp_timer_get_time();
229:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Encode wake word opus %d packets in %ld ms", packets, (long)((end_time - start_time) / 1000));
230: 
231:             std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(this_->wake_word_mutex_);
232:             this_->wake_word_opus_.push_back(std::vector<uint8_t>());
233:             this_->wake_word_cv_.notify_all();
234:         }
235:         vTaskDelete(NULL); }, "encode_wake_word", stack_size, this, 2, wake_word_encode_task_stack_, wake_word_encode_task_buffer_);
236: }
237: 
238: bool CustomWakeWord::GetWakeWordOpus(std::vector<uint8_t> &opus)
239: {
240:     std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(wake_word_mutex_);
241:     wake_word_cv_.wait(lock, [this]()
242:                        { return !wake_word_opus_.empty(); });
243:     opus.swap(wake_word_opus_.front());
244:     wake_word_opus_.pop_front();
245:     return !opus.empty();
246: }
247: 
</file>
The `CustomWakeWord::Feed` function processes audio data to detect predefined wake words using a multinet model. It handles both mono and stereo input by extracting the left channel if necessary, stores the processed audio data, and runs the wake word detection algorithm. Upon successful detection, it triggers a callback with the identified wake word and stops further processing until reactivated.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to implement wake word detection but misses critical logic from the ground truth.
- It incorrectly assumes stereo based on even-sized data rather than checking codec input channels.
- It does not handle different detection states (ESP_MN_STATE_DETECTING, ESP_MN_STATE_DETECTED, ESP_MN_STATE_TIMEOUT).
- The result interpretation is oversimplified (using integer result > 0 instead of proper state machine).
- Missing command ID mapping and action filtering ("wake" command check).

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches correctly.
- Control flow diverges significantly due to missing state handling and incorrect stereo conversion logic.
- API calls differ: uses get_command_name() instead of get_results(), and doesn't call clean().
- Early return conditions are reordered but semantically similar.

Functional Completeness:
- Lacks proper multinet state handling which is central to the original functionality.
- Misses logging of detection results with detailed information (command_id, probability).
- No support for multiple command results processing.
- Missing timeout state cleanup.
- Incorrect assumption about stereo data leads to potential incorrect behavior.
- Callback invocation lacks context (doesn't set last_detected_wake_word_ properly).

170: void CustomWakeWord::Feed(const std::vector<int16_t> &data)
171: {
172:     if (multinet_model_data_ == nullptr || !running_)
173:     {
174:         return;
175:     }
176: 
177:     esp_mn_state_t mn_state;
178:     // If input channels is 2, we need to fetch the left channel data
179:     if (codec_->input_channels() == 2)
180:     {
181:         auto mono_data = std::vector<int16_t>(data.size() / 2);
182:         for (size_t i = 0, j = 0; i < mono_data.size(); ++i, j += 2)
183:         {
184:             mono_data[i] = data[j];
185:         }
186: 
187:         StoreWakeWordData(mono_data);
188:         mn_state = multinet_->detect(multinet_model_data_, const_cast<int16_t *>(mono_data.data()));
189:     }
190:     else
191:     {
192:         StoreWakeWordData(data);
193:         mn_state = multinet_->detect(multinet_model_data_, const_cast<int16_t *>(data.data()));
194:     }
195: 
196:     if (mn_state == ESP_MN_STATE_DETECTING)
197:     {
198:         return;
199:     }
200:     else if (mn_state == ESP_MN_STATE_DETECTED)
201:     {
202:         esp_mn_results_t *mn_result = multinet_->get_results(multinet_model_data_);
203:         for (int i = 0; i < mn_result->num && running_; i++)
204:         {
205:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Custom wake word detected: command_id=%d, string=%s, prob=%f",
206:                      mn_result->command_id[i], mn_result->string, mn_result->prob[i]);
207:             auto &command = commands_[mn_result->command_id[i] - 1];
208:             if (command.action == "wake")
209:             {
210:                 last_detected_wake_word_ = command.text;
211:                 running_ = false;
212: 
213:                 if (wake_word_detected_callback_)
214:                 {
215:                     wake_word_detected_callback_(last_detected_wake_word_);
216:                 }
217:             }
218:         }
219:         multinet_->clean(multinet_model_data_);
220:     }
221:     else if (mn_state == ESP_MN_STATE_TIMEOUT)
222:     {
223:         ESP_LOGD(TAG, "Command word detection timeout, cleaning state");
224:         multinet_->clean(multinet_model_data_);
225:     }
226: }

The `EncodeWakeWordData` function initiates a background task to encode stored PCM audio data into Opus format for efficient transmission. It manages memory allocation for the task stack and buffer, then processes each PCM segment using an Opus encoder with low complexity settings. Upon completion, it signals the availability of encoded data and appends a termination marker to indicate the end of the wake word audio stream.

variables: stack_size (missing, though CONFIG_ESP_MAIN_TASK_STACK_SIZE is used directly)
encoder (present but not managed same as reference)

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to perform the same high-level operation: encoding wake word PCM data into Opus format using a separate task/thread.
- However, there are significant differences in implementation that affect semantics:
  - Uses `xTaskCreatePinnedToCore` instead of `xTaskCreateStatic`, changing memory management and task creation behavior.
  - Implements a continuous processing loop with delays rather than a one-shot batch encoding process.
  - Manages PCM data through a queue-like pattern (`pop_front`) instead of iterating over a collection and clearing it afterward.
  - Uses raw libopus API directly instead of a wrapper class (`OpusEncoderWrapper`), missing complexity setting and callback-based encoding.
  - Termination logic differs significantly; uses a placeholder marker instead of an empty vector to signal completion.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches but internal structure diverges considerably.
- Stack and buffer allocation is attempted but with incorrect types and sizes compared to ground truth.
- Task creation method differs fundamentally (`xTaskCreatePinnedToCore` vs `xTaskCreateStatic`).
- Use of lambdas and capturing context differs between implementations.
- Memory allocation error handling exists but doesn't align with original assertions.
- Timing measurement for performance logging is omitted entirely in generated version.

Functional Completeness:
- Core functionality of encoding PCM to Opus is present, though implemented differently.
- Misses use of `OpusEncoderWrapper` which encapsulates configuration and callbacks.
- Lacks proper timing measurements and logging of packet count.
- Does not clear PCM data after processing as done in the ground truth.
- Incorrectly allocates `wake_word_encode_task_buffer_` as `uint8_t*` instead of `StaticTask_t*`.
- Misses mutex locking around opus data access outside the main loop.
- Fails to push back an empty vector as EOF indicator correctly.
- Priority level and core pinning differ from original implementation.

28:     {
29:         uint16_t data;
30:         int index = bit;
31:

38:             data = ReadReg(0x03);
39:             index -= 8;

41: 
42:         data = (data & ~(1 << index)) | (level << index);
43: 
44:         if (bit < 8)
45:         {
46:             WriteReg(0x02, data);
47:         }
48:         else
49:         {
50:             WriteReg(0x03, data);
51:         }
52:     }
