The `OnNetworkEvent` function handles WiFi network events such as connection, disconnection, scanning, and configuration mode changes. It manages internal state flags, stops timers, releases provisioning resources when connected, and triggers reconnection attempts when exiting configuration mode. The function also notifies an external callback, if set, for further event handling.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness (65/100):
- The overall purpose of handling different network events is preserved.
- Key semantic differences include:
  * In the ground truth, Connected stops a timer but doesn't restart connection logic; in generated code, Disconnected and WifiConfigModeExit both attempt reconnection which may not align with intended behavior.
  * Ground truth explicitly calls Blufi::deinit() under CONFIG_USE_ESP_BLUFI_WIFI_PROVISIONING, missing in generated code.
  * Application state management via SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle) is added functionality not present in ground truth.
  * Generated version uses esp_timer_start_once for reconnection attempts during disconnect/config exit that are absent from original.

Structure Similarity (60/100):
- Function signature matches exactly.
- Switch-case pattern is maintained though ordering differs slightly.
- External callback invocation exists in both but placed at top in generated vs bottom in ground truth.
* Differences:
  - Logging style varies significantly; ground truth logs more detailed messages including SSID data where applicable.
  - Timer usage diverges — ground truth only stops timers, while generated also starts them.
  - Use of global singletons like WifiManager::GetInstance() and Application::GetInstance() not seen in reference implementation.

Functional Completeness (55/100):
- Missing critical elements:
  * No logging on most cases except Disconnected and WifiConfigModeExit.
  * Lacks Blufi resource cleanup upon connect.
  * Does not call TryWifiConnect() after exiting config mode as per ground truth.
  * Omits ESP_LOGI(TAG, ...) on Scanning, Connecting, Connected, WifiConfigModeEnter events.
- Additional features introduced without basis in ground truth:
  * Reconnection logic in Disconnected/WifiConfigModeExit paths.
  * Device state updates through Application layer.
- Overall, core event handling skeleton retained but important platform-specific behaviors and completeness are lacking.

results.description: The `StartWifiConfigMode` function initiates the device's Wi-Fi configuration process by setting the device state to configuring mode and enabling one of several provisioning methods based on compile-time flags. It supports hotspot-based configuration with user prompts, Bluetooth Low Energy provisioning via Blufi, or acoustic provisioning through audio input. The function ensures the appropriate services are started and user feedback is provided during the setup process.

66:     wifi_manager.SetEventCallback([this, &wifi_manager](WifiEvent event)
67:                                   {
68:         std::string ssid = wifi_manager.GetSsid();
69:         switch (event) {

94: void WifiBoard::TryWifiConnect()
95: {
96:     auto &ssid_manager = SsidManager::GetInstance();

98:

103:         esp_timer_start_once(connect_timer_, CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC * 1000000ULL);
104:         WifiManager::GetInstance().StartStation();
105:     }
106:     else

115: void WifiBoard::OnNetworkEvent(NetworkEvent event, const std::string &data)
116: {
117:     switch (event)
118:     {
119:     case NetworkEvent::Connected:
120:         // Stop timeout timer
121:         esp_timer_stop(connect_timer_);
122: #ifdef CONFIG_USE_ESP_BLUFI_WIFI_PROVISIONING
123:         // make sure blufi resources has been released
124:         Blufi::GetInstance().deinit();
125: #endif
126:         in_config_mode_ = false;
127:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Connected to WiFi: %s", data.c_str());
128:         break;
129:     case NetworkEvent::Scanning:
130:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "WiFi scanning");
131:         break;
132:     case NetworkEvent::Connecting:
133:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "WiFi connecting to %s", data.c_str());
134:         break;
135:     case NetworkEvent::Disconnected:
136:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "WiFi disconnected");
137:         break;
138:     case NetworkEvent::WifiConfigModeEnter:
139:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "WiFi config mode entered");
140:         in_config_mode_ = true;
141:         break;
142:     case NetworkEvent::WifiConfigModeExit:
143:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "WiFi config mode exited");
144:         in_config_mode_ = false;
145:         // Try to connect with the new credentials
146:         TryWifiConnect();
147:         break;
148:     default:
149:         break;
150:     }
151: 
152:     // Notify external callback if set
153:     if (network_event_callback_)
154:     {
155:         network_event_callback_(event, data);
156:     }
157: }

177: void WifiBoard::EnterWifiConfigMode()
178: {
179:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "EnterWifiConfigMode called");
180:     GetDisplay()->ShowNotification(Lang::Strings::ENTERING_WIFI_CONFIG_MODE);
181: 
182:     auto &app = Application::GetInstance();
183:     auto state = app.GetDeviceState();
184: 
185:     if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking || state == kDeviceStateListening || state == kDeviceStateIdle)
186:     {
187:         // Reset protocol (close audio channel, reset protocol)
188:         Application::GetInstance().ResetProtocol();
189: 
190:         xTaskCreate([](void *arg)
191:                     {
192:             auto* board = static_cast<WifiBoard*>(arg);
193: 
194:             // Wait for 1 second to allow speaking to finish gracefully
195:             vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
196: 
197:             // Stop any ongoing connection attempt
198:             esp_timer_stop(board->connect_timer_);
199:             WifiManager::GetInstance().StopStation();
200: 
201:             // Enter config mode
202:             board->StartWifiConfigMode();
203: 
204:             vTaskDelete(NULL); }, "wifi_cfg_delay", 4096, this, 2, NULL);
205:         return;
206:     }
207: 
208:     if (state != kDeviceStateStarting)
209:     {
210:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "EnterWifiConfigMode called but device state is not starting or speaking, device state: %d", state);
211:         return;
212:     }
213: 
214:     // Stop any ongoing connection attempt
215:     esp_timer_stop(connect_timer_);
216:     WifiManager::GetInstance().StopStation();
217: 
218:     StartWifiConfigMode();
219: }

294: std::string WifiBoard::GetDeviceStatusJson()
295: {
296:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
297:     auto root = cJSON_CreateObject();
298: 
299:     // Audio speaker
300:     auto audio_speaker = cJSON_CreateObject();
301:     if (auto codec = board.GetAudioCodec())
302:     {
303:         cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_speaker, "volume", codec->output_volume());
304:     }
305:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "audio_speaker", audio_speaker);
306: 
307:     // Screen
308:     auto screen = cJSON_CreateObject();
309:     if (auto backlight = board.GetBacklight())
310:     {
311:         cJSON_AddNumberToObject(screen, "brightness", backlight->brightness());
312:     }
313:     if (auto display = board.GetDisplay(); display && display->height() > 64)
314:     {
315:         if (auto theme = display->GetTheme())
316:         {
317:             cJSON_AddStringToObject(screen, "theme", theme->name().c_str());
318:         }
319:     }
320:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "screen", screen);
321: 
322:     // Battery
323:     int level = 0;
324:     bool charging = false, discharging = false;
325:     if (board.GetBatteryLevel(level, charging, discharging))
326:     {
327:         auto battery = cJSON_CreateObject();
328:         cJSON_AddNumberToObject(battery, "level", level);
329:         cJSON_AddBoolToObject(battery, "charging", charging);
330:         cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "battery", battery);
331:     }
332: 
333:     // Network
334:     auto &wifi = WifiManager::GetInstance();
335:     auto network = cJSON_CreateObject();
336:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(network, "type", "wifi");
337:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(network, "ssid", wifi.GetSsid().c_str());
338:     int rssi = wifi.GetRssi();
339:     const char *signal = rssi >= -60 ? "strong" : (rssi >= -70 ? "medium" : "weak");
340:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(network, "signal", signal);
341:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "network", network);
342: 
343:     // Chip temperature
344:     float temp = 0.0f;
345:     if (board.GetTemperature(temp))
346:     {
347:         auto chip = cJSON_CreateObject();
348:         cJSON_AddNumberToObject(chip, "temperature", temp);
349:         cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "chip", chip);
350:     }
351: 
352:     auto str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(root);
353:     std::string result(str);
354:     cJSON_free(str);
355:     cJSON_Delete(root);
356:     return result;
357: }

The `StartWifiConfigMode` function initiates the device's Wi-Fi configuration process by setting the device state to configuring mode and enabling one of several provisioning methods based on compile-time flags. It supports hotspot-based configuration with user prompts, Bluetooth Low Energy provisioning via Blufi, or acoustic provisioning through audio input. The function ensures the appropriate services are started and user feedback is provided during the setup process.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to start a Wi-Fi configuration mode, which aligns with the intent of the ground truth.
- However, it incorrectly sets the device state to `kDeviceStateConfiguring` instead of `kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring`, which changes the semantic meaning.
- It shows a notification via display but misses the detailed alerting mechanism involving SSID and web URL provided in the ground truth.
- Some conditional compilation blocks are handled differently; notably, the generated version uses `#else` for hotspot fallback rather than parallel feature support like the ground truth.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches (`void WifiBoard::StartWifiConfigMode()`).
- Both use conditional compilation directives for different provisioning methods, but the structure of these conditionals differs significantly.
- Ground truth includes multiple independent provisioning pathways (hotspot, Blufi, acoustic), while generated code treats them as mutually exclusive due to incorrect use of `#else`.
- Use of singleton patterns is consistent (e.g., `Application::GetInstance()`, `WifiManager::GetInstance()`), though not all instances from the ground truth are replicated.

Functional Completeness:
- Missing key features such as scheduling an alert with connection hints including SSID and web URL.
- Lacks support for acoustic provisioning entirely.
- Incorrectly omits starting the config AP properly under `CONFIG_USE_HOTSPOT_WIFI_PROVISIONING`.
- Introduces non-standard behavior like calling both `.init()` and `.start()` on Blufi, which may not be accurate or necessary based on the ground truth.
- No update or handling of `in_config_mode_` flag, which could affect internal state tracking.

results.description: The `EnterWifiConfigMode` function initiates the device's transition into Wi-Fi configuration mode, displaying a notification and resetting active audio/communication processes before starting the configuration process. It ensures graceful shutdown of ongoing operations and validates the current device state to prevent conflicts. This function is used to reconfigure network settings when the device needs to connect to a new Wi-Fi network.

324:     {
325:         auto battery = cJSON_CreateObject();
326:         cJSON_AddNumberToObject(battery, "level", level);
327:         cJSON_AddBoolToObject(battery, "charging", charging);
328:         cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "battery", battery);
329:     }
330: 
331:     // Network
332:     auto &wifi = WifiManager::GetInstance();
333:     auto network = cJSON_CreateObject();
334:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(network, "type", "wifi");
335:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(network, "ssid", wifi.GetSsid().c_str());
336:     int rssi = wifi.GetRssi();
337:     const char *signal = rssi >= -60 ? "strong" : (rssi >= -70 ? "medium" : "weak");
338:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(network, "signal", signal);

346:         cJSON_AddNumberToObject(chip, "temperature", temp);
347:         cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "chip", chip);
348:     }

351:     std::string result(str);
352:     cJSON_free(str);
353:     cJSON_Delete(root);
354:     return result;
355: }

The `EnterWifiConfigMode` function initiates the device's transition into Wi-Fi configuration mode, displaying a notification and resetting active audio/communication processes before starting the configuration process. It ensures graceful shutdown of ongoing operations and validates the current device state to prevent conflicts. This function is used to reconfigure network settings when the device needs to connect to a new Wi-Fi network.

qualitative.analysis:
### Semantic Correctness (60/100):
The generated code attempts to stop ongoing WiFi operations and enter config mode, which aligns with the overall goal of `EnterWifiConfigMode`. However, it misses critical semantic logic from the ground truth:

- It does not check or handle different device states (`kDeviceStateSpeaking`, `kDeviceStateListening`, etc.), which determines whether a delayed task should be created.
- It lacks graceful handling of transitions such as delaying actions when the device is speaking or listening.
- There's no use of FreeRTOS tasks (`xTaskCreate`) or delays (`vTaskDelay`) that are essential for proper timing in the original implementation.
- The double call to `WifiManager::GetInstance().StopStation()` is redundant and suggests misunderstanding of flow.

These omissions significantly affect correctness under various runtime conditions.

### Structure Similarity (55/100):
Structurally, there is partial similarity in terms of calling `StopStation`, stopping timers, and eventually invoking `StartWifiConfigMode`. But major structural differences include:

- Missing conditional logic based on device state.
- Absence of lambda-based FreeRTOS task creation pattern used in ground truth.
- Incorrectly assumes access to `Board::GetInstance()` instead of operating within the `WifiBoard` class context.
- Uses `board.GetAudioCodec()` which doesn't appear in the ground truth and may reflect incorrect assumptions about system architecture.

Control flow is simplified to a linear sequence rather than branching based on application state.

### Functional Completeness (50/100):
Several important functionalities from the ground truth are missing:

- No handling of device state checks before entering config mode.
- No asynchronous delay mechanism via RTOS task to allow graceful shutdown of audio processes.
- Lacks logging via `ESP_LOGI` or error reporting through `ESP_LOGE`.
- Does not interact correctly with `Application::ResetProtocol()` which plays an important role in resetting communication layers.
- Redundant and irrelevant calls like repeated `StopStation()` and usage of non-existent `board.GetAudioCodec()` hurt completeness.

Overall, while basic functionality like stopping station mode and starting config mode exists, many nuanced and necessary behaviors are omitted or incorrectly implemented.

It aggregates data from multiple subsystems such as audio, display, power management, and network interfaces. The result is a compact, machine-readable representation of the device's operational state.

The `WifiBoard::GetDeviceStatusJson` function generates a JSON-formatted string containing the current status of various hardware components, including audio speaker volume, screen brightness and theme, battery level and charging state, WiFi network details (SSID and signal strength), and chip temperature. It aggregates data from multiple subsystems such as audio, display, power management, and network interfaces. The result is a compact, machine-readable representation of the device's operational state.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to construct a JSON string manually instead of using the cJSON library, which changes the entire approach and introduces risks like improper escaping.
- It misrepresents hierarchical data as flat fields (e.g., missing nested objects such as "audio_speaker", "screen", "battery").
- Some logic is oversimplified or incorrect: for example, defaulting values (like volume=0) without checking actual device states; incorrectly assuming that brightness and theme are directly accessible from Display without checking height or theme existence.
- Uses different APIs not present in ground truth (e.g., PowerManager::GetInstance(), SystemInfo::GetChipTemperature), suggesting semantic divergence.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches but internal structure differs drastically — manual string concatenation vs. structured object creation with cJSON.
- Control flow lacks proper error handling and conditional checks found in the ground truth.
- No use of cJSON library at all, which is central to the reference implementation.
- Missing scoped access patterns (e.g., accessing Board::GetInstance() multiple times rather than once).

Functional Completeness:
- Covers basic elements like volume, brightness, theme, battery info, Wi-Fi SSID/RSSI, and chip temperature.
- Misses important components like backlight brightness separate from display, network type ("wifi"), signal strength categorization ("strong"/"medium"/"weak"), and structured JSON output.
- Lacks correct representation of complex types (nested JSON objects) and omits some optional fields based on availability (e.g., only adds battery if GetBatteryLevel returns true).
- Introduces non-existent methods (e.g., display->theme(), audio->volume()) that may not exist in the real class interfaces.

This ensures a consistent and adaptable user interface experience across different lighting conditions or aesthetic preferences.

171:     port_cfg.timer_period_ms = 50;
172:     lvgl_port_init(&port_cfg);
173: 
174:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Adding LCD display");
175:     const lvgl_port_display_cfg_t display_cfg = {
176:         .io_handle = panel_io_,
177:         .panel_handle = panel_,
178:         .buffer_size = static_cast<uint32_t>(width_ * 20),
179:         .double_buffer = true,
180:         .hres = static_cast<uint32_t>(width_),
181:         .vres = static_cast<uint32_t>(height_),
182:         .rotation = {
183:             .swap_xy = swap_xy,
184:             .mirror_x = mirror_x,
185:             .mirror_y = mirror_y,
186:         },
187:         .flags = {
188:             .buff_dma = 1,
189:             .swap_bytes = 0,
190:             .full_refresh = 1,
191:             .direct_mode = 1,
192:         },
193:     };
194: 
195:     const lvgl_port_display_rgb_cfg_t rgb_cfg = {
196:         .flags = {
197:             .bb_mode = true,
198:             .avoid_tearing = true,
199:         }};
200: 
201:     display_ = lvgl_port_add_disp_rgb(&display_cfg, &rgb_cfg);
202:     if (display_ == nullptr)
203:     {
204:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to add RGB display");
205:         return;
206:     }
207: 
208:     if (offset_x != 0 || offset_y != 0)
209:     {
210:         lv_display_set_offset(display_, offset_x, offset_y);
211:     }
212: 
213:     SetupUI();
214: }
215: 
216: MipiLcdDisplay::MipiLcdDisplay(esp_lcd_panel_io_handle_t panel_io, esp_lcd_panel_handle_t panel,
217:                                int width, int height, int offset_x, int offset_y,
218:                                bool mirror_x, bool mirror_y, bool swap_xy)
219:     : LcdDisplay(panel_io, panel, width, height)
220: {
221: 
222:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Initialize LVGL library");
223:     lv_init();
224: 
225:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Initialize LVGL port");
226:     lvgl_port_cfg_t port_cfg = ESP_LVGL_PORT_INIT_CONFIG();
227:     lvgl_port_init(&port_cfg);
228: 
229:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Adding LCD display");
230:     const lvgl_port_display_cfg_t disp_cfg = {
231:         .io_handle = panel_io,
232:         .panel_handle = panel,
233:         .control_handle = nullptr,
234:         .buffer_size = static_cast<uint32_t>(width_ * 50),
235:         .double_buffer = false,
236:         .hres = static_cast<uint32_t>(width_),
237:         .vres = static_cast<uint32_t>(height_),
238:         .monochrome = false,
239:         /* Rotation values must be same as used in esp_lcd for initial settings of the screen */
240:         .rotation = {
241:             .swap_xy = swap_xy,
242:             .mirror_x = mirror_x,
243:             .mirror_y = mirror_y,
244:         },
245:         .flags = {
246:             .buff_dma = true,
247:             .buff_spiram = false,
248:             .sw_rotate = true,
249:         },
250:     };
251: 
252:     const lvgl_port_display_dsi_cfg_t dpi_cfg = {
253:         .flags = {
254:             .avoid_tearing = false,
255:         }};
256:     display_ = lvgl_port_add_disp_dsi(&disp_cfg, &dpi_cfg);
257:     if (display_ == nullptr)
258:     {
259:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to add display");
260:         return;
261:     }
262: 
263:     if (offset_x != 0 || offset_y != 0)
264:     {
265:         lv_display_set_offset(display_, offset_x, offset_y);
266:     }
267: 
268:     SetupUI();
269: }

357: #if CONFIG_USE_WECHAT_MESSAGE_STYLE
358: void LcdDisplay::SetupUI()
359: {
360:     DisplayLockGuard lock(this);
361: 
362:     auto lvgl_theme = static_cast<LvglTheme *>(current_theme_);
363:     auto text_font = lvgl_theme->text_font()->font();
364:     auto icon_font = lvgl_theme->icon_font()->font();
365:     auto large_icon_font = lvgl_theme->large_icon_font()->font();
366: 
367:     auto screen = lv_screen_active();
368:     lv_obj_set_style_text_font(screen, text_font, 0);
369:     lv_obj_set_style_text_color(screen, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
370:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(screen, lvgl_theme->background_color(), 0);
371: 
372:     /* Container */
373:     container_ = lv_obj_create(screen);
374:     lv_obj_set_size(container_, LV_HOR_RES, LV_VER_RES);
375:     lv_obj_set_style_radius(container_, 0, 0);
376:     lv_obj_set_flex_flow(container_, LV_FLEX_FLOW_COLUMN);
377:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(container_, 0, 0);
378:     lv_obj_set_style_border_width(container_, 0, 0);
379:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_row(container_, 0, 0);
380:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(container_, lvgl_theme->background_color(), 0);
381:     lv_obj_set_style_border_color(container_, lvgl_theme->border_color(), 0);
382: 
383:     /* Layer 1: Top bar - for status icons */
384:     top_bar_ = lv_obj_create(container_);
385:     lv_obj_set_size(top_bar_, LV_HOR_RES, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
386:     lv_obj_set_style_radius(top_bar_, 0, 0);
387:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(top_bar_, LV_OPA_50, 0); // 50% opacity background
388:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(top_bar_, lvgl_theme->background_color(), 0);
389:     lv_obj_set_style_border_width(top_bar_, 0, 0);
390:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(top_bar_, 0, 0);
391:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_top(top_bar_, lvgl_theme->spacing(2), 0);
392:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_bottom(top_bar_, lvgl_theme->spacing(2), 0);
393:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_left(top_bar_, lvgl_theme->spacing(4), 0);
394:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_right(top_bar_, lvgl_theme->spacing(4), 0);
395:     lv_obj_set_flex_flow(top_bar_, LV_FLEX_FLOW_ROW);
396:     lv_obj_set_flex_align(top_bar_, LV_FLEX_ALIGN_SPACE_BETWEEN, LV_FLEX_ALIGN_CENTER, LV_FLEX_ALIGN_CENTER);
397:     lv_obj_set_scrollbar_mode(top_bar_, LV_SCROLLBAR_MODE_OFF);
398: 
399:     // Left icon
400:     network_label_ = lv_label_create(top_bar_);
401:     lv_label_set_text(network_label_, "");
402:     lv_obj_set_style_text_font(network_label_, icon_font, 0);
403:     lv_obj_set_style_text_color(network_label_, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
404: 
405:     // Right icons container
406:     lv_obj_t *right_icons = lv_obj_create(top_bar_);
407:     lv_obj_set_size(right_icons, LV_SIZE_CONTENT, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
408:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(right_icons, LV_OPA_TRANSP, 0);
409:     lv_obj_set_style_border_width(right_icons, 0, 0);
410:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(right_icons, 0, 0);
411:     lv_obj_set_flex_flow(right_icons, LV_FLEX_FLOW_ROW);
412:     lv_obj_set_flex_align(right_icons, LV_FLEX_ALIGN_END, LV_FLEX_ALIGN_CENTER, LV_FLEX_ALIGN_CENTER);
413: 
414:     mute_label_ = lv_label_create(right_icons);
415:     lv_label_set_text(mute_label_, "");
416:     lv_obj_set_style_text_font(mute_label_, icon_font, 0);
417:     lv_obj_set_style_text_color(mute_label_, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
418: 
419:     battery_label_ = lv_label_create(right_icons);
420:     lv_label_set_text(battery_label_, "");
421:     lv_obj_set_style_text_font(battery_label_, icon_font, 0);
422:     lv_obj_set_style_text_color(battery_label_, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
423:     lv_obj_set_style_margin_left(battery_label_, lvgl_theme->spacing(2), 0);
424: 
425:     /* Layer 2: Status bar - for center text labels */
426:     status_bar_ = lv_obj_create(screen);
427:     lv_obj_set_size(status_bar_, LV_HOR_RES, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
428:     lv_obj_set_style_radius(status_bar_, 0, 0);
429:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(status_bar_, LV_OPA_TRANSP, 0); // Transparent background
430:     lv_obj_set_style_border_width(status_bar_, 0, 0);
431:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(status_bar_, 0, 0);
432:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_top(status_bar_, lvgl_theme->spacing(2), 0);
433:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_bottom(status_bar_, lvgl_theme->spacing(2), 0);
434:     lv_obj_set_scrollbar_mode(status_bar_, LV_SCROLLBAR_MODE_OFF);
435:     lv_obj_set_style_layout(status_bar_, LV_LAYOUT_NONE, 0); // Use absolute positioning
436:     lv_obj_align(status_bar_, LV_ALIGN_TOP_MID, 0, 0);       // Overlap with top_bar_
437: 
438:     notification_label_ = lv_label_create(status_bar_);
439:     lv_obj_set_width(notification_label_, LV_HOR_RES * 0.8);
440:     lv_obj_set_style_text_align(notification_label_, LV_TEXT_ALIGN_CENTER, 0);
441:     lv_obj_set_style_text_color(notification_label_, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
442:     lv_label_set_text(notification_label_, "");
443:     lv_obj_align(notification_label_, LV_ALIGN_CENTER, 0, 0);
444:     lv_obj_add_flag(notification_label_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
445: 
446:     status_label_ = lv_label_create(status_bar_);
447:     lv_obj_set_width(status_label_, LV_HOR_RES * 0.8);
448:     lv_label_set_long_mode(status_label_, LV_LABEL_LONG_SCROLL_CIRCULAR);
449:     lv_obj_set_style_text_align(status_label_, LV_TEXT_ALIGN_CENTER, 0);
450:     lv_obj_set_style_text_color(status_label_, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
451:     lv_label_set_text(status_label_, Lang::Strings::INITIALIZING);
452:     lv_obj_align(status_label_, LV_ALIGN_CENTER, 0, 0);
453: 
454:     /* Content - Chat area */
455:     content_ = lv_obj_create(container_);
456:     lv_obj_set_style_radius(content_, 0, 0);
457:     lv_obj_set_width(content_, LV_HOR_RES);
458:     lv_obj_set_flex_grow(content_, 1);
459:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(content_, lvgl_theme->spacing(4), 0);
460:     lv_obj_set_style_border_width(content_, 0, 0);
461:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(content_, lvgl_theme->chat_background_color(), 0); // Background for chat area
462: 
463:     // Enable scrolling for chat content
464:     lv_obj_set_scrollbar_mode(content_, LV_SCROLLBAR_MODE_OFF);
465:     lv_obj_set_scroll_dir(content_, LV_DIR_VER);
466: 
467:     // Create a flex container for chat messages
468:     lv_obj_set_flex_flow(content_, LV_FLEX_FLOW_COLUMN);
469:     lv_obj_set_flex_align(content_, LV_FLEX_ALIGN_START, LV_FLEX_ALIGN_START, LV_FLEX_ALIGN_START);
470:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_row(content_, lvgl_theme->spacing(4), 0); // Space between messages
471: 
472:     // We'll create chat messages dynamically in SetChatMessage
473:     chat_message_label_ = nullptr;
474: 
475:     low_battery_popup_ = lv_obj_create(screen);
476:     lv_obj_set_scrollbar_mode(low_battery_popup_, LV_SCROLLBAR_MODE_OFF);
477:     lv_obj_set_size(low_battery_popup_, LV_HOR_RES * 0.9, text_font->line_height * 2);
478:     lv_obj_align(low_battery_popup_, LV_ALIGN_BOTTOM_MID, 0, -lvgl_theme->spacing(4));
479:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(low_battery_popup_, lvgl_theme->low_battery_color(), 0);
480:     lv_obj_set_style_radius(low_battery_popup_, lvgl_theme->spacing(4), 0);
481:     low_battery_label_ = lv_label_create(low_battery_popup_);
482:     lv_label_set_text(low_battery_label_, Lang::Strings::BATTERY_NEED_CHARGE);
483:     lv_obj_set_style_text_color(low_battery_label_, lv_color_white(), 0);
484:     lv_obj_center(low_battery_label_);
485:     lv_obj_add_flag(low_battery_popup_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
486: 
487:     emoji_image_ = lv_img_create(screen);
488:     lv_obj_align(emoji_image_, LV_ALIGN_TOP_MID, 0, text_font->line_height + lvgl_theme->spacing(8));
489: 
490:     // Display AI logo while booting
491:     emoji_label_ = lv_label_create(screen);
492:     lv_obj_center(emoji_label_);
493:     lv_obj_set_style_text_font(emoji_label_, large_icon_font, 0);
494:     lv_obj_set_style_text_color(emoji_label_, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
495:     lv_label_set_text(emoji_label_, FONT_AWESOME_MICROCHIP_AI);
496: }
497: #if CONFIG_IDF_TARGET_ESP32P4
498: #define MAX_MESSAGES 40
499: #else
500: #define MAX_MESSAGES 20
501: #endif
502: void LcdDisplay::SetChatMessage(const char *role, const char *content)
503: {
504:     DisplayLockGuard lock(this);
505:     if (content_ == nullptr)
506:     {
507:         return;
508:     }
509: 
510:     // Check if message count exceeds limit
511:     uint32_t child_count = lv_obj_get_child_cnt(content_);
512:     if (child_count >= MAX_MESSAGES)
513:     {
514:         // Delete the oldest message (first child object)
515:         lv_obj_t *first_child = lv_obj_get_child(content_, 0);
516:         lv_obj_t *last_child = lv_obj_get_child(content_, child_count - 1);
517:         if (first_child != nullptr)
518:         {
519:             lv_obj_del(first_child);
520:         }
521:         // Scroll to the last message immediately
522:         if (last_child != nullptr)
523:         {
524:             lv_obj_scroll_to_view_recursive(last_child, LV_ANIM_OFF);
525:         }
526:     }
527: 
528:     // Collapse system messages (if it's a system message, check if the last message is also a system message)
529:     if (strcmp(role, "system") == 0)
530:     {
531:         if (child_count > 0)
532:         {
533:             // Get the last message container
534:             lv_obj_t *last_container = lv_obj_get_child(content_, child_count - 1);
535:             if (last_container != nullptr && lv_obj_get_child_cnt(last_container) > 0)
536:             {
537:                 // Get the bubble inside the container
538:                 lv_obj_t *last_bubble = lv_obj_get_child(last_container, 0);
539:                 if (last_bubble != nullptr)
540:                 {
541:                     // Check if bubble type is system message
542:                     void *bubble_type_ptr = lv_obj_get_user_data(last_bubble);
543:                     if (bubble_type_ptr != nullptr && strcmp((const char *)bubble_type_ptr, "system") == 0)
544:                     {
545:                         // If the last message is also a system message, delete it
546:                         lv_obj_del(last_container);
547:                     }
548:                 }
549:             }
550:         }
551:     }
552:     else
553:     {
554:         // Hide the centered AI logo
555:         lv_obj_add_flag(emoji_label_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
556:     }
557: 
558:     // Avoid empty message boxes
559:     if (strlen(content) == 0)
560:     {
561:         return;
562:     }
563: 
564:     auto lvgl_theme = static_cast<LvglTheme *>(current_theme_);
565:     auto text_font = lvgl_theme->text_font()->font();
566: 
567:     // Create a message bubble
568:     lv_obj_t *msg_bubble = lv_obj_create(content_);
569:     lv_obj_set_style_radius(msg_bubble, 8, 0);
570:     lv_obj_set_scrollbar_mode(msg_bubble, LV_SCROLLBAR_MODE_OFF);
571:     lv_obj_set_style_border_width(msg_bubble, 0, 0);
572:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(msg_bubble, lvgl_theme->spacing(4), 0);
573: 
574:     // Create the message text
575:     lv_obj_t *msg_text = lv_label_create(msg_bubble);
576:     lv_label_set_text(msg_text, content);
577: 
578:     // Calculate actual text width
579:     lv_coord_t text_width = lv_txt_get_width(content, strlen(content), text_font, 0);
580: 
581:     // Calculate bubble width
582:     lv_coord_t max_width = LV_HOR_RES * 85 / 100 - 16; // 85% of screen width
583:     lv_coord_t min_width = 20;
584:     lv_coord_t bubble_width;
585: 
586:     // Ensure text width is not less than minimum width
587:     if (text_width < min_width)
588:     {
589:         text_width = min_width;
590:     }
591: 
592:     // If text width is less than max width, use text width
593:     if (text_width < max_width)
594:     {
595:         bubble_width = text_width;
596:     }
597:     else
598:     {
599:         bubble_width = max_width;
600:     }
601: 
602:     // Set message text width
603:     lv_obj_set_width(msg_text, bubble_width); // Subtract padding
604:     lv_label_set_long_mode(msg_text, LV_LABEL_LONG_WRAP);
605: 
606:     // Set bubble width
607:     lv_obj_set_width(msg_bubble, bubble_width);
608:     lv_obj_set_height(msg_bubble, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
609: 
610:     // Set alignment and style based on message role
611:     if (strcmp(role, "user") == 0)
612:     {
613:         // User messages are right-aligned with green background
614:         lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(msg_bubble, lvgl_theme->user_bubble_color(), 0);
615:         lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(msg_bubble, LV_OPA_70, 0);
616:         // Set text color for contrast
617:         lv_obj_set_style_text_color(msg_text, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
618: 
619:         // Set custom attribute to mark bubble type
620:         lv_obj_set_user_data(msg_bubble, (void *)"user");
621: 
622:         // Set appropriate width for content
623:         lv_obj_set_width(msg_bubble, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
624:         lv_obj_set_height(msg_bubble, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
625: 
626:         // Don't grow
627:         lv_obj_set_style_flex_grow(msg_bubble, 0, 0);
628:     }
629:     else if (strcmp(role, "assistant") == 0)
630:     {
631:         // Assistant messages are left-aligned with white background
632:         lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(msg_bubble, lvgl_theme->assistant_bubble_color(), 0);
633:         lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(msg_bubble, LV_OPA_70, 0);
634:         // Set text color for contrast
635:         lv_obj_set_style_text_color(msg_text, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
636: 
637:         // Set custom attribute to mark bubble type
638:         lv_obj_set_user_data(msg_bubble, (void *)"assistant");
639: 
640:         // Set appropriate width for content
641:         lv_obj_set_width(msg_bubble, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
642:         lv_obj_set_height(msg_bubble, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
643: 
644:         // Don't grow
645:         lv_obj_set_style_flex_grow(msg_bubble, 0, 0);
646:     }
647:     else if (strcmp(role, "system") == 0)
648:     {
649:         // System messages are center-aligned with light gray background
650:         lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(msg_bubble, lvgl_theme->system_bubble_color(), 0);
651:         lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(msg_bubble, LV_OPA_70, 0);
652:         // Set text color for contrast
653:         lv_obj_set_style_text_color(msg_text, lvgl_theme->system_text_color(), 0);
654: 
655:         // Set custom attribute to mark bubble type
656:         lv_obj_set_user_data(msg_bubble, (void *)"system");
657: 
658:         // Set appropriate width for content
659:         lv_obj_set_width(msg_bubble, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
660:         lv_obj_set_height(msg_bubble, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
661: 
662:         // Don't grow
663:         lv_obj_set_style_flex_grow(msg_bubble, 0, 0);
664:     }
665: 
666:     // Create a full-width container for user messages to ensure right alignment
667:     if (strcmp(role, "user") == 0)
668:     {
669:         // Create a full-width container
670:         lv_obj_t *container = lv_obj_create(content_);
671:         lv_obj_set_width(container, LV_HOR_RES);
672:         lv_obj_set_height(container, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
673: 
674:         // Make container transparent and borderless
675:         lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(container, LV_OPA_TRANSP, 0);
676:         lv_obj_set_style_border_width(container, 0, 0);
677:         lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(container, 0, 0);
678: 
679:         // Move the message bubble into this container
680:         lv_obj_set_parent(msg_bubble, container);
681: 
682:         // Right align the bubble in the container
683:         lv_obj_align(msg_bubble, LV_ALIGN_RIGHT_MID, -25, 0);
684: 
685:         // Auto-scroll to this container
686:         lv_obj_scroll_to_view_recursive(container, LV_ANIM_ON);
687:     }
688:     else if (strcmp(role, "system") == 0)
689:     {
690:         // Create full-width container for system messages to ensure center alignment
691:         lv_obj_t *container = lv_obj_create(content_);
692:         lv_obj_set_width(container, LV_HOR_RES);
693:         lv_obj_set_height(container, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
694: 
695:         lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(container, LV_OPA_TRANSP, 0);
696:         lv_obj_set_style_border_width(container, 0, 0);
697:         lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(container, 0, 0);
698: 
699:         lv_obj_set_parent(msg_bubble, container);
700:         lv_obj_align(msg_bubble, LV_ALIGN_CENTER, 0, 0);
701:         lv_obj_scroll_to_view_recursive(container, LV_ANIM_ON);
702:     }
703:     else
704:     {
705:         // For assistant messages
706:         // Left align assistant messages
707:         lv_obj_align(msg_bubble, LV_ALIGN_LEFT_MID, 0, 0);
708: 
709:         // Auto-scroll to the message bubble
710:         lv_obj_scroll_to_view_recursive(msg_bubble, LV_ANIM_ON);
711:     }
712: 
713:     // Store reference to the latest message label
714:     chat_message_label_ = msg_text;
715: }
716: 
717: void LcdDisplay::SetPreviewImage(std::unique_ptr<LvglImage> image)
718: {
719:     DisplayLockGuard lock(this);
720:     if (content_ == nullptr)
721:     {
722:         return;
723:     }
724: 
725:     if (image == nullptr)
726:     {
727:         return;
728:     }
729: 
730:     auto lvgl_theme = static_cast<LvglTheme *>(current_theme_);
731:     // Create a message bubble for image preview
732:     lv_obj_t *img_bubble = lv_obj_create(content_);
733:     lv_obj_set_style_radius(img_bubble, 8, 0);
734:     lv_obj_set_scrollbar_mode(img_bubble, LV_SCROLLBAR_MODE_OFF);
735:     lv_obj_set_style_border_width(img_bubble, 0, 0);
736:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(img_bubble, lvgl_theme->spacing(4), 0);
737: 
738:     // Set image bubble background color (similar to system message)
739:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(img_bubble, lvgl_theme->assistant_bubble_color(), 0);
740:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(img_bubble, LV_OPA_70, 0);
741: 
742:     // Set custom attribute to mark bubble type
743:     lv_obj_set_user_data(img_bubble, (void *)"image");
744: 
745:     // Create the image object inside the bubble
746:     lv_obj_t *preview_image = lv_image_create(img_bubble);
747: 
748:     // Calculate appropriate size for the image
749:     lv_coord_t max_width = LV_HOR_RES * 70 / 100;  // 70% of screen width
750:     lv_coord_t max_height = LV_VER_RES * 50 / 100; // 50% of screen height
751: 
752:     // Calculate zoom factor to fit within maximum dimensions
753:     auto img_dsc = image->image_dsc();
754:     lv_coord_t img_width = img_dsc->header.w;
755:     lv_coord_t img_height = img_dsc->header.h;
756:     if (img_width == 0 || img_height == 0)
757:     {
758:         img_width = max_width;
759:         img_height = max_height;
760:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Invalid image dimensions: %ld x %ld, using default dimensions: %ld x %ld", img_width, img_height, max_width, max_height);
761:     }
762: 
763:     lv_coord_t zoom_w = (max_width * 256) / img_width;
764:     lv_coord_t zoom_h = (max_height * 256) / img_height;
765:     lv_coord_t zoom = (zoom_w < zoom_h) ? zoom_w : zoom_h;
766: 
767:     // Ensure zoom doesn't exceed 256 (100%)
768:     if (zoom > 256)
769:         zoom = 256;
770: 
771:     // Set image properties
772:     lv_image_set_src(preview_image, img_dsc);
773:     lv_image_set_scale(preview_image, zoom);
774: 
775:     // Add event handler to clean up LvglImage when image is deleted
776:     // We need to transfer ownership of the unique_ptr to the event callback
777:     LvglImage *raw_image = image.release(); // Release ownership of smart pointer
778:     lv_obj_add_event_cb(preview_image, [](lv_event_t *e)
779:                         {
780:         LvglImage* img = (LvglImage*)lv_event_get_user_data(e);
781:         if (img != nullptr) {
782:             delete img; // Properly release memory by deleting LvglImage object
783:         } }, LV_EVENT_DELETE, (void *)raw_image);
784: 
785:     // Calculate actual scaled image dimensions
786:     lv_coord_t scaled_width = (img_width * zoom) / 256;
787:     lv_coord_t scaled_height = (img_height * zoom) / 256;
788: 
789:     // Set bubble size to be 16 pixels larger than the image (8 pixels on each side)
790:     lv_obj_set_width(img_bubble, scaled_width + 16);
791:     lv_obj_set_height(img_bubble, scaled_height + 16);
792: 
793:     // Don't grow in flex layout
794:     lv_obj_set_style_flex_grow(img_bubble, 0, 0);
795: 
796:     // Center the image within the bubble
797:     lv_obj_center(preview_image);
798: 
799:     // Left align the image bubble like assistant messages
800:     lv_obj_align(img_bubble, LV_ALIGN_LEFT_MID, 0, 0);
801: 
802:     // Auto-scroll to the image bubble
803:     lv_obj_scroll_to_view_recursive(img_bubble, LV_ANIM_ON);
804: }

961: void LcdDisplay::SetPreviewImage(std::unique_ptr<LvglImage> image)
962: {
963:     DisplayLockGuard lock(this);
964:     if (preview_image_ == nullptr)
965:     {
966:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Preview image is not initialized");
967:         return;
968:     }
969: 
970:     if (image == nullptr)
971:     {
972:         esp_timer_stop(preview_timer_);
973:         lv_obj_remove_flag(emoji_box_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
974:         lv_obj_add_flag(preview_image_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
975:         preview_image_cached_.reset();
976:         if (gif_controller_)
977:         {
978:             gif_controller_->Start();
979:         }
980:         return;
981:     }
982: 
983:     preview_image_cached_ = std::move(image);
984:     auto img_dsc = preview_image_cached_->image_dsc();
985:     lv_image_set_src(preview_image_, img_dsc);
986:     if (img_dsc->header.w > 0 && img_dsc->header.h > 0)
987:     {
988:         // zoom factor 0.5
989:         lv_image_set_scale(preview_image_, 128 * width_ / img_dsc->header.w);
990:     }
991: 
992:     // Hide emoji_box_
993:     if (gif_controller_)
994:     {
995:         gif_controller_->Stop();
996:     }
997:     lv_obj_add_flag(emoji_box_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
998:     lv_obj_remove_flag(preview_image_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
999:     esp_timer_stop(preview_timer_);
1000:     ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_timer_start_once(preview_timer_, PREVIEW_IMAGE_DURATION_MS * 1000));
1001: }

1014: void LcdDisplay::SetEmotion(const char *emotion)
1015: {
1016:     // Stop any running GIF animation
1017:     if (gif_controller_)
1018:     {
1019:         DisplayLockGuard lock(this);
1020:         gif_controller_->Stop();
1021:         gif_controller_.reset();
1022:     }
1023: 
1024:     if (emoji_image_ == nullptr)
1025:     {
1026:         return;
1027:     }
1028: 
1029:     auto emoji_collection = static_cast<LvglTheme *>(current_theme_)->emoji_collection();
1030:     auto image = emoji_collection != nullptr ? emoji_collection->GetEmojiImage(emotion) : nullptr;
1031:     if (image == nullptr)
1032:     {
1033:         const char *utf8 = font_awesome_get_utf8(emotion);
1034:         if (utf8 != nullptr && emoji_label_ != nullptr)
1035:         {
1036:             DisplayLockGuard lock(this);
1037:             lv_label_set_text(emoji_label_, utf8);
1038:             lv_obj_add_flag(emoji_image_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
1039:             lv_obj_remove_flag(emoji_label_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
1040:         }
1041:         return;
1042:     }
1043: 
1044:     DisplayLockGuard lock(this);
1045:     if (image->IsGif())
1046:     {
1047:         // Create new GIF controller
1048:         gif_controller_ = std::make_unique<LvglGif>(image->image_dsc());
1049: 
1050:         if (gif_controller_->IsLoaded())
1051:         {
1052:             // Set up frame update callback
1053:             gif_controller_->SetFrameCallback([this]()
1054:                                               { lv_image_set_src(emoji_image_, gif_controller_->image_dsc()); });
1055: 
1056:             // Set initial frame and start animation
1057:             lv_image_set_src(emoji_image_, gif_controller_->image_dsc());
1058:             gif_controller_->Start();
1059: 
1060:             // Show GIF, hide others
1061:             lv_obj_add_flag(emoji_label_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
1062:             lv_obj_remove_flag(emoji_image_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
1063:         }
1064:         else
1065:         {
1066:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to load GIF for emotion: %s", emotion);
1067:             gif_controller_.reset();
1068:         }
1069:     }
1070:     else
1071:     {
1072:         lv_image_set_src(emoji_image_, image->image_dsc());
1073:         lv_obj_add_flag(emoji_label_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
1074:         lv_obj_remove_flag(emoji_image_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
1075:     }
1076: 
1077: #if CONFIG_USE_WECHAT_MESSAGE_STYLE
1078:     // In WeChat message style, if emotion is neutral, don't display it
1079:     uint32_t child_count = lv_obj_get_child_cnt(content_);
1080:     if (strcmp(emotion, "neutral") == 0 && child_count > 0)
1081:     {
1082:         // Stop GIF animation if running
1083:         if (gif_controller_)
1084:         {
1085:             gif_controller_->Stop();
1086:             gif_controller_.reset();
1087:         }
1088: 
1089:         lv_obj_add_flag(emoji_image_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
1090:         lv_obj_add_flag(emoji_label_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
1091:     }
1092: #endif
1093: }
1094: 
1095: void LcdDisplay::SetTheme(Theme *theme)
1096: {
1097:     DisplayLockGuard lock(this);
1098: 
1099:     auto lvgl_theme = static_cast<LvglTheme *>(theme);
1100: 
1101:     // Get the active screen
1102:     lv_obj_t *screen = lv_screen_active();
1103: 
1104:     // Set font
1105:     auto text_font = lvgl_theme->text_font()->font();
1106:     auto icon_font = lvgl_theme->icon_font()->font();
1107:     auto large_icon_font = lvgl_theme->large_icon_font()->font();
1108: 
1109:     if (text_font->line_height >= 40)
1110:     {
1111:         lv_obj_set_style_text_font(mute_label_, large_icon_font, 0);
1112:         lv_obj_set_style_text_font(battery_label_, large_icon_font, 0);
1113:         lv_obj_set_style_text_font(network_label_, large_icon_font, 0);
1114:     }
1115:     else
1116:     {
1117:         lv_obj_set_style_text_font(mute_label_, icon_font, 0);
1118:         lv_obj_set_style_text_font(battery_label_, icon_font, 0);
1119:         lv_obj_set_style_text_font(network_label_, icon_font, 0);
1120:     }
1121: 
1122:     // Set parent text color
1123:     lv_obj_set_style_text_font(screen, text_font, 0);
1124:     lv_obj_set_style_text_color(screen, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
1125: 
1126:     // Set background image

The `InitializeLcdThemes` function sets up light and dark display themes for the LCD interface, defining visual properties such as background colors, text styles, chat bubble appearances, and font configurations. It registers these themes with the theme manager to enable dynamic switching between them based on user preference or system state. This ensures a consistent and adaptable user interface experience across different lighting conditions or aesthetic preferences.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The overall purpose of initializing LCD themes (light and dark) is preserved.
- Both themes set similar color schemes and fonts, although some color values differ from the ground truth (e.g., chat background colors).
- Uses shared_ptr instead of raw pointers for theme objects which changes memory management but not functionality.
- Some semantic differences exist due to different color constants used (lv_color_white() vs lv_color_hex(0xFFFFFF)) which may or may not be equivalent depending on implementation.

Structure Similarity:
- General flow is maintained: create themes -> configure properties -> register with manager.
- Major structural difference: uses std::make_shared<LvglTheme>() without parameters vs new LvglTheme("name") in ground truth.
- Method call patterns differ significantly: e.g., set_name("light") vs constructor parameter.
- Font handling differs: passes raw font pointers directly vs wrapping them in LvglBuiltInFont shared_ptrs.
- Registration differs slightly: RegisterTheme(key, theme) vs RegisterTheme(theme), suggesting a potential API mismatch.

Functional Completeness:
- Covers core functionality: creates both light/dark themes, sets various visual properties, registers themes.
- Missing some specific color customizations found in ground truth (like exact hex codes for bubble colors).
- Adds extra functionality (set_spacing(4)) not present in ground truth, which could be seen as an extension or deviation.
- Theme registration mechanism appears functionally equivalent despite syntactic differences.

381:     // Right icons container
382:     lv_obj_t *right_icons = lv_obj_create(top_bar_);
383:     lv_obj_set_size(right_icons, LV_SIZE_CONTENT, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
384:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(right_icons, LV_OPA_TRANSP, 0);
385:     lv_obj_set_style_border_width(right_icons, 0, 0);
386:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(right_icons, 0, 0);
387:     lv_obj_set_flex_flow(right_icons, LV_FLEX_FLOW_ROW);
388:     lv_obj_set_flex_align(right_icons, LV_FLEX_ALIGN_END, LV_FLEX_ALIGN_CENTER, LV_FLEX_ALIGN_CENTER);
389: 
390:     mute_label_ = lv_label_create(right_icons);
391:     lv_label_set_text(mute_label_, "");
392:     lv_obj_set_style_text_font(mute_label_, icon_font, 0);
393:     lv_obj_set_style_text_color(mute_label_, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
394: 
395:     battery_label_ = lv_label_create(right_icons);
396:     lv_label_set_text(battery_label_, "");
397:     lv_obj_set_style_text_font(battery_label_, icon_font, 0);
398:     lv_obj_set_style_text_color(battery_label_, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
399:     lv_obj_set_style_margin_left(battery_label_, lvgl_theme->spacing(2), 0);
400: 
401:     /* Layer 2: Status bar - for center text labels */
402:     status_bar_ = lv_obj_create(screen);
403:     lv_obj_set_size(status_bar_, LV_HOR_RES, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
404:     lv_obj_set_style_radius(status_bar_, 0, 0);
405:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(status_bar_, LV_OPA_TRANSP, 0); // Transparent background
406:     lv_obj_set_style_border_width(status_bar_, 0, 0);
407:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(status_bar_, 0, 0);
408:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_top(status_bar_, lvgl_theme->spacing(2), 0);
409:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_bottom(status_bar_, lvgl_theme->spacing(2), 0);
410:     lv_obj_set_scrollbar_mode(status_bar_, LV_SCROLLBAR_MODE_OFF);
411:     lv_obj_set_style_layout(status_bar_, LV_LAYOUT_NONE, 0); // Use absolute positioning
412:     lv_obj_align(status_bar_, LV_ALIGN_TOP_MID, 0, 0);       // Overlap with top_bar_
413: 
414:     notification_label_ = lv_label_create(status_bar_);
415:     lv_obj_set_width(notification_label_, LV_HOR_RES * 0.8);
416:     lv_obj_set_style_text_align(notification_label_, LV_TEXT_ALIGN_CENTER, 0);
417:     lv_obj_set_style_text_color(notification_label_, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
418:     lv_label_set_text(notification_label_, "");
419:     lv_obj_align(notification_label_, LV_ALIGN_CENTER, 0, 0);
420:     lv_obj_add_flag(notification_label_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
421: 
422:     status_label_ = lv_label_create(status_bar_);
423:     lv_obj_set_width(status_label_, LV_HOR_RES * 0.8);
424:     lv_label_set_long_mode(status_label_, LV_LABEL_LONG_SCROLL_CIRCULAR);
425:     lv_obj_set_style_text_align(status_label_, LV_TEXT_ALIGN_CENTER, 0);
426:     lv_obj_set_style_text_color(status_label_, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
427:     lv_label_set_text(status_label_, Lang::Strings::INITIALIZING);
428:     lv_obj_align(status_label_, LV_ALIGN_CENTER, 0, 0);
429: 
430:     /* Content - Chat area */
431:     content_ = lv_obj_create(container_);
432:     lv_obj_set_style_radius(content_, 0, 0);
433:     lv_obj_set_width(content_, LV_HOR_RES);
434:     lv_obj_set_flex_grow(content_, 1);
435:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(content_, lvgl_theme->spacing(4), 0);
436:     lv_obj_set_style_border_width(content_, 0, 0);
437:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(content_, lvgl_theme->chat_background_color(), 0); // Background for chat area
438: 
439:     // Enable scrolling for chat content
440:     lv_obj_set_scrollbar_mode(content_, LV_SCROLLBAR_MODE_OFF);
441:     lv_obj_set_scroll_dir(content_, LV_DIR_VER);
442: 
443:     // Create a flex container for chat messages
444:     lv_obj_set_flex_flow(content_, LV_FLEX_FLOW_COLUMN);
445:     lv_obj_set_flex_align(content_, LV_FLEX_ALIGN_START, LV_FLEX_ALIGN_START, LV_FLEX_ALIGN_START);
446:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_row(content_, lvgl_theme->spacing(4), 0); // Space between messages
447: 
448:     // We'll create chat messages dynamically in SetChatMessage
449:     chat_message_label_ = nullptr;
450: 
451:     low_battery_popup_ = lv_obj_create(screen);
452:     lv_obj_set_scrollbar_mode(low_battery_popup_, LV_SCROLLBAR_MODE_OFF);
453:     lv_obj_set_size(low_battery_popup_, LV_HOR_RES * 0.9, text_font->line_height * 2);
454:     lv_obj_align(low_battery_popup_, LV_ALIGN_BOTTOM_MID, 0, -lvgl_theme->spacing(4));
455:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(low_battery_popup_, lvgl_theme->low_battery_color(), 0);
456:     lv_obj_set_style_radius(low_battery_popup_, lvgl_theme->spacing(4), 0);
457:     low_battery_label_ = lv_label_create(low_battery_popup_);
458:     lv_label_set_text(low_battery_label_, Lang::Strings::BATTERY_NEED_CHARGE);
459:     lv_obj_set_style_text_color(low_battery_label_, lv_color_white(), 0);
460:     lv_obj_center(low_battery_label_);
461:     lv_obj_add_flag(low_battery_popup_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
462: 
463:     emoji_image_ = lv_img_create(screen);
464:     lv_obj_align(emoji_image_, LV_ALIGN_TOP_MID, 0, text_font->line_height + lvgl_theme->spacing(8));
465: 
466:     // Display AI logo while booting
467:     emoji_label_ = lv_label_create(screen);
468:     lv_obj_center(emoji_label_);
469:     lv_obj_set_style_text_font(emoji_label_, large_icon_font, 0);
470:     lv_obj_set_style_text_color(emoji_label_, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
471:     lv_label_set_text(emoji_label_, FONT_AWESOME_MICROCHIP_AI);
472: }
473: #if CONFIG_IDF_TARGET_ESP32P4
474: #define MAX_MESSAGES 40
475: #else
476: #define MAX_MESSAGES 20
477: #endif
478: void LcdDisplay::SetChatMessage(const char *role, const char *content)
479: {
480:     DisplayLockGuard lock(this);
481:     if (content_ == nullptr)
482:     {
483:         return;
484:     }
485: 
486:     // Check if message count exceeds limit
487:     uint32_t child_count = lv_obj_get_child_cnt(content_);
488:     if (child_count >= MAX_MESSAGES)
489:     {
490:         // Delete the oldest message (first child object)
491:         lv_obj_t *first_child = lv_obj_get_child(content_, 0);
492:         lv_obj_t *last_child = lv_obj_get_child(content_, child_count - 1);
493:         if (first_child != nullptr)
494:         {
495:             lv_obj_del(first_child);
496:         }
497:         // Scroll to the last message immediately
498:         if (last_child != nullptr)
499:         {
500:             lv_obj_scroll_to_view_recursive(last_child, LV_ANIM_OFF);
501:         }
502:     }
503: 
504:     // Collapse system messages (if it's a system message, check if the last message is also a system message)
505:     if (strcmp(role, "system") == 0)
506:     {
507:         if (child_count > 0)
508:         {
509:             // Get the last message container
510:             lv_obj_t *last_container = lv_obj_get_child(content_, child_count - 1);
511:             if (last_container != nullptr && lv_obj_get_child_cnt(last_container) > 0)
512:             {
513:                 // Get the bubble inside the container
514:                 lv_obj_t *last_bubble = lv_obj_get_child(last_container, 0);
515:                 if (last_bubble != nullptr)
516:                 {
517:                     // Check if bubble type is system message
518:                     void *bubble_type_ptr = lv_obj_get_user_data(last_bubble);
519:                     if (bubble_type_ptr != nullptr && strcmp((const char *)bubble_type_ptr, "system") == 0)
520:                     {
521:                         // If the last message is also a system message, delete it
522:                         lv_obj_del(last_container);
523:                     }
524:                 }
525:             }
526:         }
527:     }
528:     else
529:     {
530:         // Hide the centered AI logo
531:         lv_obj_add_flag(emoji_label_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
532:     }
533: 
534:     // Avoid empty message boxes
535:     if (strlen(content) == 0)
536:     {
537:         return;
538:     }
539: 
540:     auto lvgl_theme = static_cast<LvglTheme *>(current_theme_);
541:     auto text_font = lvgl_theme->text_font()->font();
542: 
543:     // Create a message bubble
544:     lv_obj_t *msg_bubble = lv_obj_create(content_);
545:     lv_obj_set_style_radius(msg_bubble, 8, 0);
546:     lv_obj_set_scrollbar_mode(msg_bubble, LV_SCROLLBAR_MODE_OFF);
547:     lv_obj_set_style_border_width(msg_bubble, 0, 0);
548:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(msg_bubble, lvgl_theme->spacing(4), 0);
549: 
550:     // Create the message text
551:     lv_obj_t *msg_text = lv_label_create(msg_bubble);
552:     lv_label_set_text(msg_text, content);
553: 
554:     // Calculate actual text width
555:     lv_coord_t text_width = lv_txt_get_width(content, strlen(content), text_font, 0);
556: 
557:     // Calculate bubble width
558:     lv_coord_t max_width = LV_HOR_RES * 85 / 100 - 16; // 85% of screen width
559:     lv_coord_t min_width = 20;
560:     lv_coord_t bubble_width;
561: 
562:     // Ensure text width is not less than minimum width
563:     if (text_width < min_width)
564:     {
565:         text_width = min_width;
566:     }
567: 
568:     // If text width is less than max width, use text width
569:     if (text_width < max_width)
570:     {
571:         bubble_width = text_width;
572:     }
573:     else
574:     {
575:         bubble_width = max_width;
576:     }
577: 
578:     // Set message text width
579:     lv_obj_set_width(msg_text, bubble_width); // Subtract padding
580:     lv_label_set_long_mode(msg_text, LV_LABEL_LONG_WRAP);
581: 
582:     // Set bubble width
583:     lv_obj_set_width(msg_bubble, bubble_width);
584:     lv_obj_set_height(msg_bubble, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
585: 
586:     // Set alignment and style based on message role
587:     if (strcmp(role, "user") == 0)
588:     {
589:         // User messages are right-aligned with green background
590:         lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(msg_bubble, lvgl_theme->user_bubble_color(), 0);
591:         lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(msg_bubble, LV_OPA_70, 0);
592:         // Set text color for contrast
593:         lv_obj_set_style_text_color(msg_text, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
594: 
595:         // Set custom attribute to mark bubble type
596:         lv_obj_set_user_data(msg_bubble, (void *)"user");
597: 
598:         // Set appropriate width for content
599:         lv_obj_set_width(msg_bubble, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
600:         lv_obj_set_height(msg_bubble, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
601: 
602:         // Don't grow
603:         lv_obj_set_style_flex_grow(msg_bubble, 0, 0);
604:     }
605:     else if (strcmp(role, "assistant") == 0)
606:     {
607:         // Assistant messages are left-aligned with white background
608:         lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(msg_bubble, lvgl_theme->assistant_bubble_color(), 0);
609:         lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(msg_bubble, LV_OPA_70, 0);
610:         // Set text color for contrast
611:         lv_obj_set_style_text_color(msg_text, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
612: 
613:         // Set custom attribute to mark bubble type
614:         lv_obj_set_user_data(msg_bubble, (void *)"assistant");
615: 
616:         // Set appropriate width for content
617:         lv_obj_set_width(msg_bubble, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
618:         lv_obj_set_height(msg_bubble, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
619: 
620:         // Don't grow
621:         lv_obj_set_style_flex_grow(msg_bubble, 0, 0);
622:     }
623:     else if (strcmp(role, "system") == 0)
624:     {
625:         // System messages are center-aligned with light gray background
626:         lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(msg_bubble, lvgl_theme->system_bubble_color(), 0);
627:         lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(msg_bubble, LV_OPA_70, 0);
628:         // Set text color for contrast
629:         lv_obj_set_style_text_color(msg_text, lvgl_theme->system_text_color(), 0);
630: 
631:         // Set custom attribute to mark bubble type
632:         lv_obj_set_user_data(msg_bubble, (void *)"system");
633: 
634:         // Set appropriate width for content
635:         lv_obj_set_width(msg_bubble, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
636:         lv_obj_set_height(msg_bubble, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
637: 
638:         // Don't grow
639:         lv_obj_set_style_flex_grow(msg_bubble, 0, 0);
640:     }
641: 
642:     // Create a full-width container for user messages to ensure right alignment
643:     if (strcmp(role, "user") == 0)
644:     {
645:         // Create a full-width container
646:         lv_obj_t *container = lv_obj_create(content_);
647:         lv_obj_set_width(container, LV_HOR_RES);
648:         lv_obj_set_height(container, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
649: 
650:         // Make container transparent and borderless
651:         lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(container, LV_OPA_TRANSP, 0);
652:         lv_obj_set_style_border_width(container, 0, 0);
653:         lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(container, 0, 0);
654: 
655:         // Move the message bubble into this container
656:         lv_obj_set_parent(msg_bubble, container);
657: 
658:         // Right align the bubble in the container
659:         lv_obj_align(msg_bubble, LV_ALIGN_RIGHT_MID, -25, 0);
660: 
661:         // Auto-scroll to this container
662:         lv_obj_scroll_to_view_recursive(container, LV_ANIM_ON);
663:     }
664:     else if (strcmp(role, "system") == 0)
665:     {
666:         // Create full-width container for system messages to ensure center alignment
667:         lv_obj_t *container = lv_obj_create(content_);
668:         lv_obj_set_width(container, LV_HOR_RES);
669:         lv_obj_set_height(container, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
670: 
671:         lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(container, LV_OPA_TRANSP, 0);
672:         lv_obj_set_style_border_width(container, 0, 0);
673:         lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(container, 0, 0);
674: 
675:         lv_obj_set_parent(msg_bubble, container);
676:         lv_obj_align(msg_bubble, LV_ALIGN_CENTER, 0, 0);
677:         lv_obj_scroll_to_view_recursive(container, LV_ANIM_ON);
678:     }
679:     else
680:     {
681:         // For assistant messages
682:         // Left align assistant messages
683:         lv_obj_align(msg_bubble, LV_ALIGN_LEFT_MID, 0, 0);
684: 
685:         // Auto-scroll to the message bubble
686:         lv_obj_scroll_to_view_recursive(msg_bubble, LV_ANIM_ON);
687:     }
688: 
689:     // Store reference to the latest message label
690:     chat_message_label_ = msg_text;
691: }

693: void LcdDisplay::SetPreviewImage(std::unique_ptr<LvglImage> image)
694: {
695:     DisplayLockGuard lock(this);
696:     if (content_ == nullptr)
697:     {
698:         return;
699:     }
700: 
701:     if (image == nullptr)
702:     {
703:         return;
704:     }
705: 
706:     auto lvgl_theme = static_cast<LvglTheme *>(current_theme_);
707:     // Create a message bubble for image preview
708:     lv_obj_t *img_bubble = lv_obj_create(content_);
709:     lv_obj_set_style_radius(img_bubble, 8, 0);
710:     lv_obj_set_scrollbar_mode(img_bubble, LV_SCROLLBAR_MODE_OFF);
711:     lv_obj_set_style_border_width(img_bubble, 0, 0);
712:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(img_bubble, lvgl_theme->spacing(4), 0);
713: 
714:     // Set image bubble background color (similar to system message)
715:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(img_bubble, lvgl_theme->assistant_bubble_color(), 0);
716:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(img_bubble, LV_OPA_70, 0);
717: 
718:     // Set custom attribute to mark bubble type
719:     lv_obj_set_user_data(img_bubble, (void *)"image");
720: 
721:     // Create the image object inside the bubble
722:     lv_obj_t *preview_image = lv_image_create(img_bubble);
723: 
724:     // Calculate appropriate size for the image
725:     lv_coord_t max_width = LV_HOR_RES * 70 / 100;  // 70% of screen width
726:     lv_coord_t max_height = LV_VER_RES * 50 / 100; // 50% of screen height
727: 
728:     // Calculate zoom factor to fit within maximum dimensions
729:     auto img_dsc = image->image_dsc();
730:     lv_coord_t img_width = img_dsc->header.w;
731:     lv_coord_t img_height = img_dsc->header.h;
732:     if (img_width == 0 || img_height == 0)
733:     {
734:         img_width = max_width;
735:         img_height = max_height;
736:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Invalid image dimensions: %ld x %ld, using default dimensions: %ld x %ld", img_width, img_height, max_width, max_height);
737:     }
738: 
739:     lv_coord_t zoom_w = (max_width * 256) / img_width;
740:     lv_coord_t zoom_h = (max_height * 256) / img_height;
741:     lv_coord_t zoom = (zoom_w < zoom_h) ? zoom_w : zoom_h;
742: 
743:     // Ensure zoom doesn't exceed 256 (100%)
744:     if (zoom > 256)
745:         zoom = 256;
746: 
747:     // Set image properties
748:     lv_image_set_src(preview_image, img_dsc);
749:     lv_image_set_scale(preview_image, zoom);
750: 
751:     // Add event handler to clean up LvglImage when image is deleted
752:     // We need to transfer ownership of the unique_ptr to the event callback
753:     LvglImage *raw_image = image.release(); // Release ownership of smart pointer
754:     lv_obj_add_event_cb(preview_image, [](lv_event_t *e)
755:                         {
756:         LvglImage* img = (LvglImage*)lv_event_get_user_data(e);
757:         if (img != nullptr) {
758:             delete img; // Properly release memory by deleting LvglImage object
759:         } }, LV_EVENT_DELETE, (void *)raw_image);
760: 
761:     // Calculate actual scaled image dimensions
762:     lv_coord_t scaled_width = (img_width * zoom) / 256;
763:     lv_coord_t scaled_height = (img_height * zoom) / 256;
764: 
765:     // Set bubble size to be 16 pixels larger than the image (8 pixels on each side)
766:     lv_obj_set_width(img_bubble, scaled_width + 16);
767:     lv_obj_set_height(img_bubble, scaled_height + 16);
768: 
769:     // Don't grow in flex layout
770:     lv_obj_set_style_flex_grow(img_bubble, 0, 0);
771: 
772:     // Center the image within the bubble
773:     lv_obj_center(preview_image);
774: 
775:     // Left align the image bubble like assistant messages
776:     lv_obj_align(img_bubble, LV_ALIGN_LEFT_MID, 0, 0);
777: 
778:     // Auto-scroll to the image bubble
779:     lv_obj_scroll_to_view_recursive(img_bubble, LV_ANIM_ON);
780: }

782: void LcdDisplay::SetupUI()
783: {
784:     DisplayLockGuard lock(this);
785:     LvglTheme *lvgl_theme = static_cast<LvglTheme *>(current_theme_);
786:     auto text_font = lvgl_theme->text_font()->font();
787:     auto icon_font = lvgl_theme->icon_font()->font();
788:     auto large_icon_font = lvgl_theme->large_icon_font()->font();
789: 
790:     auto screen = lv_screen_active();
791:     lv_obj_set_style_text_font(screen, text_font, 0);
792:     lv_obj_set_style_text_color(screen, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
793:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(screen, lvgl_theme->background_color(), 0);
794: 
795:     /* Container - used as background */
796:     container_ = lv_obj_create(screen);
797:     lv_obj_set_size(container_, LV_HOR_RES, LV_VER_RES);
798:     lv_obj_set_style_radius(container_, 0, 0);
799:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(container_, 0, 0);
800:     lv_obj_set_style_border_width(container_, 0, 0);
801:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(container_, lvgl_theme->background_color(), 0);
802:     lv_obj_set_style_border_color(container_, lvgl_theme->border_color(), 0);
803: 
804:     /* Bottom layer: emoji_box_ - centered display */
805:     emoji_box_ = lv_obj_create(screen);
806:     lv_obj_set_size(emoji_box_, LV_SIZE_CONTENT, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
807:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(emoji_box_, LV_OPA_TRANSP, 0);
808:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(emoji_box_, 0, 0);
809:     lv_obj_set_style_border_width(emoji_box_, 0, 0);
810:     lv_obj_align(emoji_box_, LV_ALIGN_CENTER, 0, 0);
811: 
812:     emoji_label_ = lv_label_create(emoji_box_);
813:     lv_obj_set_style_text_font(emoji_label_, large_icon_font, 0);
814:     lv_obj_set_style_text_color(emoji_label_, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
815:     lv_label_set_text(emoji_label_, FONT_AWESOME_MICROCHIP_AI);
816: 
817:     emoji_image_ = lv_img_create(emoji_box_);
818:     lv_obj_center(emoji_image_);
819:     lv_obj_add_flag(emoji_image_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
820: 
821:     /* Middle layer: preview_image_ - centered display */
822:     preview_image_ = lv_image_create(screen);
823:     lv_obj_set_size(preview_image_, width_ / 2, height_ / 2);
824:     lv_obj_align(preview_image_, LV_ALIGN_CENTER, 0, 0);
825:     lv_obj_add_flag(preview_image_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
826: 
827:     /* Layer 1: Top bar - for status icons */
828:     top_bar_ = lv_obj_create(screen);
829:     lv_obj_set_size(top_bar_, LV_HOR_RES, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
830:     lv_obj_set_style_radius(top_bar_, 0, 0);
831:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(top_bar_, LV_OPA_50, 0); // 50% opacity background
832:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(top_bar_, lvgl_theme->background_color(), 0);
833:     lv_obj_set_style_border_width(top_bar_, 0, 0);
834:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(top_bar_, 0, 0);

937: void LcdDisplay::SetPreviewImage(std::unique_ptr<LvglImage> image)
938: {
939:     DisplayLockGuard lock(this);
940:     if (preview_image_ == nullptr)
941:     {
942:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Preview image is not initialized");
943:         return;
944:     }
945: 
946:     if (image == nullptr)
947:     {
948:         esp_timer_stop(preview_timer_);
949:         lv_obj_remove_flag(emoji_box_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
950:         lv_obj_add_flag(preview_image_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
951:         preview_image_cached_.reset();
952:         if (gif_controller_)
953:         {
954:             gif_controller_->Start();
955:         }
956:         return;
957:     }
958: 
959:     preview_image_cached_ = std::move(image);
960:     auto img_dsc = preview_image_cached_->image_dsc();
961:     lv_image_set_src(preview_image_, img_dsc);
962:     if (img_dsc->header.w > 0 && img_dsc->header.h > 0)
963:     {
964:         // zoom factor 0.5
965:         lv_image_set_scale(preview_image_, 128 * width_ / img_dsc->header.w);
966:     }
967: 
968:     // Hide emoji_box_
969:     if (gif_controller_)
970:     {
971:         gif_controller_->Stop();
972:     }
973:     lv_obj_add_flag(emoji_box_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
974:     lv_obj_remove_flag(preview_image_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
975:     esp_timer_stop(preview_timer_);
976:     ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_timer_start_once(preview_timer_, PREVIEW_IMAGE_DURATION_MS * 1000));
977: }

990: void LcdDisplay::SetEmotion(const char *emotion)
991: {
992:     // Stop any running GIF animation
993:     if (gif_controller_)
994:     {
995:         DisplayLockGuard lock(this);
996:         gif_controller_->Stop();
997:         gif_controller_.reset();
998:     }
999: 
1000:     if (emoji_image_ == nullptr)
1001:     {
1002:         return;
1003:     }
1004: 
1005:     auto emoji_collection = static_cast<LvglTheme *>(current_theme_)->emoji_collection();
1006:     auto image = emoji_collection != nullptr ? emoji_collection->GetEmojiImage(emotion) : nullptr;
1007:     if (image == nullptr)
1008:     {
1009:         const char *utf8 = font_awesome_get_utf8(emotion);
1010:         if (utf8 != nullptr && emoji_label_ != nullptr)
1011:         {
1012:             DisplayLockGuard lock(this);
1013:             lv_label_set_text(emoji_label_, utf8);
1014:             lv_obj_add_flag(emoji_image_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
1015:             lv_obj_remove_flag(emoji_label_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
1016:         }
1017:         return;
1018:     }
1019: 
1020:     DisplayLockGuard lock(this);
1021:     if (image->IsGif())
1022:     {
1023:         // Create new GIF controller
1024:         gif_controller_ = std::make_unique<LvglGif>(image->image_dsc());
1025: 
1026:         if (gif_controller_->IsLoaded())
1027:         {
1028:             // Set up frame update callback
1029:             gif_controller_->SetFrameCallback([this]()
1030:                                               { lv_image_set_src(emoji_image_, gif_controller_->image_dsc()); });
1031: 
1032:             // Set initial frame and start animation
1033:             lv_image_set_src(emoji_image_, gif_controller_->image_dsc());
1034:             gif_controller_->Start();
1035: 
1036:             // Show GIF, hide others
1037:             lv_obj_add_flag(emoji_label_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
1038:             lv_obj_remove_flag(emoji_image_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
1039:         }
1040:         else
1041:         {
1042:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to load GIF for emotion: %s", emotion);
1043:             gif_controller_.reset();
1044:         }
1045:     }
1046:     else
1047:     {
1048:         lv_image_set_src(emoji_image_, image->image_dsc());
1049:         lv_obj_add_flag(emoji_label_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
1050:         lv_obj_remove_flag(emoji_image_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
1051:     }
1052: 
1053: #if CONFIG_USE_WECHAT_MESSAGE_STYLE
1054:     // In WeChat message style, if emotion is neutral, don't display it
1055:     uint32_t child_count = lv_obj_get_child_cnt(content_);
1056:     if (strcmp(emotion, "neutral") == 0 && child_count > 0)
1057:     {
1058:         // Stop GIF animation if running
1059:         if (gif_controller_)
1060:         {
1061:             gif_controller_->Stop();
1062:             gif_controller_.reset();
1063:         }
1064: 
1065:         lv_obj_add_flag(emoji_image_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
1066:         lv_obj_add_flag(emoji_label_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
1067:     }
1068: #endif
1069: }
